Parameters.Name: Query
Parameters.Description: The query representing what will be reviewed in an access review.

Parameters.Name: QueryRoot
Parameters.Description: In the scenario where reviewers need to be specified dynamically, this property is used to indicate the relative source of the query. This property is only required if a relative query is specified. For example, ./manager.

Parameters.Name: QueryType
Parameters.Description: Indicates the type of query. Types include MicrosoftGraph and ARM.

Parameters.Name: PrincipalScopes
Parameters.Description: Defines the scopes of the principals for which access to resources are reviewed in the access review.

Parameters.Name: ResourceScopes
Parameters.Description: Defines the scopes of the resources for which access is reviewed.

Parameters.Name: odataType
Parameters.Description: The type of the entity.

Description: Azure AD Access Review Definition

Parameters.Name: ApplyActions
Parameters.Description: Optional field. Describes the  actions to take once a review is complete. There are two types that are currently supported: removeAccessApplyAction (default) and disableAndDeleteUserApplyAction. Field only needs to be specified in the case of disableAndDeleteUserApplyAction.

Parameters.Name: AutoApplyDecisionsEnabled
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether decisions are automatically applied. When set to false, an admin must apply the decisions manually once the reviewer completes the access review. When set to true, decisions are applied automatically after the access review instance duration ends, whether or not the reviewers have responded. Default value is false.  CAUTION: If both autoApplyDecisionsEnabled and defaultDecisionEnabled are true, all access for the principals to the resource risks being revoked if the reviewers fail to respond.

Parameters.Name: DecisionHistoriesForReviewersEnabled
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether decisions on previous access review stages are available for reviewers on an accessReviewInstance with multiple subsequent stages. If not provided, the default is disabled (false).

Parameters.Name: DefaultDecision
Parameters.Description: Decision chosen if defaultDecisionEnabled is enabled. Can be one of Approve, Deny, or Recommendation.

Parameters.Name: DefaultDecisionEnabled
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether the default decision is enabled or disabled when reviewers do not respond. Default value is false.  CAUTION: If both autoApplyDecisionsEnabled and defaultDecisionEnabled are true, all access for the principals to the resource risks being revoked if the reviewers fail to respond.

Parameters.Name: InstanceDurationInDays
Parameters.Description: Duration of each recurrence of review (accessReviewInstance) in number of days. NOTE: If the stageSettings of the accessReviewScheduleDefinition object is defined, its durationInDays setting will be used instead of the value of this property.

Parameters.Name: JustificationRequiredOnApproval
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether reviewers are required to provide justification with their decision. Default value is false.

Parameters.Name: MailNotificationsEnabled
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether emails are enabled or disabled. Default value is false.

Parameters.Name: RecommendationInsightSettings
Parameters.Description: Optional. Describes the types of insights that aid reviewers to make access review decisions. NOTE: If the stageSettings of the accessReviewScheduleDefinition object is defined, its recommendationInsightSettings setting will be used instead of the value of this property.

Parameters.Name: RecommendationLookBackDuration
Parameters.Description: Optional field. Indicates the period of inactivity (with respect to the start date of the review instance) that recommendations will be configured from. The recommendation will be to deny if the user is inactive during the look-back duration. For reviews of groups and Microsoft Entra roles, any duration is accepted. For reviews of applications, 30 days is the maximum duration. If not specified, the duration is 30 days. NOTE: If the stageSettings of the accessReviewScheduleDefinition object is defined, its recommendationLookBackDuration setting will be used instead of the value of this property.

Parameters.Name: RecommendationsEnabled
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether decision recommendations are enabled or disabled. NOTE: If the stageSettings of the accessReviewScheduleDefinition object is defined, its recommendationsEnabled setting will be used instead of the value of this property.

Parameters.Name: Recurrence
Parameters.Description: Detailed settings for recurrence using the standard Outlook recurrence object. Note: Only dayOfMonth, interval, and type (weekly, absoluteMonthly) properties are supported. Use the property startDate on recurrenceRange to determine the day the review starts.

Parameters.Name: ReminderNotificationsEnabled
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether reminders are enabled or disabled. Default value is false.

Description: Azure AD Access Review Definition

Parameters.Name: odataType
Parameters.Description: The type of the entity.

Description: Azure AD Access Review Definition

Parameters.Name: RecommendationLookBackDuration
Parameters.Description: Optional. Indicates the time period of inactivity (with respect to the start date of the review instance) that recommendations will be configured from. The recommendation will be to deny if the user is inactive during the look-back duration. For reviews of groups and Microsoft Entra roles, any duration is accepted. For reviews of applications, 30 days is the maximum duration. If not specified, the duration is 30 days.

Parameters.Name: SignInScope
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether inactivity is calculated based on the user's inactivity in the tenant or in the application. The possible values are tenant, application, unknownFutureValue. application is only relevant when the access review is a review of an assignment to an application.

Parameters.Name: odataType
Parameters.Description: The type of the entity.

Description: Azure AD Access Review Definition

Parameters.Name: Pattern
Parameters.Description: The frequency of an event. Do not specify for a one-time access review.  For access reviews: Do not specify this property for a one-time access review.   Only interval, dayOfMonth, and type (weekly, absoluteMonthly) properties of recurrencePattern are supported.

Parameters.Name: Range
Parameters.Description: The duration of an event.

Description: Azure AD Access Review Definition

Parameters.Name: DayOfMonth
Parameters.Description: The day of the month on which the event occurs. Required if type is absoluteMonthly or absoluteYearly.

Parameters.Name: DaysOfWeek
Parameters.Description: A collection of the days of the week on which the event occurs. The possible values are: sunday, monday, tuesday, wednesday, thursday, friday, saturday. If type is relativeMonthly or relativeYearly, and daysOfWeek specifies more than one day, the event falls on the first day that satisfies the pattern.  Required if type is weekly, relativeMonthly, or relativeYearly.

Parameters.Name: FirstDayOfWeek
Parameters.Description: The first day of the week. The possible values are: sunday, monday, tuesday, wednesday, thursday, friday, saturday. Default is sunday. Required if type is weekly.

Parameters.Name: Index
Parameters.Description: Specifies on which instance of the allowed days specified in daysOfWeek the event occurs, counted from the first instance in the month. The possible values are: first, second, third, fourth, last. Default is first. Optional and used if type is relativeMonthly or relativeYearly.

Parameters.Name: Interval
Parameters.Description: The number of units between occurrences, where units can be in days, weeks, months, or years, depending on the type. Required.

Parameters.Name: Month
Parameters.Description: The month in which the event occurs.  This is a number from 1 to 12.

Parameters.Name: Type
Parameters.Description: The recurrence pattern type: daily, weekly, absoluteMonthly, relativeMonthly, absoluteYearly, relativeYearly. Required. For more information, see values of type property.

Description: Azure AD Access Review Definition

Parameters.Name: EndDate
Parameters.Description: The date to stop applying the recurrence pattern. Depending on the recurrence pattern of the event, the last occurrence of the meeting may not be this date. Required if type is endDate.

Parameters.Name: NumberOfOccurrences
Parameters.Description: The number of times to repeat the event. Required and must be positive if type is numbered.

Parameters.Name: RecurrenceTimeZone
Parameters.Description: Time zone for the startDate and endDate properties. Optional. If not specified, the time zone of the event is used.

Parameters.Name: StartDate
Parameters.Description: The date to start applying the recurrence pattern. The first occurrence of the meeting may be this date or later, depending on the recurrence pattern of the event. Must be the same value as the start property of the recurring event. Required.

Parameters.Name: Type
Parameters.Description: The recurrence range. Possible values are: endDate, noEnd, numbered. Required.

Description: Azure AD Access Review Definition

Parameters.Name: DecisionsThatWillMoveToNextStage
Parameters.Description: Indicate which decisions will go to the next stage. Can be a subset of Approve, Deny, Recommendation, or NotReviewed. If not provided, all decisions will go to the next stage. Optional.

Parameters.Name: DependsOnValue
Parameters.Description: Defines the sequential or parallel order of the stages and depends on the stageId. Only sequential stages are currently supported. For example, if stageId is 2, then dependsOn must be 1. If stageId is 1, don't specify dependsOn. Required if stageId isn't 1.

Parameters.Name: DurationInDays
Parameters.Description: The duration of the stage. Required.  NOTE: The cumulative value of this property across all stages  1. Will override the instanceDurationInDays setting on the accessReviewScheduleDefinition object. 2. Can't exceed the length of one recurrence. That is, if the review recurs weekly, the cumulative durationInDays can't exceed 7.

Parameters.Name: RecommendationInsightSettings
Parameters.Description: Recommendation Insights Settings

Parameters.Name: RecommendationLookBackDuration
Parameters.Description: Optional field. Indicates the time period of inactivity (with respect to the start date of the review instance) from which that recommendations will be configured. The recommendation is to deny if the user is inactive during the look back duration. For reviews of groups and Microsoft Entra roles, any duration is accepted. For reviews of applications, 30 days is the maximum duration. If not specified, the duration is 30 days. NOTE: The value of this property overrides the corresponding setting on the accessReviewScheduleDefinition object.

Parameters.Name: RecommendationsEnabled
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether showing recommendations to reviewers is enabled. Required. NOTE: The value of this property overrides the corresponding setting on the accessReviewScheduleDefinition object.

Parameters.Name: StageId
Parameters.Description: Unique identifier of the accessReviewStageSettings. The stageId is used in dependsOn property to indicate the stage relationship. Required.

Description: Azure AD Access Review Definition

Parameters.Name: Id
Parameters.Description: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: Name of the access review series. Supports $select and $orderby. Required on create.

Parameters.Name: DescriptionForAdmins
Parameters.Description: Description provided by review creators to provide more context of the review to admins. Supports $select.

Parameters.Name: DescriptionForReviewers
Parameters.Description: Description provided  by review creators to provide more context of the review to reviewers. Reviewers see this description in the email sent to them requesting their review. Email notifications support up to 256 characters. Supports $select.

Parameters.Name: ScopeValue
Parameters.Description: Defines the entities whose access is reviewed. For supported scopes, see accessReviewScope. Required on create. Supports $select and $filter (contains only). For examples of options for configuring scope, see Configure the scope of your access review definition using the Microsoft Graph API.

Parameters.Name: SettingsValue
Parameters.Description: The settings for an access review series, see type definition below. Supports $select. Required on create.

Parameters.Name: StageSettings
Parameters.Description: Required only for a multi-stage access review to define the stages and their settings. You can break down each review instance into up to three sequential stages, where each stage can have a different set of reviewers, fallback reviewers, and settings. Stages are created sequentially based on the dependsOn property. Optional.  When this property is defined, its settings are used instead of the corresponding settings in the accessReviewScheduleDefinition object and its settings, reviewers, and fallbackReviewers properties.

Parameters.Name: allowedHeaders
Parameters.Description: The request headers that the origin domain may specify on the CORS request. The wildcard character * indicates that any header beginning with the specified prefix is allowed.

Parameters.Name: maxAgeInSeconds
Parameters.Description: The maximum amount of time that a browser should cache the response to the preflight OPTIONS request.

Parameters.Name: resource
Parameters.Description: Resource within the application segment for which CORS permissions are granted. / grants permission for whole app segment.

Parameters.Name: allowedMethods
Parameters.Description: The HTTP request methods that the origin domain may use for a CORS request.

Parameters.Name: allowedOrigins
Parameters.Description: The origin domains that are permitted to make a request against the service via CORS. The origin domain is the domain from which the request originates. The origin must be an exact case-sensitive match with the origin that the user age sends to the service.

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Application.

Parameters.Name: alternateUrl
Parameters.Description: If you're configuring a traffic manager in front of multiple App Proxy application segments, contains the user-friendly URL that will point to the traffic manager.

Parameters.Name: corsConfigurations
Parameters.Description: CORS Rule definition for a particular application segment.

Parameters.Name: externalUrl
Parameters.Description: The published external URL for the application segment; for example, https://intranet.contoso.com./

Parameters.Name: internalUrl
Parameters.Description: The internal URL of the application segment; for example, https://intranet/.

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Application.

Parameters.Name: kerberosServicePrincipalName
Parameters.Description: The Internal Application SPN of the application server. This SPN needs to be in the list of services to which the connector can present delegated credentials.

Parameters.Name: kerberosSignOnMappingAttributeType
Parameters.Description: The Delegated Login Identity for the connector to use on behalf of your users. For more information, see Working with different on-premises and cloud identities . Possible values are: userPrincipalName, onPremisesUserPrincipalName, userPrincipalUsername, onPremisesUserPrincipalUsername, onPremisesSAMAccountName.

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Application.

Parameters.Name: singleSignOnMode
Parameters.Description: The preferred single-sign on mode for the application. Possible values are: none, onPremisesKerberos, aadHeaderBased,pingHeaderBased, oAuthToken.

Parameters.Name: kerberosSignOnSettings
Parameters.Description: The Kerberos Constrained Delegation settings for applications that use Integrated Window Authentication.

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Application.

Parameters.Name: alternateUrl
Parameters.Description: If you're configuring a traffic manager in front of multiple App Proxy applications, the alternateUrl is the user-friendly URL that points to the traffic manager.

Parameters.Name: applicationServerTimeout
Parameters.Description: The duration the connector waits for a response from the backend application before closing the connection. Possible values are default, long.

Parameters.Name: externalAuthenticationType
Parameters.Description: Details the pre-authentication setting for the application. Pre-authentication enforces that users must authenticate before accessing the app. Pass through doesn't require authentication. Possible values are: passthru, aadPreAuthentication.

Parameters.Name: externalUrl
Parameters.Description: The published external url for the application. For example, https://intranet-contoso.msappproxy.net/.

Parameters.Name: internalUrl
Parameters.Description: The internal url of the application. For example, https://intranet/.

Parameters.Name: isBackendCertificateValidationEnabled
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether backend SSL certificate validation is enabled for the application. For all new Application Proxy apps, the property is set to true by default. For all existing apps, the property is set to false.

Parameters.Name: isHttpOnlyCookieEnabled
Parameters.Description: Indicates if the HTTPOnly cookie flag should be set in the HTTP response headers. Set this value to true to have Application Proxy cookies include the HTTPOnly flag in the HTTP response headers. If using Remote Desktop Services, set this value to False. Default value is false.

Parameters.Name: isPersistentCookieEnabled
Parameters.Description: Indicates if the Persistent cookie flag should be set in the HTTP response headers. Keep this value set to false. Only use this setting for applications that can't share cookies between processes. For more information about cookie settings, see Cookie settings for accessing on-premises applications in Microsoft Entra ID. Default value is false.

Parameters.Name: isSecureCookieEnabled
Parameters.Description: Indicates if the Secure cookie flag should be set in the HTTP response headers. Set this value to true to transmit cookies over a secure channel such as an encrypted HTTPS request. Default value is true.

Parameters.Name: isStateSessionEnabled
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether validation of the state parameter when the client uses the OAuth 2.0 authorization code grant flow is enabled. This setting allows admins to specify whether they want to enable CSRF protection for their apps.

Parameters.Name: isTranslateHostHeaderEnabled
Parameters.Description: Indicates if the application should translate urls in the response headers. Keep this value as true unless your application required the original host header in the authentication request. Default value is true.

Parameters.Name: isTranslateLinksInBodyEnabled
Parameters.Description: Indicates if the application should translate urls in the application body. Keep this value as false unless you have hardcoded HTML links to other on-premises applications and don't use custom domains. For more information, see Link translation with Application Proxy. Default value is false.

Parameters.Name: onPremisesApplicationSegments
Parameters.Description: Represents the collection of application segments for an on-premises wildcard application that's published through Microsoft Entra application proxy.

Parameters.Name: singleSignOnSettings
Parameters.Description: Represents the single sign-on configuration for the on-premises application.

Parameters.Name: SourceAPI
Parameters.Description: Name of the API from which the permission comes from.

Parameters.Name: Type
Parameters.Description: Type of permission.

Parameters.Name: AdminConsentGranted
Parameters.Description: Represented whether or not the Admin consent been granted on the app.

Parameters.Description: The optional claims returned in the JWT access token.

Parameters.Name: IdToken

Parameters.Name: Saml2Token
Parameters.Description: The optional claims returned in the SAML token.

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Application.

Parameters.Name: Essential
Parameters.Description: If the value is true, the claim specified by the client is necessary to ensure a smooth authorization experience for the specific task requested by the end user. The default value is false.

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: The name of the optional claim.

Parameters.Name: Source
Parameters.Description: The source (directory object) of the claim. There are predefined claims and user-defined claims from extension properties. If the source value is null, the claim is a predefined optional claim. If the source value is user, the value in the name property is the extension property from the user object.

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Application.

Parameters.Name: AppId
Parameters.Description: The unique identifier for the client application.

Parameters.Name: PermissionIds
Parameters.Description: The unique identifier for the scopes the client application is granted.

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Application.

Parameters.Name: adminConsentDescription
Parameters.Description: A description of the delegated permissions, intended to be read by an administrator granting the permission on behalf of all users. This text appears in tenant-wide admin consent experiences.

Parameters.Name: adminConsentDisplayName
Parameters.Description: The permission's title, intended to be read by an administrator granting the permission on behalf of all users.

Parameters.Name: userConsentDescription
Parameters.Description: A description of the delegated permissions, intended to be read by a user granting the permission on their own behalf. This text appears in consent experiences where the user is consenting only on behalf of themselves.

Parameters.Name: userConsentDisplayName
Parameters.Description: A title for the permission, intended to be read by a user granting the permission on their own behalf. This text appears in consent experiences where the user is consenting only on behalf of themselves.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: Specifies the value to include in the scp (scope) claim in access tokens. Must not exceed 120 characters in length.

Parameters.Name: isEnabled
Parameters.Description: When you create or update a permission, this property must be set to true (which is the default). To delete a permission, this property must first be set to false. At that point, in a subsequent call, the permission may be removed.

Parameters.Name: type
Parameters.Description: The possible values are: User and Admin. Specifies whether this delegated permission should be considered safe for non-admin users to consent to on behalf of themselves, or whether an administrator consent should always be required.

Parameters.Name: id
Parameters.Description: Unique delegated permission identifier inside the collection of delegated permissions defined for a resource application.

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Application.

Parameters.Name: PreAuthorizedApplications
Parameters.Description: Lists the client applications that are preauthorized with the specified delegated permissions to access this application's APIs. Users aren't required to consent to any preauthorized application (for the permissions specified). However, any other permissions not listed in preAuthorizedApplications (requested through incremental consent for example) will require user consent.

Parameters.Name: Oauth2PermissionScopes
Parameters.Description: List of associated API scopes.

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Application.

Parameters.Name: BlockAzureADGraphAccess
Parameters.Description: If false, allows the app to have extended access to Azure AD Graph until June 30, 2025 when Azure AD Graph is fully retired. For more information on Azure AD retirement updates, see June 2024 update on Azure AD Graph API retirement. Use 'Null' to ensure the value is not configured.

Parameters.Name: RemoveUnverifiedEmailClaim
Parameters.Description: If true, removes the email claim from tokens sent to an application when the email address's domain can't be verified. Use 'Null' to ensure the value is not configured.

Parameters.Name: RequireClientServicePrincipal
Parameters.Description: DEPRECATED

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Application.

Parameters.Name: CustomKeyIdentifier
Parameters.Description: A 40-character binary type that can be used to identify the credential. Optional. When not provided in the payload, defaults to the thumbprint of the certificate.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: Friendly name for the key. Optional.

Parameters.Name: EndDateTime
Parameters.Description: The date and time at which the credential expires. The DateTimeOffset type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z.

Parameters.Name: KeyId
Parameters.Description: The unique identifier (GUID) for the key.

Parameters.Name: Key
Parameters.Description: The certificate's raw data in byte array converted to Base64 string.

Parameters.Name: StartDateTime
Parameters.Description: The date and time at which the credential becomes valid.The Timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z.

Parameters.Name: Type
Parameters.Description: The type of key credential for example, Symmetric, AsymmetricX509Cert.

Parameters.Name: Usage
Parameters.Description: A string that describes the purpose for which the key can be used for example, Verify.

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Application.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: Friendly name for the password. Optional.

Parameters.Name: EndDateTime
Parameters.Description: The date and time at which the password expires represented using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Optional.

Parameters.Name: Hint
Parameters.Description: Contains the first three characters of the password. Read-only.

Parameters.Name: KeyId
Parameters.Description: The unique identifier for the password.

Parameters.Name: StartDateTime
Parameters.Description: The date and time at which the password becomes valid. The Timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Optional.

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Application.

Parameters.Name: AllowedMemberTypes
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether this app role can be assigned to users and groups (by setting to 'User'), to other application's (by setting to 'Application', or both (by setting to 'User', 'Application'). App roles supporting assignment to other applications' service principals are also known as application permissions. The 'Application' value is only supported for app roles defined on application entities.

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: The description for the app role. This is displayed when the app role is being assigned and, if the app role functions as an application permission, during  consent experiences.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: Display name for the permission that appears in the app role assignment and consent experiences.

Parameters.Name: Id
Parameters.Description: Unique role identifier inside the appRoles collection. When creating a new app role, a new GUID identifier must be provided.

Parameters.Name: IsEnabled
Parameters.Description: When creating or updating an app role, this must be set to true (which is the default). To delete a role, this must first be set to false.  At that point, in a subsequent call, this role may be removed.

Parameters.Name: Origin
Parameters.Description: Specifies if the app role is defined on the application object or on the servicePrincipal entity. Must not be included in any POST or PATCH requests. Read-only.

Parameters.Name: Value
Parameters.Description: Specifies the value to include in the roles claim in ID tokens and access tokens authenticating an assigned user or service principal. Must not exceed 120 characters in length. Allowed characters are : ! # $ % & ' ( ) * + , - . / :   =       + _    } , and characters in the ranges 0-9, A-Z and a-z. Any other character, including the space character, aren't allowed. May not begin with ..

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Application.

Parameters.Name: RedirectUris
Parameters.Description: Single page application redirect URIs.

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Application.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: DisplayName of the app

Parameters.Name: ObjectId
Parameters.Description: ObjectID of the app.

Parameters.Name: AppId
Parameters.Description: AppId for the app.

Parameters.Name: AvailableToOtherTenants
Parameters.Description: DEPRECATED: Indicates whether this application is available in other tenants.

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: A free text field to provide a description of the application object to end users. The maximum allowed size is 1024 characters.

Parameters.Name: GroupMembershipClaims
Parameters.Description: A bitmask that configures the groups claim issued in a user or OAuth 2.0 access token that the application expects.

Parameters.Name: Homepage
Parameters.Description: The URL to the application's homepage.

Parameters.Name: IdentifierUris
Parameters.Description: User-defined URI(s) that uniquely identify a Web application within its Azure AD tenant, or within a verified custom domain.

Parameters.Name: IsFallbackPublicClient
Parameters.Description: Specifies the fallback application type as public client, such as an installed application running on a mobile device. The default value is false, which means the fallback application type is confidential client such as web app. There are certain scenarios where Microsoft Entra ID cannot determine the client application type (for example, ROPC flow where it is configured without specifying a redirect URI). In those cases, Microsoft Entra ID will interpret the application type based on the value of this property.

Parameters.Name: KnownClientApplications
Parameters.Description: Client applications that are tied to this resource application.

Parameters.Name: OptionalClaims
Parameters.Description: Application developers can configure optional claims in their Microsoft Entra applications to specify the claims that are sent to their application by the Microsoft security token service. For more information, see How to: Provide optional claims to your app.

Parameters.Name: Api
Parameters.Description: Specifies settings for an application that implements a web API.

Parameters.Name: AuthenticationBehaviors
Parameters.Description: The collection of breaking change behaviors related to token issuance that are configured for the application. Authentication behaviors are unset by default (null) and must be explicitly enabled or disabled. Nullable. Returned only on $select.  For more information about authentication behaviors, see Manage application authenticationBehaviors to avoid unverified use of email claims for user identification or authorization.

Parameters.Name: PasswordCredentials
Parameters.Description: The collection of password credentials associated with the application. Not nullable.

Parameters.Name: KeyCredentials
Parameters.Description: The collection of key credentials associated with the application. Not nullable. Supports $filter (eq, not, ge, le).

Parameters.Name: AppRoles
Parameters.Description: The collection of roles defined for the application. With app role assignments, these roles can be assigned to users, groups, or service principals associated with other applications. Not nullable.

Parameters.Name: LogoutURL
Parameters.Description: The logout url for this application.

Parameters.Name: PublicClient
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether this application is a public client (such as an installed application running on a mobile device). Default is false.

Parameters.Name: ReplyURLs
Parameters.Description: Specifies the URLs that user tokens are sent to for sign in, or the redirect URIs that OAuth 2.0 authorization codes and access tokens are sent to.

Parameters.Name: Owners
Parameters.Description: UPN or ObjectID values of the app's owners.

Parameters.Name: OnPremisesPublishing
Parameters.Description: Represents the set of properties required for configuring Application Proxy for this application. Configuring these properties allows you to publish your on-premises application for secure remote access.

Parameters.Name: ApplicationTemplateId
Parameters.Description: Identifier of the associated Application Template.

Parameters.Name: PublicClientRedirectUris
Parameters.Description: List of public clients redirect URIs.

Parameters.Name: Spa
Parameters.Description: List of single page application settings.

Parameters.Name: SignInAudience
Parameters.Description: Specifies the Microsoft accounts that are supported for the current application. The possible values are: AzureADMyOrg (default), AzureADMultipleOrgs, AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount, and PersonalMicrosoftAccount

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if the Azure AD App should exist or not.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials for the Microsoft Graph delegated permissions.

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: ApplicationSecret
Parameters.Description: Secret of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: Permissions
Parameters.Description: API permissions for the Azure Active Directory Application.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity
Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Application.

Parameters.Name: MaxLifetime
Parameters.Description: String value that indicates the maximum lifetime for password expiration, defined as an ISO 8601 duration. For example, P4DT12H30M5S represents four days, 12 hours, 30 minutes, and five seconds. This property is required when restrictionType is set to passwordLifetime.

Parameters.Name: RestrictForAppsCreatedAfterDateTime
Parameters.Description: Specifies the date from which the policy restriction applies to newly created applications. For existing applications, the enforcement date can be retroactively applied.

Parameters.Name: RestrictionType
Parameters.Description: The type of restriction being applied. The possible values are: passwordAddition, passwordLifetime, symmetricKeyAddition, symmetricKeyLifetime, customPasswordAddition, and unknownFutureValue. Each value of restrictionType can be used only once per policy.

Parameters.Name: State
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether the restriction is evaluated. The possible values are: enabled, disabled, unknownFutureValue. If enabled, the restriction is evaluated. If disabled, the restriction isn't evaluated or enforced.

Parameters.Name: Id
Parameters.Description: Identifier used to reference the authentication context class. The id is used to trigger step-up authentication for the referenced authentication requirements and is the value that will be issued in the acrs claim of an access token. This value in the claim is used to verify that the required authentication context has been satisfied. The allowed values are c1 through c25.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: A friendly name that identifies the authenticationContextClassReference object when building user-facing admin experiences. For example, a selection UX

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: A short explanation of the policies that are enforced by authenticationContextClassReference. This value should be used to provide secondary text to describe the authentication context class reference when building user-facing admin experiences. For example, a selection UX.

Parameters.Name: IsAvailable
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether the authenticationContextClassReference has been published by the security admin and is ready for use by apps. When it's set to false, it shouldn't be shown in admin UX experiences because the value isn't currently available for selection.

Parameters.Description: The name of the policy.

Parameters.Name: PolicyMigrationState
Parameters.Description: The state of migration of the authentication methods policy from the legacy multifactor authentication and self-service password reset (SSPR) policies. The possible values are: premigration - means the authentication methods policy is used for authentication only, legacy policies are respected. migrationInProgress - means the authentication methods policy is used for both authentication and SSPR, legacy policies are respected. migrationComplete - means the authentication methods policy is used for authentication and SSPR, legacy policies are ignored. unknownFutureValue - Evolvable enumeration sentinel value. Do not use.

Parameters.Name: PolicyVersion
Parameters.Description: The version of the policy in use.

Parameters.Name: ReconfirmationInDays
Parameters.Description: Days before the user will be asked to reconfirm their method.

Parameters.Description: Enforce registration at sign-in time. This property can be used to remind users to set up targeted authentication methods.

Parameters.Name: ReportSuspiciousActivitySettings
Parameters.Description: Allows users to report suspicious activities if they receive an authentication request that they did not initiate.

Parameters.Name: DefaultLength
Parameters.Description: Default length in characters of a Temporary Access Pass object. Must be between 8 and 48 characters.

Parameters.Name: DefaultLifetimeInMinutes
Parameters.Description: Default lifetime in minutes for a Temporary Access Pass. Value can be any integer between the minimumLifetimeInMinutes and maximumLifetimeInMinutes.

Parameters.Name: IsUsableOnce
Parameters.Description: If true, all the passes in the tenant will be restricted to one-time use. If false, passes in the tenant can be created to be either one-time use or reusable.

Parameters.Name: MaximumLifetimeInMinutes
Parameters.Description: Maximum lifetime in minutes for any Temporary Access Pass created in the tenant. Value can be between 10 and 43200 minutes (equivalent to 30 days).

Parameters.Name: MinimumLifetimeInMinutes
Parameters.Description: Minimum lifetime in minutes for any Temporary Access Pass created in the tenant. Value can be between 10 and 43200 minutes (equivalent to 30 days).

Parameters.Description: The type of the X.509 certificate mode configuration rule. The possible values are: issuerSubject, policyOID, unknownFutureValue. Required.

Description: Azure AD Authentication Method Policy X509

Parameters.Name: Priority
Parameters.Description: The priority of the binding. Azure AD uses the binding with the highest priority. This value must be a non-negative integer and unique in the collection of objects in the certificateUserBindings property of an x509CertificateAuthenticationMethodConfiguration object. Required

Parameters.Name: UserProperty
Parameters.Description: Defines the Azure AD user property of the user object to use for the binding. The possible values are: userPrincipalName, onPremisesUserPrincipalName, email. Required.

Parameters.Name: X509CertificateField
Parameters.Description: The field on the X.509 certificate to use for the binding. The possible values are: PrincipalName, RFC822Name.

Parameters.Name: IsSingleInstance
Parameters.Description: Only valid value is 'Yes'.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: Display name for this policy.

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: Description of this policy.

Parameters.Name: AllowedToSignUpEmailBasedSubscriptions
Parameters.Description: Boolean Indicates whether users can sign up for email based subscriptions.

Parameters.Name: AllowedToUseSSPR
Parameters.Description: Boolean Indicates whether the Self-Serve Password Reset feature can be used by users on the tenant.

Parameters.Name: AllowEmailVerifiedUsersToJoinOrganization
Parameters.Description: Boolean Indicates whether a user can join the tenant by email validation.

Parameters.Name: AllowInvitesFrom
Parameters.Description: Indicates who can invite external users to the organization. Possible values are: None, AdminsAndGuestInviters, AdminsGuestInvitersAndAllMembers, Everyone. Everyone is the default setting for all cloud environments except US Government.

Parameters.Name: BlockMsolPowershell
Parameters.Description: Boolean To disable the use of MSOL PowerShell, set this property to true. This will also disable user-based access to the legacy service endpoint used by MSOL PowerShell. This does not affect Azure AD Connect or Microsoft Graph.

Parameters.Name: DefaultUserRoleAllowedToCreateApps
Parameters.Description: Boolean Indicates whether the default user role can create applications.

Parameters.Name: DefaultUserRoleAllowedToCreateSecurityGroups
Parameters.Description: Boolean Indicates whether the default user role can create security groups.

Parameters.Name: DefaultUserRoleAllowedToReadBitlockerKeysForOwnedDevice
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether the registered owners of a device can read their own BitLocker recovery keys with default user role.

Parameters.Name: DefaultUserRoleAllowedToCreateTenants
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether the default user role can create tenants. This setting corresponds to the Restrict non-admin users from creating tenants setting in the User settings menu in the Azure portal. When this setting is false, users assigned the Tenant Creator role can still create tenants.

Parameters.Name: DefaultUserRoleAllowedToReadOtherUsers
Parameters.Description: Boolean Indicates whether the default user role can read other users.

Parameters.Name: GuestUserRole
Parameters.Description: The role that should be granted to guest users. Refer to List unifiedRoleDefinitions to find the list of available role templates. Only supported roles today are User, Guest User, and Restricted Guest User (2af84b1e-32c8-42b7-82bc-daa82404023b).

Parameters.Name: PermissionGrantPolicyIdsAssignedToDefaultUserRole
Parameters.Description: String collection Indicates if user consent to apps is allowed, and if it is, which permission to grant consent and which app consent policy (permissionGrantPolicy) govern the permission for users to grant consent. Value should be in the format managePermissionGrantsForSelf.{id}, where {id} is the id of a built-in or custom app consent policy. An empty list indicates user consent to apps is disabled.

Description:
This resource configures the Azure Active Directory Authorization Policy.

The policy is managed using the BETA API, some properties may have been added in the API but not in the resource

## Azure AD Permissions

To authenticate via Microsoft Graph, this resource required the following Application permissions:

* **Automate**
  * Policy.Read.All
  * Policy.ReadWrite.Authorization

* **Export**
  * Policy.Read.All

NOTE: All permissions listed above require admin consent.

Parameters.Name: IsPhoneOneTimePasswordAuthenticationEnabled
Parameters.Description: The tenant admin can configure local accounts using phone number if the phone number and one-time password authentication method is enabled.

Parameters.Name: IncludeGuestOrExternalUserTypes
Parameters.Description: Represents the Included internal guests or external user types. This is a multi-valued property. Supported values are: b2bCollaborationGuest, b2bCollaborationMember, b2bDirectConnectUser, internalGuest, OtherExternalUser, serviceProvider and unknownFutureValue.

Parameters.Name: IncludeExternalTenantsMembershipKind
Parameters.Description: Represents the Included Tenants membership kind. The possible values are: all, enumerated, unknownFutureValue. enumerated references an object of conditionalAccessEnumeratedExternalTenants derived type.

Parameters.Name: IncludeExternalTenantsMembers
Parameters.Description: Represents the Included collection of tenant ids in the scope of Conditional Access for guests and external users policy targeting.

Parameters.Name: ExcludeGuestOrExternalUserTypes
Parameters.Description: Represents the Excluded internal guests or external user types. This is a multi-valued property. Supported values are: b2bCollaborationGuest, b2bCollaborationMember, b2bDirectConnectUser, internalGuest, OtherExternalUser, serviceProvider and unknownFutureValue.

Parameters.Name: ExcludeExternalTenantsMembershipKind
Parameters.Description: Represents the Excluded Tenants membership kind. The possible values are: all, enumerated, unknownFutureValue. enumerated references an object of conditionalAccessEnumeratedExternalTenants derived type.

Parameters.Description: Represents the Excluded collection of tenant ids in the scope of Conditional Access for guests and external users policy targeting.

Parameters.Description: Name of the attribute set. Case sensitive.

Parameters.Name: Id
Parameters.Description: Unique identifier of the Attribute Definition.

Parameters.Description: Description of the custom security attribute. Can be up to 128 characters long and include Unicode characters. Can't contain spaces or special characters. Can be changed later.

Parameters.Name: IsCollection
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether multiple values can be assigned to the custom security attribute. Can't be changed later. If type is set to Boolean, isCollection can't be set to true.

Parameters.Name: IsSearchable
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether custom security attribute values are indexed for searching on objects that are assigned attribute values. Can't be changed later.

Parameters.Name: Status
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the custom security attribute is active or deactivated. Acceptable values are Available and Deprecated. Can be changed later.

Parameters.Name: Type
Parameters.Description: Data type for the custom security attribute values. Supported types are: Boolean, Integer, and String. Can't be changed later.

Parameters.Name: UsePreDefinedValuesOnly
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether only predefined values can be assigned to the custom security attribute. If set to false, free-form values are allowed. Can later be changed from true to false, but can't be changed from false to true. If type is set to Boolean, usePreDefinedValuesOnly can't be set to true.

Description: Represents the policy scope that controls quota restrictions, additional authentication, and authorization policies to register device identities to your organization.

Parameters.Name: Id
Parameters.Description: Custom domain name.

Parameters.Name: AuthenticationType
Parameters.Description: Indicates the configured authentication type for the domain. The value is either Managed or Federated. Managed indicates a cloud managed domain where Microsoft Entra ID performs user authentication. Federated indicates authentication is federated with an identity provider such as the tenant's on-premises Active Directory via Active Directory Federation Services.

Parameters.Name: AvailabilityStatus
Parameters.Description: This property is always null except when the verify action is used. When the verify action is used, a domain entity is returned in the response. The availabilityStatus property of the domain entity in the response is either AvailableImmediately or EmailVerifiedDomainTakeoverScheduled.

Parameters.Name: IsAdminManaged
Parameters.Description: The value of the property is false if the DNS record management of the domain is delegated to Microsoft 365. Otherwise, the value is true. Not nullable

Parameters.Name: IsDefault
Parameters.Description: True if this is the default domain that is used for user creation. There's only one default domain per company. Not nullable.

Parameters.Name: IsRoot
Parameters.Description: True if the domain is a verified root domain. Otherwise, false if the domain is a subdomain or unverified. Not nullable.

Parameters.Name: IsVerified
Parameters.Description: True if the domain completed domain ownership verification. Not nullable.

Parameters.Name: PasswordNotificationWindowInDays
Parameters.Description: Specifies the number of days before a user receives notification that their password expires. If the property isn't set, a default value of 14 days is used.

Parameters.Name: PasswordValidityPeriodInDays
Parameters.Description: Specifies the length of time that a password is valid before it must be changed. If the property isn't set, a default value of 90 days is used.

Parameters.Name: SupportedServices
Parameters.Description: The capabilities assigned to the domain. Can include 0, 1 or more of following values: Email, Sharepoint, EmailInternalRelayOnly, OfficeCommunicationsOnline, SharePointDefaultDomain, FullRedelegation, SharePointPublic, OrgIdAuthentication, Yammer, Intune. The values that you can add or remove using the API include: Email, OfficeCommunicationsOnline, Yammer. Not nullable.

Parameters.Name: ApprovalMode
Parameters.Description: One of SingleStage, Serial, Parallel, NoApproval (default). NoApproval is used when isApprovalRequired is false.

Parameters.Name: ApprovalStages
Parameters.Description: If approval is required, the one or two elements of this collection define each of the stages of approval. An empty array if no approval is required.

Parameters.Name: IsApprovalRequired
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether approval is required for requests in this policy.

Parameters.Name: IsApprovalRequiredForExtension
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether approval is required for a user to extend their assignment.

Parameters.Name: IsRequestorJustificationRequired
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether the requestor is required to supply a justification in their request.

Parameters.Name: ApprovalStageTimeOutInDays
Parameters.Description: The number of days that a request can be pending a response before it is automatically denied.

Parameters.Name: EscalationTimeInMinutes
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether the approver is required to provide a justification for approving a request.

Parameters.Name: IsApproverJustificationRequired
Parameters.Description: If true, then one or more escalation approvers are configured in this approval stage.

Parameters.Name: IsEscalationEnabled
Parameters.Description: If escalation is required, the time a request can be pending a response from a primary approver.

Parameters.Name: PrimaryApprovers
Parameters.Description: The users who will be asked to approve requests. A collection of singleUser, groupMembers, requestorManager, internalSponsors and externalSponsors. When creating or updating a policy, include at least one userSet in this collection.

Parameters.Name: EscalationApprovers
Parameters.Description: If escalation is enabled and the primary approvers do not respond before the escalation time, the escalationApprovers are the users who will be asked to approve requests. This can be a collection of singleUser, groupMembers, requestorManager, internalSponsors and externalSponsors. When creating or updating a policy, if there are no escalation approvers, or escalation approvers are not required for the stage, the value of this property should be an empty collection.

Description:
This resource configures an Azure Active Directory group. IMPORTANT: It does not support mail enabled security groups or mail enabled groups that are not unified or dynamic groups.

If using with AADUser, be aware that if AADUser->MemberOf is being specified and the referenced group is configured with AADGroup->Member then a conflict may arise if the two don't match. It is usually best to choose only one of them. See AADUser

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: DisplayName of the Azure Active Directory Group

Parameters.Name: MailNickname
Parameters.Description: Specifies a mail nickname for the group.

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: Specifies a description for the group.

Parameters.Name: Id
Parameters.Description: Specifies an ID for the group.

Parameters.Name: Owners
Parameters.Description: User Service Principal values for the group's owners.

Parameters.Name: Members
Parameters.Description: User Service Principal values for the group's members.

Parameters.Name: GroupAsMembers
Parameters.Description: Displayname values for the groups member of the group.

Parameters.Name: MemberOf
Parameters.Description: DisplayName values for the groups that this group is a member of.

Parameters.Name: GroupTypes
Parameters.Description: Specifies that the group is a dynamic group. To create a dynamic group, specify a value of DynamicMembership.

Parameters.Name: MembershipRule
Parameters.Description: Specifies the membership rule for a dynamic group.

Parameters.Name: MembershipRuleProcessingState
Parameters.Description: Specifies the rule processing state. The acceptable values for this parameter are: On. Process the group rule or Paused. Stop processing the group rule.

Parameters.Name: SecurityEnabled
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the group is security enabled. For security groups, this value must be $True.

Parameters.Name: MailEnabled
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether this group is mail enabled. Currently, you cannot create mail enabled groups in Azure AD.

Parameters.Name: IsAssignableToRole
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether this group can be assigned a role. Only available when creating a group and can't be modified after group is created.

Parameters.Name: AssignedToRole
Parameters.Description: DisplayName values for the roles that the group is assigned to.

Parameters.Name: Visibility
Parameters.Description: This parameter determines the visibility of the group's content and members list.

Parameters.Name: AssignedLicenses
Parameters.Description: List of Licenses assigned to the group.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if the Azure AD Group should exist or not.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials for the Microsoft Graph delegated permissions.

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ApplicationSecret
Parameters.Description: Secret of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.

Description:
This resource configures an Azure Active Directory group. IMPORTANT: It does not support mail enabled security groups or mail enabled groups that are not unified or dynamic groups.

If using with AADUser, be aware that if AADUser->MemberOf is being specified and the referenced group is configured with AADGroup->Member then a conflict may arise if the two don't match. It is usually best to choose only one of them. See AADUser

Parameters.Name: Expiration
Parameters.Description: When the eligible or active assignment expires.

Parameters.Name: Recurrence
Parameters.Description: The frequency of the  eligible or active assignment. This property is currently unsupported in PIM.

Parameters.Name: StartDateTime
Parameters.Description: When the  eligible or active assignment becomes active.

Description: Azure AD Group Eligibility Schedule

Parameters.Name: Duration
Parameters.Description: The requestor's desired duration of access represented in ISO 8601 format for durations. For example, PT3H refers to three hours.  If specified in a request, endDateTime should not be present and the type property should be set to afterDuration.

Parameters.Name: EndDateTime
Parameters.Description: Timestamp of date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z.

Parameters.Name: Type
Parameters.Description: The requestor's desired expiration pattern type. The possible values are: notSpecified, noExpiration, afterDateTime, afterDuration.

Description: Azure AD Group Eligibility Schedule

Parameters.Name: Pattern
Parameters.Description: The frequency of an event.  For access reviews: Do not specify this property for a one-time access review.  Only interval, dayOfMonth, and type (weekly, absoluteMonthly) properties of recurrencePattern are supported.

Parameters.Name: Range
Parameters.Description: The duration of an event.

Description: Azure AD Group Eligibility Schedule

Parameters.Name: DayOfMonth
Parameters.Description: The day of the month on which the event occurs. Required if type is absoluteMonthly or absoluteYearly.

Parameters.Name: DaysOfWeek
Parameters.Description: A collection of the days of the week on which the event occurs. The possible values are: sunday, monday, tuesday, wednesday, thursday, friday, saturday. If type is relativeMonthly or relativeYearly, and daysOfWeek specifies more than one day, the event falls on the first day that satisfies the pattern.  Required if type is weekly, relativeMonthly, or relativeYearly.

Parameters.Name: FirstDayOfWeek
Parameters.Description: The first day of the week. The possible values are: sunday, monday, tuesday, wednesday, thursday, friday, saturday. Default is sunday. Required if type is weekly.

Parameters.Name: Index
Parameters.Description: Specifies on which instance of the allowed days specified in daysOfWeek the event occurs, counted from the first instance in the month. The possible values are: first, second, third, fourth, last. Default is first. Optional and used if type is relativeMonthly or relativeYearly.

Parameters.Name: Interval
Parameters.Description: The number of units between occurrences, where units can be in days, weeks, months, or years, depending on the type. Required.

Parameters.Name: Month
Parameters.Description: The month in which the event occurs.  This is a number from 1 to 12.

Parameters.Name: Type
Parameters.Description: The recurrence pattern type: daily, weekly, absoluteMonthly, relativeMonthly, absoluteYearly, relativeYearly. Required. For more information, see values of type property.

Description: Azure AD Group Eligibility Schedule

Parameters.Name: EndDate
Parameters.Description: The date to stop applying the recurrence pattern. Depending on the recurrence pattern of the event, the last occurrence of the meeting may not be this date. Required if type is endDate.

Parameters.Name: NumberOfOccurrences
Parameters.Description: The number of times to repeat the event. Required and must be positive if type is numbered.

Parameters.Name: RecurrenceTimeZone
Parameters.Description: Time zone for the startDate and endDate properties. Optional. If not specified, the time zone of the event is used.

Parameters.Name: StartDate
Parameters.Description: The date to start applying the recurrence pattern. The first occurrence of the meeting may be this date or later, depending on the recurrence pattern of the event. Must be the same value as the start property of the recurring event. Required.

Parameters.Name: Type
Parameters.Description: The recurrence range. The possible values are: endDate, noEnd, numbered. Required.

Description: Azure AD Group Eligibility Schedule

Parameters.Name: AccessId
Parameters.Description: The identifier of the membership or ownership eligibility to the group that is governed by PIM. Required. The possible values are: owner, member. Supports $filter (eq).

Parameters.Name: GroupId
Parameters.Description: The identifier of the group representing the scope of the membership or ownership eligibility through PIM for groups. Required. Supports $filter (eq).

Parameters.Name: GroupDisplayName
Parameters.Description: Displayname of the group representing the scope of the membership or ownership eligibility through PIM for groups.

Parameters.Name: MemberType
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether the assignment is derived from a group assignment. It can further imply whether the caller can manage the schedule. Required. The possible values are: direct, group, unknownFutureValue. Supports $filter (eq).

Parameters.Name: PrincipalId
Parameters.Description: The identifier of the principal whose membership or ownership eligibility is granted through PIM for groups. Required. Supports $filter (eq).

Parameters.Name: PrincipalType
Parameters.Description: Principal type user or group

Parameters.Name: PrincipalDisplayName
Parameters.Description: Displayname of the Principal

Parameters.Name: ScheduleInfo
Parameters.Description: Represents the period of the access assignment or eligibility. The scheduleInfo can represent a single occurrence or multiple recurring instances. Required.

Parameters.Description: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

Parameters.Description: Boolean indicating whether or not a guest user can have access to Office 365 groups content. This setting does not require an Azure Active Directory Premium P1 license.

Parameters.Description: Name of the security group for which the members are allowed to create Office 365 groups even when EnableGroupCreation == false.

Parameters.Name: UsageGuidelinesUrl

Parameters.Description: Boolean, a tenant-wide setting that assigns the default value to the writebackConfiguration/isEnabled property of new groups, if the property isn't specified during group creation. This setting is applicable when group writeback is configured in Microsoft Entra Connect.

Parameters.Description: Only valid value is 'Yes'.

Parameters.Name: SenderDomain
Parameters.Description: Specifies the domain that should be used when sending email notifications. This domain must be verified in order to be used. We recommend that you use a domain that has the appropriate DNS records to facilitate email validation, like SPF, DKIM, DMARC, and MX, because this then complies with the RFC compliance for sending and receiving email. For details, see Learn more about Exchange Online Email Routing.

Parameters.Name: WorkflowScheduleIntervalInHours
Parameters.Description: The interval in hours at which all workflows running in the tenant should be scheduled for execution. This interval has a minimum value of 1 and a maximum value of 24. The default value is 3 hours.

Parameters.Name: UseCompanyBranding
Parameters.Description: Specifies if the organization's banner logo should be included in email notifications. The banner logo will replace the Microsoft logo at the top of the email notification. If true the banner logo will be taken from the tenant's branding settings. This value can only be set to true if the organizationalBranding bannerLogo property is set.

Description: Defines an optional cross-tenant access policy template with user synchronization settings for multitenant organization tenants.

Parameters.Name: IsSingleInstance
Parameters.Description: Only valid value is 'Yes'.

Parameters.Name: TemplateApplicationLevel
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the template will be applied to user synchronization settings of certain tenants. The possible values are: none, newPartners, existingPartners, unknownFutureValue. You can also specify multiple values like newPartners,existingPartners (default). none indicates the template is not applied to any new or existing partner tenants. newPartners indicates the template is applied to new partner tenants. existingPartners indicates the template is applied to existing partner tenants, those who already had partner-specific user synchronization settings in place.

Parameters.Name: UserSyncInbound
Parameters.Description: Determines whether users can be synchronized from the partner tenant. false causes any current user synchronization from the source tenant to the target tenant to stop. This property has no impact on existing users who have already been synchronized.

Parameters.Name: endDate
Parameters.Description: The date to stop applying the recurrence pattern. Depending on the recurrence pattern of the event, the last occurrence of the meeting may not be this date.

Parameters.Name: numberOfOccurrences
Parameters.Description: The number of times to repeat the event. Required and must be positive if type is numbered.

Parameters.Name: recurrenceTimeZone
Parameters.Description: Time zone for the startDate and endDate properties.

Parameters.Name: startDate
Parameters.Description: The date to start applying the recurrence pattern. The first occurrence of the meeting may be this date or later, depending on the recurrence pattern of the event. Must be the same value as the start property of the recurring event.

Parameters.Name: type
Parameters.Description: The recurrence range. The possible values are: endDate, noEnd, numbered.

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Privilege Identity Management assignment.

Parameters.Name: dayOfMonth
Parameters.Description: The day of the month on which the event occurs.

Parameters.Name: daysOfWeek
Parameters.Description: A collection of the days of the week on which the event occurs. The possible values are: sunday, monday, tuesday, wednesday, thursday, friday, saturday

Parameters.Name: firstDayOfWeek
Parameters.Description: The first day of the week.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: Specifies on which instance of the allowed days specified in daysOfWeek the event occurs, counted from the first instance in the month. The possible values are: first, second, third, fourth, last.

Parameters.Name: interval
Parameters.Description: The number of units between occurrences, where units can be in days, weeks, months, or years, depending on the type.

Parameters.Name: month
Parameters.Description: The month in which the event occurs. This is a number from 1 to 12.

Parameters.Name: type
Parameters.Description: The recurrence pattern type: daily, weekly, absoluteMonthly, relativeMonthly, absoluteYearly, relativeYearly.

Parameters.Description: The frequency of an event.

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Privilege Identity Management assignment.

Parameters.Name: duration
Parameters.Description: The requestor's desired duration of access represented in ISO 8601 format for durations. For example, PT3H refers to three hours. If specified in a request, endDateTime should not be present and the type property should be set to afterDuration.

Parameters.Name: endDateTime
Parameters.Description: Timestamp of date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z.

Parameters.Name: type
Parameters.Description: The requestor's desired expiration pattern type. The possible values are: notSpecified, noExpiration, afterDateTime, afterDuration.

Description: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Privilege Identity Management assignment.

Parameters.Name: Principal
Parameters.Description: User Principal Name of the eligibility request.

Parameters.Name: RoleDefinition
Parameters.Description: Role associated with the eligibility request.

Parameters.Name: PrincipalType
Parameters.Description: Represented the type of principal to assign the request to. Accepted values are: Group and User.

Parameters.Name: DirectoryScopeId
Parameters.Description: Identifier of the directory object representing the scope of the role eligibility. The scope of an role eligibility determines the set of resources for which the principal has been granted access. Directory scopes are shared scopes stored in the directory that are understood by multiple applications. Use / for tenant-wide scope. Use appScopeId to limit the scope to an application only. Either directoryScopeId or appScopeId is required.

Parameters.Name: Id
Parameters.Description: Identifier for the Role Eligibility Schedule Request.

Parameters.Name: AppScopeId
Parameters.Description: Identifier of the app-specific scope when the role eligibility is scoped to an app. The scope of a role eligibility determines the set of resources for which the principal is eligible to access. App scopes are scopes that are defined and understood by this application only. Use / for tenant-wide app scopes. Use directoryScopeId to limit the scope to particular directory objects, for example, administrative units. Either directoryScopeId or appScopeId is required.

Parameters.Name: Action
Parameters.Description: Represents the type of operation on the role eligibility request.The possible values are: adminAssign, adminUpdate, adminRemove, selfActivate, selfDeactivate, adminExtend, adminRenew, selfExtend, selfRenew, unknownFutureValue.

Parameters.Name: IsValidationOnly
Parameters.Description: Determines whether the call is a validation or an actual call. Only set this property if you want to check whether an activation is subject to additional rules like MFA before actually submitting the request.

Parameters.Name: Justification
Parameters.Description: A message provided by users and administrators when create they create the unifiedRoileAssignmentScheduleRequest object. Optional when action is adminRemove. Whether this property is required or optional is also dependent on the settings for the Azure AD role.

Parameters.Name: ScheduleInfo
Parameters.Description: The period of the role eligibility. Optional when action is adminRemove. The period of eligibility is dependent on the settings of the Azure AD role.

Parameters.Name: TicketInfo
Parameters.Description: Ticket details linked to the role eligibility request including details of the ticket number and ticket system.

Parameters.Description: Present ensures the instance exists, absent ensures it is removed.

Parameters.Description: Specifies the date from which the policy restriction applies to newly created applications. For existing applications, the enforcement date can be retroactively applied.

Parameters.Name: RestrictionType
Parameters.Description: The type of restriction being applied. The possible values are: passwordAddition, passwordLifetime, symmetricKeyAddition, symmetricKeyLifetime, customPasswordAddition, and unknownFutureValue. Each value of restrictionType can be used only once per policy.

Parameters.Name: State
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether the restriction is evaluated. The possible values are: enabled, disabled, unknownFutureValue. If enabled, the restriction is evaluated. If disabled, the restriction isn't evaluated or enforced.

Description:
Enables or disables Microsoft Defender plans for a subscription in Microsoft Defender for Cloud.
For more information about the available Defender plans, sub plans and plan extensions refer to Defender for Cloud onboarding API documentation.
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/defenderforcloud/pricings/update?view=rest-defenderforcloud-2024-01-01&tabs=HTTP


To have all security features enabled during plan enablement, make sure to assign the required Azure RBAC permissions to the application running this module.
For more information about the required permissions refer to the documentation https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/defender-for-cloud/permissions.
