Parameters.Description: Specifies whether push notifications are allowed for Apple mobile devices.

Parameters.Name: AllowBluetooth
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the Bluetooth capabilities of the mobile phone are allowed.

Parameters.Name: AllowBrowser
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether Microsoft Pocket Internet Explorer is allowed on the mobile phone.

Parameters.Name: AllowCamera
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the mobile phone's camera is allowed.

Parameters.Name: AllowConsumerEmail
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the mobile phone user can configure a personal email account on the device.

Parameters.Name: AllowDesktopSync
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the mobile phone can synchronize with a desktop computer through a cable.

Parameters.Name: AllowExternalDeviceManagement
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether an external device management program is allowed to manage the device.

Parameters.Name: AllowHTMLEmail
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether HTML email is enabled on the device.

Parameters.Name: AllowInternetSharing
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the mobile phone can be used as a modem to connect a computer to the Internet.

Parameters.Name: AllowIrDA
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether infrared connections are allowed to the mobile phone.

Parameters.Name: AllowMobileOTAUpdate
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether certain updates are seen by devices that implemented support for this restricting functionality.

Parameters.Name: AllowNonProvisionableDevices
Parameters.Description: Enables all devices to synchronize with the computer running Exchange, regardless of whether the device can enforce all the specific settings established in the Mobile Device mailbox policy.

Parameters.Name: AllowPOPIMAPEmail
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the user can configure a POP3 or IMAP4 email account on the device.

Parameters.Name: AllowRemoteDesktop
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the mobile phone can initiate a remote desktop connection.

Parameters.Name: AllowSimpleDevicePassword
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether a simple device password is allowed.

Parameters.Name: AllowSMIMEEncryptionAlgorithmNegotiation
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the messaging application on the device can negotiate the encryption algorithm in case a recipient's certificate doesn't support the specified encryption algorithm.

Parameters.Name: AllowSMIMESoftCerts
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether S/MIME software certificates are allowed.

Parameters.Name: AllowStorageCard
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the device can access information stored on a storage card.

Parameters.Name: AllowTextMessaging
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether text messaging is allowed from the device.

Parameters.Name: AllowUnsignedApplications
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether unsigned applications can be installed on the device.

Parameters.Name: AllowUnsignedInstallationPackages
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether unsigned installation packages can be run on the device.

Parameters.Name: AllowWiFi
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether wireless Internet access is allowed on the device.

Parameters.Name: AlphanumericDevicePasswordRequired
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the device password must be alphanumeric.

Parameters.Name: ApprovedApplicationList
Parameters.Description: Specifies a list of approved applications for the device.

Parameters.Name: AttachmentsEnabled
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the user can download attachments.

Parameters.Name: DeviceEncryptionEnabled
Parameters.Description: Enables device encryption on the mobile phone.

Parameters.Name: DevicePasswordEnabled
Parameters.Description: Specifies that the user set a password for the device.

Parameters.Name: DevicePasswordExpiration
Parameters.Description: Specifies the length of time, in days, that a password can be used.

Parameters.Name: DevicePasswordHistory
Parameters.Description: Specifies the number of previously used passwords to store.

Parameters.Name: DevicePolicyRefreshInterval
Parameters.Description: Specifies how often the policy is sent from the server to the mobile phone

Parameters.Name: IrmEnabled
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether Information Rights Management (IRM) is enabled for the mailbox policy.

Parameters.Name: IsDefault
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether this policy is the default Mobile Device mailbox policy.

Parameters.Name: IsDefaultPolicy
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether this policy is the default Mobile Device mailbox policy.

Parameters.Name: MaxAttachmentSize
Parameters.Description: Specifies the maximum size of attachments that can be downloaded to the mobile phone.

Parameters.Name: MaxCalendarAgeFilter
Parameters.Description: Specifies the maximum range of calendar days that can be synchronized to the device.

Parameters.Name: MaxDevicePasswordFailedAttempts
Parameters.Description: Specifies the number of attempts a user can make to enter the correct password for the device.

Parameters.Name: MaxEmailAgeFilter
Parameters.Description: Specifies the maximum number of days of email items to synchronize to the device.

Parameters.Name: MaxEmailBodyTruncationSize
Parameters.Description: Specifies the maximum size at which email messages are truncated when synchronized to the device.

Parameters.Name: MaxEmailHTMLBodyTruncationSize
Parameters.Description: Specifies the maximum size at which HTML-formatted email messages are synchronized to the device.

Parameters.Name: MaxInactivityTimeDeviceLock
Parameters.Description: Specifies the length of time that the device can be inactive before the password is required to reactivate the device.

Parameters.Name: MinDevicePasswordComplexCharacters
Parameters.Description: Specifies the minimum number of complex characters required in a device password.

Parameters.Name: MinDevicePasswordLength
Parameters.Description: Specifies the minimum number of characters in the device password.

Parameters.Name: PasswordRecoveryEnabled
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether you can store the recovery password for the device on an Exchange server.

Parameters.Name: RequireDeviceEncryption
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether encryption is required on the device.

Parameters.Name: RequireEncryptedSMIMEMessages
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether you must encrypt S/MIME messages.

Parameters.Name: RequireEncryptionSMIMEAlgorithm
Parameters.Description: Specifies what required algorithm must be used when encrypting a message.

Parameters.Name: RequireManualSyncWhenRoaming
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the device must synchronize manually while roaming.

Parameters.Name: RequireSignedSMIMEAlgorithm
Parameters.Description: Specifies what required algorithm must be used when signing a message.

Parameters.Name: RequireSignedSMIMEMessages
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the device must send signed S/MIME messages.

Parameters.Name: RequireStorageCardEncryption
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether encryption of a storage card is required.

Parameters.Name: UnapprovedInROMApplicationList
Parameters.Description: Specifies a list of applications that can't be run in ROM.

Parameters.Name: UNCAccessEnabled
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether access to Microsoft Windows file shares is enabled.

Parameters.Name: WSSAccessEnabled
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether access to Microsoft Windows SharePoint Services is enabled.

Parameters.Name: AddressLists

Parameters.Name: GlobalAddressList
Parameters.Description: The GlobalAddressList parameter specifies the identity of the global address list (GAL) that will be used by mailbox users who are assigned this address book policy. You can specify only one GAL for each address book policy.

Parameters.Name: OfflineAddressBook
Parameters.Description: The OfflineAddressBook parameter specifies the identity of the offline address book (OAB) that will be used by mailbox users who are assigned this address book policy. You can specify only one OAB for each address book policy.

Description: This resource configures Exchange Online address lists.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the name of the antiphishing policy that you want to modify.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if this policy should exist or not.

Parameters.Name: AdminDisplayName
Parameters.Description: The AdminDisplayName parameter specifies a description for the policy.

Parameters.Name: PhishThresholdLevel
Parameters.Description: The PhishThresholdLevel parameter specifies the tolerance level that's used by machine learning in the handling of phishing messages.

Parameters.Name: AuthenticationFailAction
Parameters.Description: The AuthenticationFailAction parameter specifies the action to take when the message fails composite authentication.

Parameters.Name: TargetedUserProtectionAction
Parameters.Description: The TargetedUserProtectionAction parameter specifies the action to take on detected user impersonation messages for the users specified by the TargetedUsersToProtect parameter.

Parameters.Name: Enabled
Parameters.Description: Specify if this policy should be enabled. Default is $true.

Parameters.Name: EnableFirstContactSafetyTips
Parameters.Description: The EnableFirstContactSafetyTips parameter specifies whether to enable or disable the safety tip that's shown when recipients first receive an email from a sender or do not often receive email from a sender.

Parameters.Name: EnableMailboxIntelligence
Parameters.Description: The EnableMailboxIntelligence parameter specifies whether to enable or disable mailbox intelligence (the first contact graph) in domain and user impersonation protection.

Parameters.Name: EnableMailboxIntelligenceProtection
Parameters.Description: The EnableMailboxIntelligenceProtection specifies whether to enable or disable enhanced impersonation results based on each user's individual sender map. This intelligence allows Microsoft 365 to customize user impersonation detection and better handle false positives.

Parameters.Name: EnableOrganizationDomainsProtection
Parameters.Description: The EnableOrganizationDomainsProtection parameter specifies whether to enable domain impersonation protection for all registered domains in the Office 365 organization.

Parameters.Name: EnableSimilarDomainsSafetyTips
Parameters.Description: The EnableSimilarDomainsSafetyTips parameter specifies whether to enable safety tips that are shown to recipients in messages for domain impersonation detections.

Parameters.Name: EnableSimilarUsersSafetyTips
Parameters.Description: The EnableSimilarUsersSafetyTips parameter specifies whether to enable safety tips that are shown to recipients in messages for user impersonation detections.

Parameters.Name: EnableSpoofIntelligence
Parameters.Description: The EnableSpoofIntelligence parameter specifies whether to enable or disable antispoofing protection for the policy.

Parameters.Name: EnableTargetedDomainsProtection
Parameters.Description: The EnableTargetedDomainsProtection parameter specifies whether to enable domain impersonation protection for a list of specified domains.

Parameters.Name: EnableTargetedUserProtection
Parameters.Description: The EnableTargetedUserProtection parameter specifies whether to enable user impersonation protection for the users specified by the TargetedUsersToProtect parameter

Parameters.Name: EnableUnauthenticatedSender
Parameters.Description: The EnableUnauthenticatedSender parameter enables or disables unauthenticated sender identification in Outlook.

Parameters.Name: EnableUnusualCharactersSafetyTips
Parameters.Description: The EnableUnusualCharactersSafetyTips parameter specifies whether to enable safety tips that are shown to recipients in messages for unusual characters in domain and user impersonation detections.

Parameters.Name: EnableViaTag
Parameters.Description: This setting is part of spoof protection. The EnableViaTag parameter enables or disables adding the via tag to the From address in Outlook.

Parameters.Name: MakeDefault
Parameters.Description: Make this the default antiphishing policy

Parameters.Name: ExcludedDomains
Parameters.Description: The ExcludedDomains parameter specifies trusted domains that are excluded from scanning by antiphishing protection. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ExcludedSenders
Parameters.Description: The ExcludedSenders parameter specifies a list of trusted sender email addresses that are excluded from scanning by antiphishing protection. You can specify multiple email addresses separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: HonorDmarcPolicy
Parameters.Description: The HonorDmarcPolicy enables or disables using the sender's DMARC policy to determine what to do to messages that fail DMARC checks.

Parameters.Name: ImpersonationProtectionState
Parameters.Description: The ImpersonationProtectionState parameter specifies the configuration of impersonation protection.

Parameters.Name: MailboxIntelligenceProtectionAction
Parameters.Description: The MailboxIntelligenceProtectionAction parameter specifies what to do with messages that fail mailbox intelligence protection.

Parameters.Name: MailboxIntelligenceProtectionActionRecipients
Parameters.Description: The MailboxIntelligenceProtectionActionRecipients parameter specifies the recipients to add to detected messages when the MailboxIntelligenceProtectionAction parameter is set to the value Redirect or BccMessage.

Parameters.Name: MailboxIntelligenceQuarantineTag
Parameters.Description: The MailboxIntelligenceQuarantineTag specifies the quarantine policy that's used on messages that are quarantined by mailbox intelligence.

Parameters.Name: SpoofQuarantineTag
Parameters.Description: The SpoofQuarantineTag specifies the quarantine policy that's used on messages that are quarantined by spoof intelligence.

Parameters.Name: TargetedDomainActionRecipients
Parameters.Description: The TargetedDomainActionRecipients parameter specifies the recipients to add to detected domain impersonation messages when the TargetedDomainProtectionAction parameter is set to the value Redirect or BccMessage. A valid value for this parameter is an email address. You can specify multiple email addresses separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: TargetedDomainProtectionAction
Parameters.Description: The TargetedDomainProtectionAction parameter specifies the action to take on detected domain impersonation messages.

Parameters.Name: TargetedDomainsToProtect
Parameters.Description: The TargetedDomainsToProtect parameter specifies the domains that are included in domain impersonation protection when the EnableTargetedDomainsProtection parameter is set to $true.

Parameters.Name: TargetedDomainQuarantineTag
Parameters.Description: The TargetedDomainQuarantineTag specifies the quarantine policy that's used on messages that are quarantined by domain impersonation protection.

Parameters.Name: TargetedUserActionRecipients
Parameters.Description: The TargetedUserActionRecipients parameter specifies the replacement or additional recipients for detected user impersonation messages when the TargetedUserProtectionAction parameter is set to the value Redirect or BccMessage. A valid value for this parameter is an email address. You can specify multiple email addresses separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: TargetedUsersToProtect
Parameters.Description: The TargetedUsersToProtect parameter specifies the users that are included in user impersonation protection when the EnableTargetedUserProtection parameter is set to $true.

Parameters.Name: TargetedUserQuarantineTag
Parameters.Description: The TargetedUserQuarantineTag specifies the quarantine policy that's used on messages that are quarantined by user impersonation protection.

Parameters.Name: DmarcQuarantineAction
Parameters.Description: The DmarcQuarantineAction parameter specifies the action to take when a message fails DMARC checks and the sender's DMARC policy is p=quarantine

Parameters.Name: DmarcRejectAction
Parameters.Description: The DmarcRejectAction parameter specifies the action to take when a message fails DMARC checks and the sender's DMARC policy is p=reject.

Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Exchange Global Admin

Description:
This resource configures an Anti-Phish Policy in Exchange Online.
Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/advanced-threat-protection/new-antiphishpolicy?view=exchange-ps

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the name of the antiphishing rule that you want to modify.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if this rule should exist or not.

Parameters.Name: AntiPhishPolicy
Parameters.Description: The AntiPhishPolicy parameter specifies the name of the antiphishing policy that's associated with the antiphishing rule.

Parameters.Name: Enabled
Parameters.Description: Specify if this rule should be enabled. Default is $true.

Parameters.Name: Priority
Parameters.Description: The Priority parameter specifies a priority value for the rule that determines the order of rule processing. A lower integer value indicates a higher priority, the value 0 is the highest priority, and rules can't have the same priority value.

Parameters.Name: Comments
Parameters.Description: The Comments parameter specifies informative comments for the rule, such as what the rule is used for or how it has changed over time. The length of the comment can't exceed 1024 characters.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSentTo
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSentTo parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSentToMemberOf
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSentToMemberOf parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages sent to members of groups. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

Parameters.Name: RecipientDomainIs
Parameters.Description: The RecipientDomainIs parameter specifies a condition that looks for recipients with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: SentTo
Parameters.Description: The SentTo parameter specifies a condition that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

Parameters.Name: SentToMemberOf
Parameters.Description: The SentToMemberOf parameter looks for messages sent to members of groups. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Exchange Global Admin

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificatePassword
Parameters.Description: Username can be made up to anything but password will be used for CertificatePassword

Parameters.Name: CertificatePath
Parameters.Description: Path to certificate used in service principal usually a PFX file.

Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity
Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Description:
This resource configures the Advanced Threat Protection (ATP) policy
in Office 365.  Tenant must be subscribed to ATP.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: Identifier for the rule

Parameters.Name: Enabled
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the rule is enabled

Parameters.Name: Comments
Parameters.Description: Informative comments for the rule, such as what the rule is used for or how it has changed over time. The length of the comment can't exceed 1024 characters.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs
Parameters.Description: Specifies an exception that looks for recipients with email addresses in the specified domains.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSentTo
Parameters.Description: Specifies an exception that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSentToMemberOf
Parameters.Description: Specifies an exception that looks for messages sent to members of groups. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: Unique name for the rule. The maximum length is 64 characters.

Parameters.Name: Priority
Parameters.Description: Specifies a priority value for the rule that determines the order of rule processing. A lower integer value indicates a higher priority, the value 0 is the highest priority, and rules can't have the same priority value.

Parameters.Name: RecipientDomainIs
Parameters.Description: Specifies a condition that looks for recipients with email addresses in the specified domains.

Parameters.Name: SafeAttachmentPolicy
Parameters.Description: Specifies the existing Safe Attachments policy that's associated with the preset security policy.

Parameters.Name: SafeLinksPolicy
Parameters.Description: Specifies the existing Safe Links policy that's associated with the preset security policy.

Parameters.Name: SentTo
Parameters.Description: Specifies a condition that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

Parameters.Name: SentToMemberOf
Parameters.Description: Specifies a condition that looks for messages sent to members of distribution groups, dynamic distribution groups, or mail-enabled security groups.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Present ensures the instance exists, absent ensures it is removed.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the workload's Admin

Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity
Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the authentication policy you want to view or modify.

Parameters.Name: AllowBasicAuthActiveSync
Parameters.Description: The AllowBasicAuthActiveSync switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with Exchange Active Sync.

Parameters.Name: AllowBasicAuthAutodiscover
Parameters.Description: The AllowBasicAuthAutodiscover switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with Autodiscover.

Parameters.Name: AllowBasicAuthImap
Parameters.Description: The AllowBasicAuthImap switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with IMAP.

Parameters.Name: AllowBasicAuthMapi
Parameters.Description: The AllowBasicAuthMapi switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with MAPI.

Parameters.Name: AllowBasicAuthOfflineAddressBook
Parameters.Description: The AllowBasicAuthOfflineAddressBook switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with Offline Address Books.

Parameters.Name: AllowBasicAuthOutlookService
Parameters.Description: The AllowBasicAuthOutlookService switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with the Outlook service.

Parameters.Name: AllowBasicAuthPop
Parameters.Description: The AllowBasicAuthPop switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with POP.

Parameters.Name: AllowBasicAuthPowershell
Parameters.Description: The AllowBasicAuthPowerShell switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with PowerShell.

Parameters.Name: AllowBasicAuthReportingWebServices
Parameters.Description: The AllowBasicAuthReporting Web Services switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with reporting web services.

Parameters.Name: AllowBasicAuthRpc
Parameters.Description: The AllowBasicAuthRpc switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with RPC.

Parameters.Name: AllowBasicAuthSmtp
Parameters.Description: The AllowBasicAuthSmtp switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with SMTP.

Parameters.Name: AllowBasicAuthWebServices
Parameters.Description: The AllowBasicAuthWebServices switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with Exchange Web Services (EWS).

Parameters.Description: The Credentials parameter specifies the username and password that's used to access the Availability services in the target forest.

Parameters.Name: ForestName
Parameters.Description: The ForestName parameter specifies the SMTP domain name of the target forest for users whose free/busy data must be retrieved. If your users are distributed among multiple SMTP domains in the target forest, run the Add-AvailabilityAddressSpace command once for each SMTP domain.

Parameters.Name: TargetAutodiscoverEpr
Parameters.Description: The TargetAutodiscoverEpr parameter specifies the Autodiscover URL of Exchange Web Services for the external organization. Exchange uses Autodiscover to automatically detect the correct server endpoint for external requests.

Parameters.Name: TargetServiceEpr
Parameters.Description: The TargetServiceEpr parameter specifies the Exchange Online Calendar Service URL of the external Microsoft 365 organization that you're trying to read free/busy information from.

Parameters.Name: TargetTenantId
Parameters.Description: The TargetTenantID parameter specifies the tenant ID of the external Microsoft 365 organization that you're trying to read free/busy information from.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the resource mailbox that you want to view. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: AddAdditionalResponse
Parameters.Description: The AddAdditionalResponse parameter specifies whether additional information (the value of the AdditionalResponse parameter) is added to meeting request responses

Parameters.Name: AdditionalResponse
Parameters.Description: The AdditionalResponse parameter specifies the additional information to be included in responses to meeting requests when the value of the AddAdditionalResponse parameter is $true. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Parameters.Name: AddNewRequestsTentatively
Parameters.Description: The AddNewRequestsTentatively parameter specifies whether new meeting requests are added to the calendar as tentative

Parameters.Name: AddOrganizerToSubject
Parameters.Description: The AddOrganizerToSubject parameter specifies whether the meeting organizer's name is used as the subject of the meeting request.

Parameters.Name: AllBookInPolicy
Parameters.Description: The AllBookInPolicy parameter specifies whether to automatically approve in-policy requests from all users to the resource mailbox.

Parameters.Name: AllowConflicts
Parameters.Description: The AllowConflicts parameter specifies whether to allow conflicting meeting requests.

Parameters.Name: AllowRecurringMeetings
Parameters.Description: The AllowRecurringMeetings parameter specifies whether to allow recurring meetings in meeting requests.

Parameters.Name: AllRequestInPolicy
Parameters.Description: The AllRequestInPolicy parameter specifies whether to allow all users to submit in-policy requests to the resource mailbox.

Parameters.Name: AllRequestOutOfPolicy
Parameters.Description: The AllRequestOutOfPolicy parameter specifies whether to allow all users to submit out-of-policy requests to the resource mailbox.

Parameters.Name: AutomateProcessing
Parameters.Description: The AutomateProcessing parameter enables or disables calendar processing on the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: BookingType
Parameters.Description: The BookingType parameter specifies how reservations work on the resource mailbox.

Parameters.Name: BookingWindowInDays
Parameters.Description: The BookingWindowInDays parameter specifies the maximum number of days in advance that the resource can be reserved. A valid value is an integer from 0 through 1080. The default value is 180 days. The value 0 means today.

Parameters.Name: BookInPolicy
Parameters.Description: The BookInPolicy parameter specifies users or groups who are allowed to submit in-policy meeting requests to the resource mailbox that are automatically approved. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the user or group.

Parameters.Name: ConflictPercentageAllowed
Parameters.Description: The ConflictPercentageAllowed parameter specifies the maximum percentage of meeting conflicts for new recurring meeting requests. A valid value is an integer from 0 through 100. The default value is 0.

Parameters.Name: DeleteAttachments
Parameters.Description: The DeleteAttachments parameter specifies whether to remove attachments from all incoming messages.

Parameters.Name: DeleteComments
Parameters.Description: The DeleteComments parameter specifies whether to remove or keep any text in the message body of incoming meeting requests.

Parameters.Name: DeleteNonCalendarItems
Parameters.Description: The DeleteNonCalendarItems parameter specifies whether to remove or keep all non-calendar-related messages that are received by the resource mailbox.

Parameters.Name: DeleteSubject
Parameters.Description: The DeleteSubject parameter specifies whether to remove or keep the subject of incoming meeting requests.

Parameters.Name: EnableAutoRelease
Parameters.Description: N/A

Parameters.Name: EnableResponseDetails
Parameters.Description: The EnableResponseDetails parameter specifies whether to include the reasons for accepting or declining a meeting in the response email message.

Parameters.Name: EnforceCapacity
Parameters.Description: The EnforceCapacity parameter specifies whether to restrict the number of attendees to the capacity of the workspace. For example, if capacity is set to 10, then only 10 people can book the workspace.

Parameters.Name: EnforceSchedulingHorizon
Parameters.Description: The EnforceSchedulingHorizon parameter controls the behavior of recurring meetings that extend beyond the date specified by the BookingWindowInDays parameter.

Parameters.Name: ForwardRequestsToDelegates
Parameters.Description: The ForwardRequestsToDelegates parameter specifies whether to forward incoming meeting requests to the delegates that are configured for the resource mailbox.

Parameters.Name: MaximumConflictInstances
Parameters.Description: The MaximumConflictInstances parameter specifies the maximum number of conflicts for new recurring meeting requests when the AllowRecurringMeetings parameter is set to $true. A valid value is an integer from 0 through INT32 (2147483647). The default value is 0.

Parameters.Name: MaximumDurationInMinutes
Parameters.Description: The MaximumDurationInMinutes parameter specifies the maximum duration in minutes for meeting requests. A valid value is an integer from 0 through INT32 (2147483647). The default value is 1440 (24 hours).

Parameters.Name: MinimumDurationInMinutes
Parameters.Description: The MinimumDurationInMinutes parameter specifies the minimum duration in minutes for meeting requests in workspace mailboxes. A valid value is an integer from 0 through INT32 (2147483647). The default value is 0, which means there is no minimum duration.

Parameters.Name: OrganizerInfo
Parameters.Description: The OrganizerInfo parameter specifies whether the resource mailbox sends organizer information when a meeting request is declined because of conflicts.

Parameters.Name: PostReservationMaxClaimTimeInMinutes
Parameters.Description: N/A

Parameters.Name: ProcessExternalMeetingMessages
Parameters.Description: The ProcessExternalMeetingMessages parameter specifies whether to process meeting requests that originate outside the Exchange organization.

Parameters.Name: RemoveCanceledMeetings
Parameters.Description: The RemoveCanceledMeetings parameter specifies whether to automatically delete meetings that were cancelled by the organizer from the resource mailbox's calendar.

Parameters.Name: RemoveForwardedMeetingNotifications
Parameters.Description: The RemoveForwardedMeetingNotifications parameter specifies whether forwarded meeting notifications are moved to the Deleted Items folder after they're processed by the Calendar Attendant.

Parameters.Name: RemoveOldMeetingMessages
Parameters.Description: The RemoveOldMeetingMessages parameter specifies whether the Calendar Attendant removes old and redundant updates and responses.

Parameters.Name: RemovePrivateProperty
Parameters.Description: The RemovePrivateProperty parameter specifies whether to clear the private flag for incoming meetings that were sent by the organizer in the original requests.

Parameters.Name: RequestInPolicy
Parameters.Description: The RemovePrivateProperty parameter specifies whether to clear the private flag for incoming meetings that were sent by the organizer in the original requests.

Parameters.Name: RequestOutOfPolicy
Parameters.Description: The RequestOutOfPolicy parameter specifies users who are allowed to submit out-of-policy requests that require approval by a resource mailbox delegate. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the user.

Parameters.Name: ResourceDelegates
Parameters.Description: The ResourceDelegates parameter specifies users can approve or reject requests that are sent to the resource mailbox. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the user.

Parameters.Name: ScheduleOnlyDuringWorkHours
Parameters.Description: The ScheduleOnlyDuringWorkHours parameter specifies whether to allow meetings to be scheduled outside of the working hours that are defined for the resource mailbox.

Parameters.Name: TentativePendingApproval
Parameters.Description: The TentativePendingApproval parameter specifies whether to mark pending requests as tentative on the calendar.

Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Exchange Global Admin

Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the CAS Mailbox Plan that you want to modify.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: The display name of the CAS Mailbox Plan.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: CASMailboxPlans cannot be created/removed in O365.  This must be set to 'Present'

Parameters.Name: ActiveSyncEnabled
Parameters.Description: The ActiveSyncEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox by using Exchange Active Sync. Default is $true.

Parameters.Name: ImapEnabled
Parameters.Description: The ImapEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox by using IMAP4 clients. The default value is $true for all CAS mailbox plans except ExchangeOnlineDeskless which is $false by default.

Parameters.Name: OwaMailboxPolicy
Parameters.Description: The OwaMailboxPolicy parameter specifies the Outlook on the web (formerly known as Outlook Web App) mailbox policy for the mailbox plan. The default value is OwaMailboxPolicy-Default. You can use the Get-OwaMailboxPolicy cmdlet to view the available Outlook on the web mailbox policies.

Parameters.Name: PopEnabled
Parameters.Description: The PopEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox by using POP3 clients. Default is $true.

Description:
This resource configures Client Access services (CAS) mailbox plans
in cloud-based organizations.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the mailbox that you want to configure.

Parameters.Name: ActiveSyncAllowedDeviceIDs
Parameters.Description: TheActiveSyncAllowedDeviceIDs parameter specifies one or more Exchange ActiveSync device IDs that are allowed to synchronize with the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: ActiveSyncBlockedDeviceIDs
Parameters.Description: The ActiveSyncBlockedDeviceIDs parameter specifies one or more Exchange ActiveSync device IDs that aren't allowed to synchronize with the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: ActiveSyncDebugLogging
Parameters.Description: The ActiveSyncDebugLogging parameter enables or disables Exchange ActiveSync debug logging for the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: ActiveSyncEnabled
Parameters.Description: The ActiveSyncEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using Exchange ActiveSync.

Parameters.Name: ActiveSyncMailboxPolicy
Parameters.Description: The ActiveSyncMailboxPolicy parameter specifies the Exchange ActiveSync mailbox policy for the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: ActiveSyncSuppressReadReceipt
Parameters.Description: The ActiveSyncSuppressReadReceipt parameter controls the behavior of read receipts for Exchange ActiveSync clients that access the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: EwsAllowEntourage
Parameters.Description: The EwsAllowEntourage parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox by Microsoft Entourage clients that use Exchange Web Services.

Parameters.Name: EwsAllowList
Parameters.Description: The EwsAllowList parameter specifies the Exchange Web Services applications (user agent strings) that are allowed to access the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: EwsAllowMacOutlook
Parameters.Description: The EwsAllowMacOutlook parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox by Outlook for Mac clients that use Exchange Web Services.

Parameters.Name: EwsAllowOutlook
Parameters.Description: The EwsAllowOutlook parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox by Outlook clients that use Exchange Web Services.

Parameters.Name: EwsApplicationAccessPolicy
Parameters.Description: The EwsApplicationAccessPolicy parameter controls access to the mailbox using Exchange Web Services applications.

Parameters.Name: EwsBlockList
Parameters.Description: The EwsBlockList parameter specifies the Exchange Web Services applications (user agent strings) that aren't allowed to access the mailbox using Exchange Web Services.

Parameters.Name: EwsEnabled
Parameters.Description: The EwsEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using Exchange Web Services clients.

Parameters.Name: ImapEnabled
Parameters.Description: The ImapEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using IMAP4 clients.

Parameters.Name: ImapMessagesRetrievalMimeFormat
Parameters.Description: The ImapMessagesRetrievalMimeFormat parameter specifies the message format for IMAP4 clients that access the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: ImapForceICalForCalendarRetrievalOption
Parameters.Description: The ImapForceICalForCalendarRetrievalOption parameter specifies how meeting requests are presented to IMAP4 clients that access the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: ImapSuppressReadReceipt
Parameters.Description: The ImapSuppressReadReceipt parameter controls the behavior of read receipts for IMAP4 clients that access the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: ImapUseProtocolDefaults
Parameters.Description: The ImapUseProtocolDefaults parameter specifies whether to use the IMAP4 protocol defaults for the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: MacOutlookEnabled
Parameters.Description: The MacOutlookEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using Outlook for Mac clients that use Microsoft Sync technology.

Parameters.Name: MAPIEnabled
Parameters.Description: The MAPIEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using MAPI clients (for example, Outlook).

Parameters.Name: OneWinNativeOutlookEnabled
Parameters.Description: The OneWinNativeOutlookEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using the new Outlook for Windows.

Parameters.Name: OutlookMobileEnabled
Parameters.Description: The OutlookMobileEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using Outlook for iOS and Android.

Parameters.Name: OWAEnabled
Parameters.Description: The OWAEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using Outlook on the web (formerly known as Outlook Web App or OWA).

Parameters.Name: OWAforDevicesEnabled
Parameters.Description: The OWAforDevicesEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using the older Outlook Web App (OWA) app on iOS and Android devices.

Parameters.Name: OwaMailboxPolicy
Parameters.Description: The OwaMailboxPolicy parameter specifies the Outlook on the web mailbox policy for the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: PopEnabled
Parameters.Description: The PopEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using POP3 clients.

Parameters.Name: PopForceICalForCalendarRetrievalOption
Parameters.Description: The PopForceICalForCalendarRetrievalOption parameter specifies how meeting requests are presented to POP3 clients that access the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: PopMessagesRetrievalMimeFormat
Parameters.Description: The PopMessagesRetrievalMimeFormat parameter specifies the message format for POP3 clients that access the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: PopSuppressReadReceipt
Parameters.Description: The PopSuppressReadReceipt parameter controls the behavior of read receipts for POP3 clients that access the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: PopUseProtocolDefaults
Parameters.Description: The PopUseProtocolDefaults parameter specifies whether to use the POP3 protocol defaults for the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: PublicFolderClientAccess
Parameters.Description: The PublicFolderClientAccess parameter enables or disables access to public folders in Microsoft Outlook.

Parameters.Name: ShowGalAsDefaultView
Parameters.Description: The ShowGalAsDefaultView parameter specifies whether the global address list (GAL) is the default recipient picker for messages.

Parameters.Name: SmtpClientAuthenticationDisabled
Parameters.Description: The SmtpClientAuthenticationDisabled parameter specifies whether to disable authenticated SMTP (SMTP AUTH) for the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: UniversalOutlookEnabled
Parameters.Description: The UniversalOutlookEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using Windows 10 Mail and Calendar.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Present ensures the Mailbox CAS settings are applied.

Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Exchange Global Admin

Description: This resource configures CAS mailbox settings.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the client access rule that you want to modify.

Parameters.Name: Action
Parameters.Description: The Action parameter specifies the action for the client access rule. Valid values for this parameter are AllowAccess and DenyAccess.

Parameters.Name: AnyOfAuthenticationTypes
Parameters.Description: The AnyOfAuthenticationTypes parameter specifies a condition for the client access rule that is based on the client's authentication type. Valid values for this parameter are AdfsAuthentication, BasicAuthentication, CertificateBasedAuthentication, NonBasicAuthentication, OAuthAuthentication.

Parameters.Name: AnyOfClientIPAddressesOrRanges
Parameters.Description: The AnyOfClientIPAddressesOrRanges parameter specifies a condition for the client access rule that is based on the client's IP address. Valid values for this parameter are: A single IP address, an IP address range, a CIDR IP.

Parameters.Name: AnyOfProtocols
Parameters.Description: The AnyOfProtocols parameter specifies a condition for the client access rule that is based on the client's protocol. Valid values for this parameter are ExchangeActiveSync,ExchangeAdminCenter,ExchangeWebServices,IMAP4,OfflineAddressBook,OutlookAnywhere,OutlookWebApp,POP3,PowerShellWebServices,RemotePowerShell,REST,UniversalOutlook.

Parameters.Name: Enabled
Parameters.Description: The Enabled parameter specifies whether the client access rule is enabled or disabled. Default is $true.

Parameters.Name: ExceptAnyOfAuthenticationTypes
Parameters.Description: The ExceptAnyOfAuthenticationTypes parameter specifies an exception for the client access rule that is based on the client's authentication type. Valid values for this parameter are AdfsAuthentication, BasicAuthentication, CertificateBasedAuthentication, NonBasicAuthentication, OAuthAuthentication.

Parameters.Name: ExceptAnyOfClientIPAddressesOrRanges
Parameters.Description: The ExceptAnyOfClientIPAddressesOrRanges parameter specifies an exception for the client access rule that is based on the client's IP address. Valid values for this parameter are: A single IP address, an IP address range, a CIDR IP.

Parameters.Name: ExceptAnyOfProtocols
Parameters.Description: The ExceptAnyOfProtocols parameter specifies an exception for the client access rule that is based on the client's protocol. Valid values for this parameter are ExchangeActiveSync,ExchangeAdminCenter,ExchangeWebServices,IMAP4,OfflineAddressBook,OutlookAnywhere,OutlookWebApp,POP3,PowerShellWebServices,RemotePowerShell,REST,UniversalOutlook.

Parameters.Name: ExceptUsernameMatchesAnyOfPatterns
Parameters.Description: The ExceptUsernameMatchesAnyOfPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the client access rule that is based on the user's account name.

Parameters.Name: Priority
Parameters.Description: The Priority parameter specifies a priority value for the client access rule. A lower integer value indicates a higher priority, and a higher priority rule is evaluated before a lower priority rule. The default value is 1.

Parameters.Name: RuleScope
Parameters.Description: The RuleScope parameter specifies the scope of the client access rule. Valid values are All and Users

Parameters.Name: UserRecipientFilter
Parameters.Description: The UserRecipientFilter parameter specifies a condition for the client access rule that uses OPath filter syntax to identify the user.

Parameters.Name: UsernameMatchesAnyOfPatterns
Parameters.Description: The UsernameMatchesAnyOfPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the client access rule that is based on the user's account name.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specifies if this Client Access Rule should exist.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Exchange Global Admin

Parameters.Name: CertificatePath
Parameters.Description: Path to certificate used in service principal usually a PFX file.

Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Description:

The supported authentication types per protocol can be found here:
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/exchange/clients-and-mobile-in-exchange-online/client-access-rules/client-access-rules

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the distribution group or mail-enabled security group that you want to modify. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies a unique name for the address list.

Parameters.Name: Alias
Parameters.Description: Exchange alias (also known as the mail nickname) for the recipient

Parameters.Name: BccBlocked
Parameters.Description: Is Bcc blocked for the distribution group.

Parameters.Name: BypassNestedModerationEnabled
Parameters.Description: The ByPassNestedModerationEnabled parameter specifies how to handle message approval when a moderated group contains other moderated groups as members.

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: Description of the distribution group.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: The DisplayName parameter specifies the display name of the group. The display name is visible in the Exchange admin center and in address lists. The maximum length is 256 characters.

Parameters.Name: HiddenGroupMembershipEnabled
Parameters.Description: The HiddenGroupMembershipEnabled switch specifies whether to hide the members of the distribution group from members of the group and users who aren't members of the group.

Parameters.Name: ManagedBy
Parameters.Description: The ManagedBy parameter specifies an owner for the group. A group must have at least one owner.

Parameters.Name: MemberDepartRestriction
Parameters.Description: The MemberDepartRestriction parameter specifies the restrictions that you put on requests to leave the group. Valid values are: Open & Closed

Parameters.Name: MemberJoinRestriction
Parameters.Description: The MemberJoinRestriction parameter specifies the restrictions that you put on requests to join the group. Valid values are: Open, Closed & ApprovalRequired

Parameters.Name: Members
Parameters.Description: The Members parameter specifies the recipients (mail-enabled objects) that are members of the group. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

Parameters.Name: ModeratedBy
Parameters.Description: The ModeratedBy parameter specifies one or more moderators for this group. A moderator approves messages sent to the group before the messages are delivered. A moderator must be a mailbox, mail user, or mail contact in your organization. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the moderator.

Parameters.Name: ModerationEnabled
Parameters.Description: The ModerationEnabled parameter specifies whether moderation is enabled for this recipient.

Parameters.Name: Notes
Parameters.Description: The Notes parameters specifies additional information about the object.

Parameters.Name: OrganizationalUnit
Parameters.Description: The OrganizationalUnit parameter specifies the location in Active Directory where the group is created.

Parameters.Name: PrimarySmtpAddress
Parameters.Description: The PrimarySmtpAddress parameter specifies the primary return email address that's used for the recipient.

Parameters.Name: RequireSenderAuthenticationEnabled
Parameters.Description: The RequireSenderAuthenticationEnabled parameter specifies whether to accept messages only from authenticated (internal) senders.

Parameters.Name: RoomList
Parameters.Description: The RoomList switch specifies that all members of this distribution group are room mailboxes. You don't need to specify a value with this switch.

Parameters.Name: AcceptMessagesOnlyFrom
Parameters.Description: The AcceptMessagesOnlyFrom parameter specifies who is allowed to send messages to this recipient. Messages from other senders are rejected.

Parameters.Name: AcceptMessagesOnlyFromDLMembers
Parameters.Description: The AcceptMessagesOnlyFromDLMembers parameter specifies who is allowed to send messages to this recipient. Messages from other senders are rejected.

Parameters.Name: AcceptMessagesOnlyFromSendersOrMembers
Parameters.Description: The AcceptMessagesOnlyFromSendersOrMembers parameter specifies who is allowed to send messages to this recipient. Messages from other senders are rejected.

Parameters.Name: EmailAddresses
Parameters.Description: The EmailAddresses parameter specifies all email addresses (proxy addresses) for the recipient, including the primary SMTP address. In on-premises Exchange organizations, the primary SMTP address and other proxy addresses are typically set by email address policies. However, you can use this parameter to configure other proxy addresses for the recipient.

Parameters.Name: GrantSendOnBehalfTo
Parameters.Description: The GrantSendOnBehalfTo parameter specifies who can send on behalf of this group. Although messages send on behalf of the group clearly show the sender in the From field (<Sender> on behalf of <Group>), replies to these messages are delivered to the group, not the sender.

Parameters.Name: HiddenFromAddressListsEnabled
Parameters.Description: The HiddenFromAddressListsEnabled parameter specifies whether this recipient is visible in address lists.

Parameters.Name: SendOofMessageToOriginatorEnabled
Parameters.Description: The SendOofMessageToOriginatorEnabled parameter specifies how to handle out of office (OOF) messages for members of the group.

Parameters.Name: SendModerationNotifications
Parameters.Description: The SendModerationNotifications parameter specifies when moderation notification messages are sent. Valid values are: Always, Internal, Never.

Parameters.Name: Type
Parameters.Description: The Type parameter specifies the type of group that you want to create. Valid values are: Distribution, Security

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the DKIM signing policy that you want to modify.  This should be the FQDN.

Parameters.Name: AdminDisplayName
Parameters.Description: The AdminDisplayName parameter specifies a description for the policy.

Parameters.Name: BodyCanonicalization
Parameters.Description: The BodyCanonicalization parameter specifies the canonicalization algorithm that's used to create and verify the message body part of the DKIM signature. This value effectively controls the sensitivity of DKIM to changes to the message body in transit. Valid values are 'Simple' or 'Relaxed'.  'Relaxed' is the default.

Parameters.Name: HeaderCanonicalization
Parameters.Description: The HeaderCanonicalization parameter specifies the canonicalization algorithm that's used to create and verify the message header part of the DKIM signature. This value effectively controls the sensitivity of DKIM to changes to the message headers in transit. Valid values are 'Simple' or 'Relaxed'.  'Relaxed' is the default.

Parameters.Name: KeySize
Parameters.Description: The KeySize parameter specifies the size in bits of the public key that's used in the DKIM signing policy. Valid values are 1024 and 2048

Parameters.Name: Enabled
Parameters.Description: The Enabled parameter specifies whether the DKIM Signing Configuration is enabled or disabled. Default is $true.

Parameters.Description: Specifies if this Client Access Rule should exist.

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the email address policy. The maximum length is 64 characters.

Parameters.Name: Priority
Parameters.Description: The Priority parameter specifies the order that the email address policies are evaluated. By default, every time that you add a new email address policy, the policy is assigned a priority of N+1, where N is the number of email address policies that you've created.

Parameters.Name: EnabledEmailAddressTemplates
Parameters.Description: The EnabledEmailAddressTemplates parameter specifies the rules in the email address policy that are used to generate email addresses for recipients.

Parameters.Name: EnabledPrimarySMTPAddressTemplate
Parameters.Description: The EnabledPrimarySMTPAddressTemplate parameter specifies the specifies the rule in the email address policy that's used to generate the primary SMTP email addresses for recipients. You can use this parameter instead of the EnabledEmailAddressTemplates if the policy only applies the primary email address and no additional proxy addresses.

Parameters.Name: ManagedByFilter
Parameters.Description: The ManagedByFilter parameter specifies the email address policies to apply to Office 365 groups based on the properties of the users who create the Office 365 groups.

Parameters.Name: Comments
Parameters.Description: The Comments parameter specifies informative comments for the rule, such as what the rule is used for or how it has changed over time. The length of the comment can't exceed 1024 characters.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients with email addresses in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSentTo
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSentTo parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSentToMemberOf
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSentToMemberOf parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages sent to members of groups. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the rule that you want to view. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the rule.

Parameters.Name: State
Parameters.Description: This parameter define if the rule is enabled or disabled

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies a unique name for the rule. The maximum length is 64 characters.

Parameters.Name: Priority
Parameters.Description: The Priority parameter specifies a priority value for the rule that determines the order of rule processing. A lower integer value indicates a higher priority, the value 0 is the highest priority, and rules can't have the same priority value.

Parameters.Name: RecipientDomainIs
Parameters.Description: The RecipientDomainIs parameter specifies a condition that looks for recipients with email addresses in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: SentTo
Parameters.Description: The SentTo parameter specifies a condition that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

Parameters.Name: SentToMemberOf
Parameters.Description: The SentToMemberOf parameter specifies a condition that looks for messages sent to members of distribution groups, dynamic distribution groups, or mail-enabled security groups. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

Parameters.Name: Enabled
Parameters.Description: The Enabled parameter enables or disables external sender identification in supported versions of Outlook. Valid values are:$true: External sender identification in Outlook is enabled. An External icon is added in the area of the subject line of messages from external senders. To exempt specific senders or sender domains from this identification, use the AllowList parameter.$false: External sender identification in Outlook is disabled.

Parameters.Name: AllowList
Parameters.Description: The AllowList parameter specifies exceptions to external sender identification in supported versions of Outlook. Messages received from the specified senders or senders in the specified domains don't receive native External sender identification. The allow list uses the 5322.From address (also known as the From address or P2 sender). Valid values are an individual domain (contoso.com), a domain and all subdomains (*.contoso.com) or email addresses (admin@contoso.com).

Parameters.Name: DataEncryptionPolicy
Parameters.Description: The DataEncryptionPolicy parameter specifies the data encryption policy that's applied to the Microsoft 365 Group.

Parameters.Name: EmailAddresses
Parameters.Description: The EmailAddresses parameter specifies all the email addresses (proxy addresses) for the recipient, including the primary SMTP address.

Parameters.Name: ExtensionCustomAttribute1
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies a value for the ExtensionCustomAttribute1 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. You can specify up to 1300 values separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ExtensionCustomAttribute2
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies a value for the ExtensionCustomAttribute2 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. You can specify up to 1300 values separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ExtensionCustomAttribute3
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies a value for the ExtensionCustomAttribute3 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. You can specify up to 1300 values separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ExtensionCustomAttribute4
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies a value for the ExtensionCustomAttribute4 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. You can specify up to 1300 values separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ExtensionCustomAttribute5
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies a value for the ExtensionCustomAttribute5 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. You can specify up to 1300 values separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: GrantSendOnBehalfTo
Parameters.Description: The GrantSendOnBehalfTo parameter specifies who can send on behalf of this Microsoft 365 Group.

Parameters.Name: HiddenFromAddressListsEnabled
Parameters.Description: The GrantSendOnBehalfTo parameter specifies who can send on behalf of this Microsoft 365 Group.

Parameters.Name: HiddenFromExchangeClientsEnabled
Parameters.Description: The HiddenFromExchangeClientsEnabled switch specifies whether the Microsoft 365 Group is hidden from Outlook clients connected to Microsoft 365.

Parameters.Name: InformationBarrierMode
Parameters.Description: The InformationBarrierMode parameter specifies the information barrier mode for the Microsoft 365 Group.

Parameters.Name: IsMemberAllowedToEditContent
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies whether or not members are allow to edit content.

Parameters.Name: Language
Parameters.Description: The Language parameter specifies language preference for the Microsoft 365 Group.

Parameters.Name: MailboxRegion
Parameters.Description: The MailboxRegion parameter specifies the preferred data location (PDL) for the Microsoft 365 Group in multi-geo environments.

Parameters.Name: MailTip
Parameters.Description: The MailTip parameter specifies the custom MailTip text for this recipient. The MailTip is shown to senders when they start drafting an email message to this recipient.

Parameters.Name: MailTipTranslations
Parameters.Description: The MailTipTranslations parameter specifies additional languages for the custom MailTip text that's defined by the MailTip parameter.

Parameters.Name: MaxReceiveSize
Parameters.Description: The MaxReceiveSize parameter specifies the maximum size of an email message that can be sent to this group. Messages that exceed the maximum size are rejected by the group.

Parameters.Name: MaxSendSize
Parameters.Description: The MaxSendSize parameter specifies the maximum size of an email message that can be sent by this group.

Parameters.Name: ModeratedBy
Parameters.Description: The ModeratedBy parameter specifies one or more moderators for this recipient. A moderator approves messages sent to the recipient before the messages are delivered. A moderator must be a mailbox, mail user, or mail contact in your organization. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the moderator.

Parameters.Name: ModerationEnabled
Parameters.Description: The ModerationEnabled parameter specifies whether moderation is enabled for this recipient.

Parameters.Name: Notes
Parameters.Description: The Notes parameter specifies the description of the Microsoft 365 Group. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Parameters.Name: PrimarySmtpAddress
Parameters.Description: The PrimarySmtpAddress parameter specifies the primary return email address that's used for the recipient. You can't use the EmailAddresses and PrimarySmtpAddress parameters in the same command.

Parameters.Name: RejectMessagesFromSendersOrMembers
Parameters.Description: The RejectMessagesFromSendersOrMembers parameter specifies who isn't allowed to send messages to this recipient. Messages from these senders are rejected.

Parameters.Name: RequireSenderAuthenticationEnabled
Parameters.Description: The RequireSenderAuthenticationEnabled parameter specifies whether to accept messages only from authenticated (internal) senders.

Parameters.Name: SensitivityLabelId
Parameters.Description: The SensitivityLabelId parameter specifies the GUID value of the sensitivity label that's assigned to the Microsoft 365 Group.

Parameters.Name: SubscriptionEnabled
Parameters.Description: The SubscriptionEnabled switch specifies whether the group owners can enable subscription to conversations and calendar events on the groups they own.

Parameters.Name: UnifiedGroupWelcomeMessageEnabled
Parameters.Description: The UnifiedGroupWelcomeMessageEnabled switch specifies whether to enable or disable sending system-generated welcome messages to users who are added as members to the Microsoft 365 Group.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Exchange Admin
