Description: Use the New-RetentionPolicy cmdlet to create a retention policy and the Set-RetentionPolicy cmdlet to change the properties of an existing retention policy.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the name of the tag.

Parameters.Name: Comment
Parameters.Description: The Description parameter specifies a comment for the tag.

Parameters.Name: AgeLimitForRetention
Parameters.Description: The AgeLimitForRetention parameter specifies the age at which retention is enforced on an item. The age limit corresponds to the number of days from the date the item was delivered, or the date an item was created if it wasn't delivered. If this parameter isn't present and the RetentionEnabled parameter is set to $true, an error is returned.

Parameters.Name: MessageClass
Parameters.Description: The MessageClass parameter specifies the message type to which the tag applies. If not specified, the default value is set to *.

Parameters.Name: MustDisplayCommentEnabled
Parameters.Description: The MustDisplayCommentEnabled parameter specifies whether the comment can be hidden. The default value is $true.

Parameters.Name: RetentionAction
Parameters.Description: The RetentionAction parameter specifies the action for the retention policy.

Parameters.Name: RetentionEnabled
Parameters.Description: The RetentionEnabled parameter specifies whether the tag is enabled. When set to $false, the tag is disabled, and no retention action is taken on messages that have the tag applied.

Parameters.Name: Type
Parameters.Description: The Type parameter specifies the type of retention tag being created.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Present ensures the instance exists, absent ensures it is removed.

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: The Description parameter specifies the description that's displayed when the role group is viewed using the Get-RoleGroup cmdlet. Enclose the description in quotation marks

Parameters.Name: Members
Parameters.Description: The Members parameter specifies the mailboxes or mail-enabled USGs to add as a member of the role group. You can identify the user or group by the name, DN, or primary SMTP address value. You can specify multiple members separated by commas (Value1,Value2,...ValueN). If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks

Parameters.Name: Roles
Parameters.Description: The Roles parameter specifies the management roles to assign to the role group when it's created. If a role name contains spaces, enclose the name in quotation marks. If you want to assign more that one role, separate the role names with commas.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the name of the SafeAttachmentpolicy that you want to modify.

Parameters.Name: Action
Parameters.Description: The Action parameter specifies the action for the Safe Attachments policy.

Parameters.Name: ActionOnError
Parameters.Description: The ActionOnError parameter specifies the error handling option for Safe Attachments scanning (what to do if scanning times out or an error occurs). Valid values are: $true: The action specified by the Action parameter is applied to messages even when the attachments aren't successfully scanned. $false: The action specified by the Action parameter isn't applied to messages when the attachments aren't successfully scanned. This is the default value.

Parameters.Name: AdminDisplayName

Description:
This resource configures the settings of the Safe Attachments policies
in your cloud-based organization.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the name of the SafeAttachment rule that you want to modify.

Parameters.Name: SafeAttachmentPolicy
Parameters.Description: The SafeAttachmentPolicy parameter specifies the name of the SafeAttachment policy that's associated with the SafeAttachment rule.

Parameters.Name: Enabled
Parameters.Description: Specify if this rule should be enabled. Default is $true.

Parameters.Name: Priority
Parameters.Description: The Priority parameter specifies a priority value for the rule that determines the order of rule processing. A lower integer value indicates a higher priority, the value 0 is the highest priority, and rules can't have the same priority value.

Parameters.Name: Comments
Parameters.Description: The Comments parameter specifies informative comments for the rule, such as what the rule is used for or how it has changed over time. The length of the comment can't exceed 1024 characters.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSentTo
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSentTo parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSentToMemberOf
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSentToMemberOf parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages sent to members of groups. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

Parameters.Name: RecipientDomainIs
Parameters.Description: The RecipientDomainIs parameter specifies a condition that looks for recipients with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: SentTo
Parameters.Description: The SentTo parameter specifies a condition that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

Parameters.Name: SentToMemberOf
Parameters.Description: The SentToMemberOf parameter looks for messages sent to members of groups. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if this rule should exist or not.

Description: This resource configures an Safe Attachment Rule in Exchange Online.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the SafeLinks policy that you want to modify.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if this policy should exist or not.

Parameters.Name: AdminDisplayName
Parameters.Description: The AdminDisplayName parameter specifies a description for the policy.

Parameters.Name: AllowClickThrough
Parameters.Description: The AllowClickThrough parameter specifies whether to allow users to click through to the original URL on warning pages.

Parameters.Name: CustomNotificationText
Parameters.Description: The custom notification text specifies the customized notification text to show to users.

Parameters.Name: DeliverMessageAfterScan
Parameters.Description: The DeliverMessageAfterScan parameter specifies whether to deliver email messages only after Safe Links scanning is complete. Valid values are: $true: Wait until Safe Links scanning is complete before delivering the message. $false: If Safe Links scanning can't complete, deliver the message anyway. This is the default value.

Parameters.Name: DoNotRewriteUrls
Parameters.Description: The DoNotRewriteUrls parameter specifies a URL that's skipped by Safe Links scanning. You can specify multiple values separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: EnableForInternalSenders
Parameters.Description: The EnableForInternalSenders parameter specifies whether the Safe Links policy is applied to messages sent between internal senders and internal recipients within the same Exchange Online organization.

Parameters.Name: EnableOrganizationBranding
Parameters.Description: The EnableOrganizationBranding parameter specifies whether your organization's logo is displayed on Safe Links warning and notification pages.

Parameters.Name: EnableSafeLinksForOffice
Parameters.Description: The EnableSafeLinksForOffice parameter specifies whether to enable Safe Links protection for supported Office desktop, mobile, or web apps.

Parameters.Name: EnableSafeLinksForTeams
Parameters.Description: The EnableSafeLinksForTeams parameter specifies whether Safe Links is enabled for Microsoft Teams. Valid values are: $true: Safe Links is enabled for Teams. If a protected user clicks a malicious link in a Teams conversation, group chat, or from channels, a warning page will appear in the default web browser. $false: Safe Links isn't enabled for Teams. This is the default value.

Parameters.Name: EnableSafeLinksForEmail
Parameters.Description: The EnableSafeLinksForEmail parameter specifies whether to enable Safe Links protection for email messages. Valid values are: $true: Safe Links is enabled for email. When a user clicks a link in an email, the link is checked by Safe Links. If the link is found to be malicious, a warning page appears in the default web browser. $false: Safe Links isn't enabled for email. This is the default value.

Parameters.Name: DisableUrlRewrite
Parameters.Description: The DisableUrlRewrite parameter specifies whether to rewrite (wrap) URLs in email messages. Valid values are: $true: URLs in messages are not rewritten, but messages are still scanned by Safe Links prior to delivery. Time of click checks on links are done using the Safe Links API in supported Outlook clients (currently, Outlook for Windows and Outlook for Mac). Typically, we don't recommend using this value. $false: URLs in messages are rewritten. API checks still occur on unwrapped URLs in supported clients if the user is in a valid Safe Links policy. This is the default value.

Parameters.Name: ScanUrls
Parameters.Description: The ScanUrls parameter specifies whether to enable or disable the scanning of links in email messages. Valid values are: $true: Scanning links in email messages is enabled. $false: Scanning links in email messages is disabled. This is the default value.

Parameters.Name: TrackClicks
Parameters.Description: The TrackClicks parameter specifies whether to track user clicks related to Safe Links protection of links.

Parameters.Name: UseTranslatedNotificationText
Parameters.Description: The UseTranslatedNotificationText specifies whether to use Microsoft Translator to automatically localize the custom notification text that you specified with the CustomNotificationText parameter.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Exchange Global Admin

Description:
Use the ServicePrincipal cmdlets to create, change service principals in your cloud-based organization.

## Parameters

- Identity: The Identity parameter specifies the service principal that you want to modify. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the service principal. For example: Name, Distinguished name (DN), GUID, AppId, ObjectId
- AppName: The AppName parameter specifies the corresponding friendly name of the unique AppId GUID value for the service principal.
- DisplayName: The DisplayName parameter specifies the friendly name of the service principal. If the name contains spaces, enclose the name in quotation marks (").
- AppId: The AppId parameter specifies the unique AppId GUID value for the service principal.
- ObjectId: The ObjectId parameter specifies the unique ObjectId GUID value for the service principal.

## Examples

- Set-ServicePrincipal -Identity dc873ad4-0397-4d74-b5c0-897cd3a94731 -DisplayName "Another App Name"
- New-ServicePrincipal -AppId 71487acd-ec93-476d-bd0e-6c8b31831053 -ObjectId 6233fba6-0198-4277-892f-9275bf728bcc

## Parameters present in New and not in Set

- AppId
- ObjectId

## Parameters present in Set and not in New

- Identity

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies the name of the Sweep rule. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Parameters.Name: Provider
Parameters.Description: The Provider parameter specifies the provider for the Sweep rule. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks. For Sweep rules that you create in Outlook on the web, the default value is Exchange16.

Parameters.Name: DestinationFolder
Parameters.Description: The DestinationFolder parameter specifies an action for the Sweep rule that moves messages to the specified folder.

Parameters.Name: Enabled
Parameters.Description: The Enabled parameter specifies whether the Sweep rule is enabled or disabled.

Parameters.Name: KeepForDays
Parameters.Description: The KeepForDays parameter specifies an action for the Sweep rule that specifies the number of days to keep messages that match the conditions of the rule. After the number of days have passed, the messages are moved to the location that's specified by the DestinationFolder parameter (by default, the Deleted Items folder). You can't use this parameter with the KeepLatest parameter and the Sweep rule must contain a KeepForDays or KeepLatest parameter value.

Parameters.Name: KeepLatest
Parameters.Description: The KeepLatest parameter specifies an action for the Sweep rule that specifies the number of messages to keep that match the conditions of the rule. After the number of messages is exceeded, the oldest messages are moved to the location that's specified by the DestinationFolder parameter (by default, the Deleted Items folder). You can't use this parameter with the KeepForDays parameter and the Sweep rule must contain a KeepForDays or KeepLatest parameter value.

Parameters.Name: Mailbox
Parameters.Description: The Mailbox parameter specifies the mailbox where you want to create the Sweep rule. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the mailbox.

Parameters.Name: SenderName
Parameters.Description: The SenderName parameter specifies a condition for the Sweep rule that looks for the specified sender in messages. For internal senders, you can use any value that uniquely identifies the sender.

Parameters.Name: SourceFolder
Parameters.Description: The SourceFolder parameter specifies a condition for the Sweep rule that looks for messages in the specified folder.

Parameters.Name: SystemCategory
Parameters.Description: The SystemCategory parameter specifies a condition for the sweep rule that looks for messages with the specified system category. System categories are available to all mailboxes in the organization.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if this rule should exist or not.

Parameters.Description: The AddressBookPolicyRoutingEnabled parameter controls how recipients are resolved in an organization that uses address book policies to create separate virtual organizations within the same Exchange organization.

Parameters.Name: AllowLegacyTLSClients
Parameters.Description: Allow legacy TLS clients

Parameters.Name: ClearCategories
Parameters.Description: The ClearCategories parameter keeps or removes Microsoft Outlook message categories during content conversion.

Parameters.Name: ConvertDisclaimerWrapperToEml
Parameters.Description: The ConvertDisclaimerWrapperToEml parameter specifies whether the original message will be added as a TNEF attachment or a regular EML attachment to a disclaimer.

Parameters.Name: DSNConversionMode
Parameters.Description: The DSNConversionMode parameter controls how Exchange handles delivery status notifications that are generated by earlier versions of Exchange or other messaging systems.

Parameters.Name: ExternalDelayDsnEnabled
Parameters.Description: The ExternalDelayDsnEnabled parameter specifies whether a delay delivery status notification (DSN) message should be created for external messages that couldn't be immediately delivered.

Parameters.Name: ExternalDsnDefaultLanguage
Parameters.Description: The ExternalDsnDefaultLanguage parameter specifies which Exchange server language should be used by default when you create external DSN messages.

Parameters.Name: ExternalDsnLanguageDetectionEnabled
Parameters.Description: The ExternalDsnLanguageDetectionEnabled parameter specifies whether the server should try to send an external DSN message in the same language as the original message that generated the notification.

Parameters.Name: ExternalDsnReportingAuthority
Parameters.Description: The ExternalDsnReportingAuthority parameter specifies the domain in the machine-readable part of external DSN messages.

Parameters.Name: ExternalDsnSendHtml
Parameters.Description: The ExternalDsnSendHtml parameter specifies whether external DSN messages should be HTML or plain text.

Parameters.Name: ExternalPostmasterAddress
Parameters.Description: The ExternalPostmasterAddress parameter specifies the email address in the From header field of an external DSN message.

Parameters.Name: HeaderPromotionModeSetting
Parameters.Description: The HeaderPromotionModeSetting parameter specifies whether named properties are created for custom X-headers on messages received.

Parameters.Name: InternalDelayDsnEnabled
Parameters.Description: The InternalDelayDsnEnabled parameter specifies whether a delay DSN message should be created for messages sent to or from recipients or senders in the same Exchange organization that couldn't be immediately delivered.

Parameters.Name: InternalDsnDefaultLanguage
Parameters.Description: The InternalDsnDefaultLanguage parameter specifies which Exchange server language should be used by default when you create internal DSN messages.

Parameters.Name: InternalDsnLanguageDetectionEnabled
Parameters.Description: The InternalDsnLanguageDetectionEnabled parameter specifies whether the server should try to send an internal DSN message in the same language as the original message that generated the notification.

Parameters.Name: InternalDsnReportingAuthority
Parameters.Description: The InternalDsnReportingAuthority parameter specifies the domain in the machine-readable part of internal DSN messages.

Parameters.Name: InternalDsnSendHtml
Parameters.Description: The InternalDsnSendHtml parameter specifies whether internal DSN messages should be HTML or plain text.

Parameters.Name: JournalMessageExpirationDays
Parameters.Description: The JournalMessageExpirationDays parameter extends the number of days that undeliverable journal reports are queued before they expire.

Parameters.Name: JournalingReportNdrTo
Parameters.Description: The JournalingReportNdrTo parameter specifies the email address to which journal reports are sent if the journaling mailbox is unavailable. Once set, it cannot be reset to blank again.

Parameters.Name: MaxRecipientEnvelopeLimit
Parameters.Description: The MaxRecipientEnvelopeLimit parameter specifies the maximum number of recipients in a message.

Parameters.Name: ReplyAllStormBlockDurationHours
Parameters.Description: Reply all storm block duration hours.

Parameters.Name: ReplyAllStormDetectionMinimumRecipients
Parameters.Description: Reply all storm detection minimum recipients.

Parameters.Name: ReplyAllStormDetectionMinimumReplies
Parameters.Description: Reply all storm detection minimum replies.

Parameters.Name: ReplyAllStormProtectionEnabled
Parameters.Description: Reply all storm protection enabled.

Parameters.Name: Rfc2231EncodingEnabled
Parameters.Description: The Rfc2231EncodingEnabled parameter specifies whether the RFC 2231 encoding of MIME parameters for outbound messages is enabled in your organization.

Parameters.Name: SmtpClientAuthenticationDisabled
Parameters.Description: The SmtpClientAuthenticationDisabled parameter specifies whether to disable authenticated SMTP (SMTP AUTH) for the whole organization.

Description: This resource configures an Intune Account Protection Local Administrator Password Solution Policy.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: Identity of the account protection local administrator password solution policy.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: Display name of the account protection local administrator password solution policy.

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: Description of the account protection local administrator password solution policy.

Parameters.Name: RoleScopeTagIds
Parameters.Description: List of Scope Tags for this policy.

Parameters.Name: Assignments
Parameters.Description: Assignments of the account protection local administrator password solution policy.

Parameters.Name: BackupDirectory
Parameters.Description: Configures which directory the local admin account password is backed up to. 0 - Disabled, 1 - Azure AD, 2 - AD

Parameters.Name: passwordagedays_aad
Parameters.Description: Configures the maximum password age of the managed local administrator account for Azure AD. Minimum - 7, Maximum - 365

Parameters.Name: PasswordAgeDays
Parameters.Description: Configures the maximum password age of the managed local administrator account for Active Directory. Minimum - 1, Maximum - 365

Parameters.Name: PasswordExpirationProtectionEnabled
Parameters.Description: Configures additional enforcement of maximum password age for the managed local administrator account.

Parameters.Name: AdEncryptedPasswordHistorySize
Parameters.Description: Configures how many previous encrypted passwords will be remembered in Active Directory. Minimum - 0, Maximum - 12

Parameters.Name: AdPasswordEncryptionEnabled
Parameters.Description: Configures whether the password is encrypted before being stored in Active Directory.

Parameters.Name: AdPasswordEncryptionPrincipal
Parameters.Description: Configures the name or SID of a user or group that can decrypt the password stored in Active Directory.

Parameters.Name: AdministratorAccountName
Parameters.Description: Configures the name of the managed local administrator account.

Parameters.Name: PasswordComplexity
Parameters.Description: Configures the password complexity of the managed local administrator account. (1: Large letters, 2: Large letters + small letters, 3: Large letters + small letters + numbers, 4: Large letters + small letters + numbers + special characters, 5: Large letters + small letters + numbers + special characters (improved readability), 6: Passphrase (long words), 7: Passphrase (short words), 8: Passphrase (short words with unique prefixes))

Parameters.Name: PassphraseLength
Parameters.Description: Passphrase Length (Minimum 3, Maximum 10 characters) - Depends on PasswordComplexity

Parameters.Name: PasswordLength
Parameters.Description: Configures the length of the password of the managed local administrator account. Minimum - 8, Maximum - 64

Parameters.Name: PostAuthenticationActions
Parameters.Description: Specifies the actions to take upon expiration of the configured grace period. (1: Reset password, 3: Reset the password and logoff the managed account, 5: Reset the password and reboot, 11: Reset the password, logoff the managed account, and terminate any remaining processes.)

Parameters.Name: PostAuthenticationResetDelay
Parameters.Description: Specifies the amount of time (in hours) to wait after an authentication before executing the specified post-authentication actions. Minimum - 0, Maximum - 24

Parameters.Name: AutomaticAccountManagementEnabled
Parameters.Description: Automatic Account Management Enabled (false: The target account will not be automatically managed, true: The target account will be automatically managed)

Parameters.Name: AutomaticAccountManagementTarget
Parameters.Description: Automatic Account Management Target - Depends on AutomaticAccountManagementEnabled (0: Manage the built-in administrator account, 1: Manage a new custom administrator account)

Parameters.Name: AutomaticAccountManagementRandomizeName
Parameters.Description: Automatic Account Management Randomize Name - Depends on AutomaticAccountManagementEnabled (false: The name of the target account will not use a random numeric suffix., true: The name of the target account will use a random numeric suffix.)

Parameters.Name: AutomaticAccountManagementNameOrPrefix
Parameters.Description: Automatic Account Management Name Or Prefix - Depends on AutomaticAccountManagementEnabled

Parameters.Name: AutomaticAccountManagementEnableAccount
Parameters.Description: Automatic Account Management Enable Account - Depends on AutomaticAccountManagementEnabled (false: The target account will be disabled, true: The target account will be enabled)

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Present ensures the policy exists, absent ensures it is removed

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Intune Admin

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Name of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication. Format contoso.onmicrosoft.com

Parameters.Name: ApplicationSecret
Parameters.Description: Secret of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity
Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Description: This resource configures a Intune Account Protection policy.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: Identity of the account protection policy.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: Display name of the account protection rules policy.

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: Description of the account protection rules policy.

Parameters.Name: RoleScopeTagIds
Parameters.Description: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

Parameters.Name: Assignments
Parameters.Description: Assignments of the Intune Policy.

Parameters.Name: WindowsHelloForBusinessBlocked
Parameters.Description: Block Windows Hello for Business.

Parameters.Name: PinMinimumLength
Parameters.Description: Minimum PIN length must be between 4 and 127. (4-127)

Parameters.Name: PinMaximumLength
Parameters.Description: Maximum PIN length must be between 4 and 127. (4-127)

Parameters.Name: PinLowercaseCharactersUsage
Parameters.Description: If required, user PIN must include at least one lowercase letter.

Parameters.Name: PinUppercaseCharactersUsage
Parameters.Description: If required, user PIN must include at least one uppercase letter.

Parameters.Name: PinSpecialCharactersUsage
Parameters.Description: If required, user PIN must include at least one special character.

Parameters.Name: PinExpirationInDays
Parameters.Description: If configured, the user will be forced to change their PIN after the set number of days. (0, 730), 0 = Never

Parameters.Name: PinPreviousBlockCount
Parameters.Description: If configured, the user will not be able to reuse this number of previous PINs. (0, 50), 0 = Do not remember.

Parameters.Name: PinRecoveryEnabled
Parameters.Description: If enabled, the PIN recovery secret will be stored on the device and the user can change their PIN if needed. If disabled or not configured, the recovery secret will not be created or stored.

Parameters.Name: SecurityDeviceRequired
Parameters.Description: If you enable this policy setting, only devices with a usable TPM provision Windows Hello for Business. If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the TPM is still preferred, but all devices provision Windows Hello for Business.

Parameters.Name: UnlockWithBiometricsEnabled
Parameters.Description: If allowed, Windows Hello for Business can authenticate using gestures, such as face and fingerprint. Users must still configure a PIN in case of failure.

Parameters.Name: EnhancedAntiSpoofingForFacialFeaturesEnabled
Parameters.Description: If enabled, devices will use enhanced anti-spoofing, when available. If not configured, the client configuration for anti-spoofing will be honored.

Parameters.Name: UseCertificatesForOnPremisesAuthEnabled
Parameters.Description: If configured, Windows Hello for Business can use certificates to authenticate to on-premise resources.

Parameters.Name: UseSecurityKeyForSignin
Parameters.Description: Enable Windows Hello security key as a logon credential for all PCs in the tenant.

Parameters.Name: DeviceGuardLocalSystemAuthorityCredentialGuardSettings
Parameters.Description: Setting this Disable will disable the use of Credential Guard, which is the Windows default. Setting this to Enable with UEFI lock will enable Credential Guard and not allow it to be disabled remotely, as the UEFI persisted configuration must be manually cleared. Setting this to Enable without UEFI lock will enable Credential Guard and allow it to be turned off without physical access to the machine.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Present ensures the site collection exists, absent ensures it is removed

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Intune Admin

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Name of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication. Format contoso.onmicrosoft.com

Parameters.Name: ApplicationSecret
Parameters.Description: Secret of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity
Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Description: Intune Antivirus Policy for macOS

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: Display name of the endpoint protection policy for Windows 10.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: Identity of the endpoint protection policy for Windows 10.

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: Description of the endpoint protection policy for Windows 10.

Parameters.Name: RoleScopeTagIds
Parameters.Description: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

Parameters.Name: TamperProtection
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows scanning of archives. (0: enable feature. 1: disable feature)

Parameters.Name: DisableAccountProtectionUI
Parameters.Description: Use this policy setting to specify if to display the Account protection area in Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: DisableAppBrowserUI
Parameters.Description: Use this policy setting if you want to disable the display of the app and browser protection area in Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: DisableClearTpmButton
Parameters.Description: Disable the Clear TPM button in Windows Security. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: DisableDeviceSecurityUI
Parameters.Description: Use this policy setting if you want to disable the display of the Device security area in the Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: DisableFamilyUI
Parameters.Description: Use this policy setting if you want to disable the display of the family options area in Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: DisableHealthUI
Parameters.Description: Use this policy setting if you want to disable the display of the device performance and health area in Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: DisableNetworkUI
Parameters.Description: Use this policy setting if you want to disable the display of the firewall and network protection area in Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: DisableEnhancedNotifications
Parameters.Description: Use this policy setting if you want to disable the display of Windows Defender Security Center notifications. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: DisableTpmFirmwareUpdateWarning
Parameters.Description: Hide the recommendation to update TPM Firmware when a vulnerable firmware is detected. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: DisableVirusUI
Parameters.Description: Use this policy setting if you want to disable the display of the virus and threat protection area in Windows Defender Security Center.  (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: HideRansomwareDataRecovery
Parameters.Description: Use this policy setting to hide the Ransomware data recovery area in Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: HideWindowsSecurityNotificationAreaControl
Parameters.Description: This policy setting hides the Windows Security notification area control. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: EnableCustomizedToasts
Parameters.Description: Enable this policy to display your company name and contact options in the notifications. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: EnableInAppCustomization
Parameters.Description: Enable this policy to have your company name and contact options displayed in a contact card fly out in Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: CompanyName
Parameters.Description: The company name that is displayed to the users. CompanyName is required for both EnableCustomizedToasts and EnableInAppCustomization.

Parameters.Name: Email
Parameters.Description: The email address that is displayed to users. The default mail application is used to initiate email actions.

Parameters.Name: Phone
Parameters.Description: The phone number or Skype ID that is displayed to users. Skype is used to initiate the call.

Parameters.Name: URL
Parameters.Description: The help portal URL that is displayed to users. The default browser is used to initiate this action.

Parameters.Name: AllowArchiveScanning
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows scanning of archives. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: AllowBehaviorMonitoring
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Behavior Monitoring functionality. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: AllowCloudProtection
Parameters.Description: To best protect your PC, Windows Defender will send information to Microsoft about any problems it finds. Microsoft will analyze that information, learn more about problems affecting you and other customers, and offer improved solutions. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: AllowDatagramProcessingOnWinServer
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Network Protection to enable datagram processing on Windows Server. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: AllowEmailScanning
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows scanning of email. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: AllowFullScanOnMappedNetworkDrives
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows a full scan of mapped network drives. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: AllowFullScanRemovableDriveScanning
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows a full scan of removable drives. During a quick scan, removable drives may still be scanned. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: AllowIntrusionPreventionSystem
Parameters.Description: https://github.com/MicrosoftDocs/memdocs/issues/2250 (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: AllowIOAVProtection
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender IOAVP Protection functionality. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: AllowNetworkProtectionDownLevel
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Network Protection to be configured into block or audit mode on windows downlevel of RS3. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: AllowRealtimeMonitoring
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender real-time Monitoring functionality. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: AllowScanningNetworkFiles
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows a scanning of network files. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: AllowScriptScanning
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Script Scanning functionality. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: AllowUserUIAccess
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows user access to the Windows Defender UI. I disallowed, all Windows Defender notifications will also be suppressed. (0: Prevents users from accessing UI. 1: Lets users access UI)

Parameters.Name: AvgCPULoadFactor
Parameters.Description: Represents the average CPU load factor for the Windows Defender scan (in percent).

Parameters.Name: ArchiveMaxDepth
Parameters.Description: Specify the maximum folder depth to extract from archive files for scanning.

Parameters.Name: ArchiveMaxSize
Parameters.Description: Specify the maximum size, in KB, of archive files to be extracted and scanned.

Parameters.Name: CheckForSignaturesBeforeRunningScan
Parameters.Description: This policy setting allows you to manage whether a check for new virus and spyware definitions will occur before running a scan. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: CloudBlockLevel
Parameters.Description: This policy setting determines how aggressive Microsoft Defender Antivirus will be in blocking and scanning suspicious files. Value type is integer.(0: Default windows defender blocking level, 2: High blocking level, 4:High+ blocking level, 6:Zero tolerance blocking level)

Parameters.Name: CloudExtendedTimeout
Parameters.Description: This feature allows Microsoft Defender Antivirus to block a suspicious file for up to 60 seconds, and scan it in the cloud to make sure it's safe. Value type is integer, range is 0 - 50.

Parameters.Name: DaysToRetainCleanedMalware
Parameters.Description: Time period (in days) that quarantine items will be stored on the system.

Parameters.Name: DisableCatchupFullScan
Parameters.Description: This policy setting allows you to configure catch-up scans for scheduled full scans.  (1: disabled, 0: enabled)

Parameters.Name: DisableCatchupQuickScan
Parameters.Description: This policy setting allows you to configure catch-up scans for scheduled quick scans.  (1: disabled, 0: enabled)

Parameters.Name: DisableCoreServiceECSIntegration
Parameters.Description: Disable Core Service ECS Integration.  (0: disabled, 1: enabled)

Parameters.Name: DisableCoreServiceTelemetry
Parameters.Description: Disable Core Service Telemetry.  (1: disabled, 0: enabled)

Parameters.Name: DisableDnsOverTcpParsing
Parameters.Description: Disables or enables DNS over TCP Parsing for Network Protection. (0: enable feature. 1: disable feature)

Parameters.Name: DisableHttpParsing
Parameters.Description: Disables or enables HTTP Parsing for Network Protection. (0: enable feature. 1: disable feature)

Parameters.Name: DisableSshParsing
Parameters.Description: Disable Ssh Parsing (1: SSH parsing is disabled, 0: SSH parsing is enabled)

Parameters.Name: EnableLowCPUPriority
Parameters.Description: This policy setting allows you to enable or disable low CPU priority for scheduled scans. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: EnableNetworkProtection
Parameters.Description: This policy allows you to turn on network protection (block/audit) or off. (0: disabled, 1: block mode, 2: audit mode)

Parameters.Name: ExcludedExtensions
Parameters.Description: Allows an administrator to specify a list of file type extensions to ignore during a scan.

Parameters.Name: ExcludedPaths
Parameters.Description: Allows an administrator to specify a list of directory paths to ignore during a scan.

Parameters.Name: ExcludedProcesses
Parameters.Description: Allows an administrator to specify a list of files opened by processes to ignore during a scan.

Parameters.Name: PUAProtection
Parameters.Description: Specifies the level of detection for potentially unwanted applications (PUAs). (0: disabled, 1: block mode, 2: audit mode)

Parameters.Name: EngineUpdatesChannel
Parameters.Description: Enable this policy to specify when devices receive Microsoft Defender engine updates during the monthly gradual rollout. (0: Not configured, 2: Beta Channel, 3: Current Channel (Preview), 4: Current Channel (Staged), 5: Current Channel (Broad), 6: Critical)

Parameters.Name: MeteredConnectionUpdates
Parameters.Description: Allow managed devices to update through metered connections. (0: disabled, 1: enabled)

Parameters.Name: PlatformUpdatesChannel
Parameters.Description: Enable this policy to specify when devices receive Microsoft Defender platform updates during the monthly gradual rollout. (0: Not configured, 2: Beta Channel, 3: Current Channel (Preview), 4: Current Channel (Staged), 5: Current Channel (Broad), 6: Critical)

Parameters.Name: SecurityIntelligenceUpdatesChannel
Parameters.Description: Enable this policy to specify when devices receive Microsoft Defender security intelligence updates during the daily gradual rollout. (0: Not configured, 4: Current Channel (Staged), 5: Current Channel (Broad))

Parameters.Name: RealTimeScanDirection
Parameters.Description: Controls which sets of files should be monitored. (0: Monitor all files (bi-directional), 1: Monitor incoming files, 2: Monitor outgoing files)

Parameters.Name: ScanParameter
Parameters.Description: Selects whether to perform a quick scan or full scan. (1: Quick scan, 2: Full scan)

Parameters.Name: ScheduleQuickScanTime
Parameters.Description: Selects the time of day that the Windows Defender quick scan should run.

Parameters.Name: ScheduleScanDay
Parameters.Description: Selects the day that the Windows Defender scan should run. (0: Every day, 1: Sunday, 2: Monday, 3: Tuesday, 4: Wednesday, 5: Thursday, 6: Friday, 7: Saturday, 8: No scheduled scan)

Parameters.Name: ScheduleScanTime
Parameters.Description: Selects the time of day that the Windows Defender scan should run. Must be between 0 and 1380 minutes.

Parameters.Name: DisableTlsParsing
Parameters.Description: This setting disables TLS Parsing for Network Protection. (0: enabled, 1: disabled)

Parameters.Name: RandomizeScheduleTaskTimes
Parameters.Description: Specifies if the start time of the scan is randomized. (0: no randomization, 1: randomized)

Parameters.Name: SchedulerRandomizationTime
Parameters.Description: This setting allows you to configure the scheduler randomization in hours. The randomization interval is [1 - 23] hours.

Parameters.Name: SignatureUpdateFallbackOrder
Parameters.Description: This policy setting allows you to define the order in which different definition update sources should be contacted.

Parameters.Name: SignatureUpdateFileSharesSources
Parameters.Description: This policy setting allows you to configure UNC file share sources for downloading definition updates.

Parameters.Name: SignatureUpdateInterval
Parameters.Description: Specifies the interval (in hours) that will be used to check for signatures, so instead of using the ScheduleDay and ScheduleTime the check for new signatures will be set according to the interval. Must be between 0 and 24 hours.

Parameters.Name: SubmitSamplesConsent
Parameters.Description: Checks for the user consent level in Windows Defender to send data. (0: Always prompt, 1: Send safe samples automatically, 2: Never send, 3: Send all samples automatically)

Parameters.Name: DisableLocalAdminMerge
Parameters.Description: This policy setting controls whether or not complex list settings configured by a local administrator are merged with managed settings. (0: enable local admin merge, 1: disable local admin merge)

Parameters.Name: AllowOnAccessProtection
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender On Access Protection functionality. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

Parameters.Name: LowSeverityThreats
Parameters.Description: Allows an administrator to specify low severity threats corresponding action ID to take.

Parameters.Name: ModerateSeverityThreats
Parameters.Description: Allows an administrator to specify moderate severity threats corresponding action ID to take.

Parameters.Name: SevereThreats
Parameters.Description: Allows an administrator to specify high severity threats corresponding action ID to take.

Parameters.Name: HighSeverityThreats
Parameters.Description: Allows an administrator to specify severe threats corresponding action ID to take.

Parameters.Name: TemplateId
Parameters.Description: Template Id of the policy. 0: Windows Security Experience, 1: Defender Update controls, 2: Microsoft Defender Antivirus exclusions, 3: Microsoft Defender Antivirus

Parameters.Name: Assignments
Parameters.Description: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Present ensures the policy exists, absent ensures it is removed

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Intune Admin

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Name of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication. Format contoso.onmicrosoft.com

Parameters.Name: ApplicationSecret
Parameters.Description: Secret of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity
Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Description:
This resource configures an Intune Endpoint Protection Antivirus policy for a Windows 10 Device.
This policy setting enables the management of Microsoft Defender Antivirus for Windows 10 using the settings catalog.

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: Policy description

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: Policy name

Parameters.Name: RoleScopeTagIds
Parameters.Description: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

Parameters.Name: Id
Parameters.Description: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

Parameters.Name: AllowWindowsDefenderApplicationGuard
Parameters.Description: Turn on Microsoft Defender Application Guard (0: Disable Microsoft Defender Application Guard, 1: Enable Microsoft Defender Application Guard for Microsoft Edge ONLY, 2: Enable Microsoft Defender Application Guard for isolated Windows environments ONLY, 3: Enable Microsoft Defender Application Guard for Microsoft Edge AND isolated Windows environments)

Parameters.Name: ClipboardSettings
Parameters.Description: Clipboard behavior settings (0: Completely turns Off the clipboard functionality for the Application Guard., 1: Turns On clipboard operation from an isolated session to the host., 2: Turns On clipboard operation from the host to an isolated session., 3: Turns On clipboard operation in both the directions.)

Parameters.Name: SaveFilesToHost
Parameters.Description: Allow files to download and save to the host operating system (0: The user cannot download files from Edge in the container to the host file system. When the policy is not configured, it is the same as disabled (0)., 1: Turns on the functionality to allow users to download files from Edge in the container to the host file system.)

Parameters.Name: InstallWindowsDefenderApplicationGuard
Parameters.Description: Install Windows defender application guard (install: Install)

Parameters.Name: ClipboardFileType
Parameters.Description: Clipboard content options (1: Allow text copying., 2: Allow image copying., 3: Allow text and image copying.)

Parameters.Name: AllowPersistence
Parameters.Description: Allow data persistence (0: Application Guard discards user-downloaded files and other items (such as, cookies, Favorites, and so on) during machine restart or user log-off., 1: Application Guard saves user-downloaded files and other items (such as, cookies, Favorites, and so on) for use in future Application Guard sessions.)

Parameters.Name: AllowVirtualGPU
Parameters.Description: Allow hardware-accelerated rendering (0: Cannot access the vGPU and uses the CPU to support rendering graphics. When the policy is not configured, it is the same as disabled (0)., 1: Turns on the functionality to access the vGPU offloading graphics rendering from the CPU. This can create a faster experience when working with graphics intense websites or watching video within the container.)

Parameters.Name: PrintingSettings
Parameters.Description: Print Settings (0: Disables all print functionality., 1: Enables only XPS printing., 2: Enables only PDF printing., 4: Enables only local printing., 8: Enables only network printing.)

Parameters.Name: AllowCameraMicrophoneRedirection
Parameters.Description: Allow camera and microphone access (0: Microsoft Defender Application Guard cannot access the device's camera and microphone. When the policy is not configured, it is the same as disabled (0)., 1: Turns on the functionality to allow Microsoft Defender Application Guard to access the device's camera and microphone.)

Parameters.Name: AuditApplicationGuard
Parameters.Description: Audit Application Guard (0: Audit event logs aren't collected for Application Guard., 1: Application Guard inherits its auditing policies from system and starts to audit security events for Application Guard container.)

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprints
Parameters.Description: Certificate Thumbprints

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseIPRange
Parameters.Description: Enterprise IP Range

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseCloudResources
Parameters.Description: Enterprise Cloud Resources

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseNetworkDomainNames
Parameters.Description: Enterprise Network Domain Names

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseProxyServers
Parameters.Description: Enterprise Proxy Servers

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseInternalProxyServers
Parameters.Description: Enterprise Internal Proxy Servers

Parameters.Name: NeutralResources
Parameters.Description: Neutral Resources

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseProxyServersAreAuthoritative
Parameters.Description: Enterprise Proxy Servers Are Authoritative (1: Enable, 0: Disable)

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseIPRangesAreAuthoritative
Parameters.Description: Enterprise IP Ranges Are Authoritative (1: Enable, 0: Disable)

Parameters.Name: Assignments
Parameters.Description: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Parameters.Name: Ensure

Description: Intune App And Browser Isolation Policy for Windows10

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: Policy description

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: Policy name

Parameters.Name: RoleScopeTagIds
Parameters.Description: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

Parameters.Name: Id
Parameters.Description: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

Parameters.Name: AllowWindowsDefenderApplicationGuard
Parameters.Description: Turn on Microsoft Defender Application Guard (0: Disable Microsoft Defender Application Guard, 1: Enable Microsoft Defender Application Guard for Microsoft Edge ONLY, 2: Enable Microsoft Defender Application Guard for isolated Windows environments ONLY, 3: Enable Microsoft Defender Application Guard for Microsoft Edge AND isolated Windows environments)

Parameters.Name: ClipboardSettings
Parameters.Description: Clipboard behavior settings (0: Completely turns Off the clipboard functionality for the Application Guard., 1: Turns On clipboard operation from an isolated session to the host., 2: Turns On clipboard operation from the host to an isolated session., 3: Turns On clipboard operation in both the directions.)

Parameters.Name: SaveFilesToHost
Parameters.Description: Allow files to download and save to the host operating system (0: The user cannot download files from Edge in the container to the host file system. When the policy is not configured, it is the same as disabled (0)., 1: Turns on the functionality to allow users to download files from Edge in the container to the host file system.)

Parameters.Name: InstallWindowsDefenderApplicationGuard
Parameters.Description: Install Windows defender application guard (install: Install). Required if AllowWindowsDefenderApplicationGuard is not set to 0.

Parameters.Name: ClipboardFileType
Parameters.Description: Clipboard content options (1: Allow text copying., 2: Allow image copying., 3: Allow text and image copying.)

Parameters.Name: AllowPersistence
Parameters.Description: Allow data persistence (0: Application Guard discards user-downloaded files and other items (such as, cookies, Favorites, and so on) during machine restart or user log-off., 1: Application Guard saves user-downloaded files and other items (such as, cookies, Favorites, and so on) for use in future Application Guard sessions.)

Parameters.Name: AllowVirtualGPU
Parameters.Description: Allow hardware-accelerated rendering (0: Cannot access the vGPU and uses the CPU to support rendering graphics. When the policy is not configured, it is the same as disabled (0)., 1: Turns on the functionality to access the vGPU offloading graphics rendering from the CPU. This can create a faster experience when working with graphics intense websites or watching video within the container.)

Parameters.Name: PrintingSettings
Parameters.Description: Print Settings (0: Disables all print functionality., 1: Enables only XPS printing., 2: Enables only PDF printing., 4: Enables only local printing., 8: Enables only network printing.)

Parameters.Name: AllowCameraMicrophoneRedirection
Parameters.Description: Allow camera and microphone access (0: Microsoft Defender Application Guard cannot access the device's camera and microphone. When the policy is not configured, it is the same as disabled (0)., 1: Turns on the functionality to allow Microsoft Defender Application Guard to access the device's camera and microphone.)

Parameters.Name: AuditApplicationGuard
Parameters.Description: Audit Application Guard (0: Audit event logs aren't collected for Application Guard., 1: Application Guard inherits its auditing policies from system and starts to audit security events for Application Guard container.)

Parameters.Description: List of allowed Android device models.

Parameters.Description: Maximum length of outbound clipboard sharing exceptions.

Parameters.Name: BiometricAuthenticationBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether biometric authentication is blocked.

Parameters.Name: BlockAfterCompanyPortalUpdateDeferralInDays
Parameters.Description: Number of days to block access after a company portal update deferral.

Parameters.Name: BlockDataIngestionIntoOrganizationDocuments
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether data ingestion into organization documents is blocked.

Parameters.Name: ConnectToVpnOnLaunch
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether to connect to VPN on launch.

Parameters.Name: CustomDialerAppDisplayName
Parameters.Description: Display name of the custom dialer app.

Parameters.Name: CustomDialerAppPackageId
Parameters.Description: Package ID of the custom dialer app.

Parameters.Name: DeviceLockRequired
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether device lock is required.

Parameters.Name: FingerprintAndBiometricEnabled
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether fingerprint and biometric authentication are enabled.

Parameters.Name: KeyboardsRestricted
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether keyboards are restricted.

Parameters.Name: MessagingRedirectAppDisplayName
Parameters.Description: Display name of the messaging redirect app.

Parameters.Name: MessagingRedirectAppPackageId
Parameters.Description: Package ID of the messaging redirect app.

Parameters.Name: MinimumWipePatchVersion
Parameters.Description: Minimum required patch version for wipe.

Parameters.Name: PreviousPinBlockCount
Parameters.Description: Number of previous PIN block counts.

Parameters.Name: WarnAfterCompanyPortalUpdateDeferralInDays
Parameters.Description: Number of days to warn after a company portal update deferral.

Parameters.Name: WipeAfterCompanyPortalUpdateDeferralInDays
Parameters.Description: Number of days to wipe after a company portal update deferral.

Parameters.Name: Alloweddataingestionlocations
Parameters.Description: Sources from which data is allowed to be transferred.

Parameters.Name: AppActionIfAndroidDeviceManufacturerNotAllowed
Parameters.Description: Defines a managed app behavior, either block or wipe, if the specified device manufacturer is not allowed.

Parameters.Name: AppActionIfAndroidDeviceModelNotAllowed
Parameters.Description: Defines a managed app behavior, either block or wipe, if the specified device model is not allowed.

Parameters.Name: AppActionIfAndroidSafetyNetAppsVerificationFailed
Parameters.Description: Defines a managed app behavior, either warn or block, if the specified Android App Verification requirement fails.

Parameters.Name: AppActionIfAndroidSafetyNetDeviceAttestationFailed
Parameters.Description: Defines a managed app behavior, either warn or block, if the specified Android SafetyNet Attestation requirement fails.

Parameters.Name: AppActionIfDeviceComplianceRequired
Parameters.Description: Defines a managed app behavior, either block or wipe, when the device is either rooted or jailbroken, if DeviceComplianceRequired is set to true.

Parameters.Name: AppActionIfDeviceLockNotSet
Parameters.Description: Defines a managed app behavior, either warn, block, or wipe, if the screen lock is required on an Android device but is not set.

Parameters.Name: AppActionIfMaximumPinRetriesExceeded
Parameters.Description: Defines a managed app behavior, either block or wipe, based on the maximum number of incorrect pin retry attempts.

Parameters.Name: appActionIfUnableToAuthenticateUser
Parameters.Description: Specifies what action to take in the case where the user is unable to check in because their authentication token is invalid, such as when the user is deleted or disabled in Azure AD.

Parameters.Name: MobileThreatDefenseRemediationAction
Parameters.Description: Determines what action to take if the mobile threat defense threat threshold isn't met. Warn isn't a supported value for this property.

Parameters.Name: DialerRestrictionLevel
Parameters.Description: The classes of dialer apps that are allowed to click-to-open a phone number. Inherited from managedAppProtection.

Parameters.Name: MaximumAllowedDeviceThreatLevel
Parameters.Description: Maximum allowed device threat level, as reported by the MTD app. Inherited from managedAppProtection.

Parameters.Name: NotificationRestriction
Parameters.Description: Specify app notification restriction. Inherited from managedAppProtection.

Parameters.Name: ProtectedMessagingRedirectAppType
Parameters.Description: Defines how app messaging redirection is protected by an App Protection Policy. Default is anyApp. Inherited from managedAppProtection.

Parameters.Name: RequiredAndroidSafetyNetAppsVerificationType
Parameters.Description: Defines the Android SafetyNet Apps Verification requirement for a managed app to work.

Parameters.Name: RequiredAndroidSafetyNetDeviceAttestationType
Parameters.Description: Defines the Android SafetyNet Device Attestation requirement for a managed app to work.

Parameters.Name: RequiredAndroidSafetyNetEvaluationType
Parameters.Description: Defines the Android SafetyNet evaluation type requirement for a managed app to work.

Parameters.Name: TargetedAppManagementLevels
Parameters.Description: The intended app management levels for this policy. Inherited from targetedManagedAppProtection.

Parameters.Name: ApprovedKeyboards
Parameters.Description: If Keyboard Restriction is enabled, only keyboards in this approved list will be allowed. A key should be Android package id for a keyboard and value should be a friendly name.

Parameters.Name: ExemptedAppPackages
Parameters.Description: App packages in this list will be exempt from the policy and will be able to receive data from managed apps.

Parameters.Name: PeriodOfflineBeforeAccessCheck
Parameters.Description: The period after which access is checked when the device is not connected to the internet.

Parameters.Name: PeriodOnlineBeforeAccessCheck
Parameters.Description: The period after which access is checked when the device is connected to the internet.

Parameters.Name: AllowedInboundDataTransferSources
Parameters.Description: Sources from which data is allowed to be transferred. Possible values are: allApps, managedApps, none.

Parameters.Name: AllowedOutboundDataTransferDestinations
Parameters.Description: Destinations to which data is allowed to be transferred. Possible values are: allApps, managedApps, none.

Parameters.Name: OrganizationalCredentialsRequired
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether organizational credentials are required for app use.

Parameters.Name: AllowedOutboundClipboardSharingLevel
Parameters.Description: The level to which the clipboard may be shared between apps on the managed device. Possible values are: allApps, managedAppsWithPasteIn, managedApps, blocked.

Parameters.Name: DataBackupBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether the backup of a managed app's data is blocked.

Parameters.Name: DeviceComplianceRequired
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether device compliance is required.

Parameters.Name: ManagedBrowserToOpenLinksRequired
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether internet links should be opened in the managed browser app, or any custom browser specified by CustomBrowserProtocol (for Android) or CustomBrowserPackageId/CustomBrowserDisplayName (for Android).

Parameters.Name: SaveAsBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether users may use the Save As menu item to save a copy of protected files.

Parameters.Name: PeriodOfflineBeforeWipeIsEnforced
Parameters.Description: The amount of time an app is allowed to remain disconnected from the internet before all managed data it is wiped.

Parameters.Name: PinRequired
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether an app-level pin is required.

Parameters.Name: DisableAppPinIfDevicePinIsSet
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether use of the app pin is required if the device pin is set.

Parameters.Name: MaximumPinRetries
Parameters.Description: Maximum number of incorrect pin retry attempts before the managed app is either blocked or wiped.

Parameters.Name: SimplePinBlocked
Parameters.Description: Block simple PIN and require complex PIN to be set.

Parameters.Name: MinimumPinLength
Parameters.Description: Minimum pin length required for an app-level pin if PinRequired is set to True.

Parameters.Name: PinCharacterSet
Parameters.Description: Character set which may be used for an app-level pin if PinRequired is set to True. Possible values are: numeric, alphanumericAndSymbol.

Parameters.Name: AllowedDataStorageLocations
Parameters.Description: Data storage locations where a user may store managed data.

Parameters.Name: ContactSyncBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether contacts can be synced to the user's device.

Parameters.Name: PeriodBeforePinReset
Parameters.Description: TimePeriod before the all-level pin must be reset if PinRequired is set to True.

Parameters.Name: PrintBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether printing is allowed from managed apps.

Parameters.Name: RequireClass3Biometrics
Parameters.Description: Require user to apply Class 3 Biometrics on their Android device.

Parameters.Name: RequirePinAfterBiometricChange
Parameters.Description: A PIN prompt will override biometric prompts if class 3 biometrics are updated on the device.

Parameters.Name: FingerprintBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether use of the fingerprint reader is allowed in place of a pin if PinRequired is set to True.

Parameters.Name: Apps
Parameters.Description: List of IDs representing the Android apps controlled by this protection policy.

Parameters.Name: Assignments
Parameters.Description: List of IDs of the groups assigned to this Android Protection Policy.

Parameters.Name: ExcludedGroups
Parameters.Description: List of IDs of the groups that are excluded from this Android Protection Policy.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Present ensures the policy exists, absent ensures it is removed.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Intune Admin

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: ID of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: ID of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ApplicationSecret
Parameters.Description: Secret of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity
Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ManagedBrowser
Parameters.Description: Indicates in which managed browser(s) that internet links should be opened. Used in conjunction with CustomBrowserPackageId, CustomBrowserDisplayName and ManagedBrowserToOpenLinksRequired. Possible values are: notConfigured, microsoftEdge.

Parameters.Name: MinimumRequiredAppVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions less than the specified version will block the managed app from accessing company data.

Parameters.Name: MinimumRequiredOSVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions less than the specified version will block the managed app from accessing company data.

Parameters.Name: MinimumRequiredPatchVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions less than the specified version will block the managed app from accessing company data.

Parameters.Name: MinimumWarningAppVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions less than the specified version will result in warning message on the managed app

Parameters.Name: MinimumWarningOSVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions less than the specified version will result in warning message on the managed app

Parameters.Name: MinimumWarningPatchVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions less than the specified version will result in warning message on the managed app

Parameters.Name: AppGroupType
Parameters.Description: The apps controlled by this protection policy, overrides any values in Apps unless this value is 'selectedPublicApps'.

Parameters.Name: IsAssigned
Parameters.Description: Indicates if the policy is deployed to any inclusion groups or not. Inherited from targetedManagedAppProtection.

Parameters.Name: ScreenCaptureBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from taking Screenshots.

Parameters.Name: EncryptAppData
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not the 'Encrypt org data' value is enabled.  True = require

Parameters.Name: DisableAppEncryptionIfDeviceEncryptionIsEnabled
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not the 'Encrypt org data on enrolled devices' value is enabled.  False = require.  Only functions if EncryptAppData is set to True

Parameters.Name: CustomBrowserDisplayName
Parameters.Description: The application name for browser associated with the 'Unmanaged Browser ID'. This name will be displayed to users if the specified browser is not installed.

Parameters.Name: CustomBrowserPackageId
Parameters.Description: The application ID for a single browser. Web content (http/s) from policy managed applications will open in the specified browser.

Parameters.Name: Id
Parameters.Description: Id of the Intune policy. To avoid creation of duplicate policies DisplayName will be searched for if the ID is not found

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: AppActionIfAccountIsClockedOut
Parameters.Description: Defines a managed app behavior, either block or warn, if the user is clocked out (non-working time).

Parameters.Name: AppActionIfUnableToAuthenticateUser
Parameters.Description: If set, it will specify what action to take in the case where the user is unable to checkin because their authentication token is invalid. This happens when the user is deleted or disabled in AAD. .

Parameters.Name: AppGroupType
Parameters.Description: Public Apps selection: group or individual Inherited from targetedManagedAppProtection.

Parameters.Name: BlockDataIngestionIntoOrganizationDocuments
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether a user can bring data into org documents.

Parameters.Name: CustomDialerAppProtocol
Parameters.Description: Protocol of a custom dialer app to click-to-open a phone number on iOS, for example, skype:.

Parameters.Name: DeployedAppCount
Parameters.Description: Count of apps to which the current policy is deployed.

Parameters.Name: DialerRestrictionLevel
Parameters.Description: The classes of dialer apps that are allowed to click-to-open a phone number.

Parameters.Name: ExemptedUniversalLinks
Parameters.Description: A list of custom urls that are allowed to invocate an unmanaged app.

Parameters.Name: GracePeriodToBlockAppsDuringOffClockHours
Parameters.Description: A grace period before blocking app access during off clock hours.

Parameters.Name: IsAssigned
Parameters.Description: Indicates if the policy is deployed to any inclusion groups or not.

Parameters.Name: managedUniversalLinks
Parameters.Description: A list of custom urls that are allowed to invocate a managed app.

Parameters.Name: MaximumAllowedDeviceThreatLevel
Parameters.Description: Maximum allowed device threat level, as reported by the MTD app Inherited from managedAppProtection.

Parameters.Name: MaximumRequiredOsVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions bigger than the specified version will block the managed app from accessing company data. Inherited from managedAppProtection.

Parameters.Name: MaximumWarningOsVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions bigger than the specified version will block the managed app from accessing company data. Inherited from managedAppProtection.

Parameters.Name: MaximumWipeOsVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions bigger than the specified version will block the managed app from accessing company data. Inherited from managedAppProtection.

Parameters.Name: MessagingRedirectAppUrlScheme
Parameters.Description: When a specific app redirection is enforced by protectedMessagingRedirectAppType in an App Protection Policy, this value defines the app url redirect schemes which are allowed to be used.

Parameters.Name: MinimumWarningSdkVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions less than the specified version will result in warning message on the managed app from accessing company data.

Parameters.Name: MobileThreatDefensePartnerPriority
Parameters.Description: Indicates how to prioritize which Mobile Threat Defense (MTD) partner is enabled for a given platform, when more than one is enabled. An app can only be actively using a single Mobile Threat Defense partner. When NULL, Microsoft Defender will be given preference. Otherwise setting the value to defenderOverThirdPartyPartner or thirdPartyPartnerOverDefender will make explicit which partner to prioritize.

Parameters.Name: MobileThreatDefenseRemediationAction
Parameters.Description: Determines what action to take if the mobile threat defense threat threshold isn't met. Warn isn't a supported value for this property Inherited from managedAppProtection.

Parameters.Name: PreviousPinBlockCount
Parameters.Description: Requires a pin to be unique from the number specified in this property. Inherited from managedAppProtection.

Parameters.Name: ProtectedMessagingRedirectAppType
Parameters.Description: Defines how app messaging redirection is protected by an App Protection Policy. Default is anyApp. Inherited from managedAppProtection.

Parameters.Name: ThirdPartyKeyboardsBlocked
Parameters.Description: Defines if third party keyboards are allowed while accessing a managed app.

Parameters.Name: PeriodOfflineBeforeAccessCheck
Parameters.Description: The period after which access is checked when the device is not connected to the internet.

Parameters.Name: PeriodOnlineBeforeAccessCheck
Parameters.Description: The period after which access is checked when the device is connected to the internet.

Parameters.Name: AllowedInboundDataTransferSources
Parameters.Description: Sources from which data is allowed to be transferred. Possible values are: allApps, managedApps, none.

Parameters.Name: AllowedOutboundDataTransferDestinations
Parameters.Description: Destinations to which data is allowed to be transferred. Possible values are: allApps, managedApps, none.

Parameters.Name: OrganizationalCredentialsRequired
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether organizational credentials are required for app use.

Parameters.Name: AllowedOutboundClipboardSharingLevel
Parameters.Description: The level to which the clipboard may be shared between apps on the managed device. Possible values are: allApps, managedAppsWithPasteIn, managedApps, blocked.

Parameters.Name: DataBackupBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether the backup of a managed app's data is blocked.

Parameters.Name: DeviceComplianceRequired
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether device compliance is required.

Parameters.Name: ManagedBrowserToOpenLinksRequired
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether internet links should be opened in the managed browser app, or any custom browser specified by CustomBrowserProtocol (for iOS) or CustomBrowserPackageId/CustomBrowserDisplayName (for Android).

Parameters.Name: SaveAsBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether users may use the Save As menu item to save a copy of protected files.

Parameters.Name: PeriodOfflineBeforeWipeIsEnforced
Parameters.Description: The amount of time an app is allowed to remain disconnected from the internet before all managed data it is wiped.

Parameters.Name: PinRequired
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether an app-level pin is required.

Parameters.Name: DisableAppPinIfDevicePinIsSet
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether use of the app pin is required if the device pin is set.

Parameters.Name: MaximumPinRetries
Parameters.Description: Maximum number of incorrect pin retry attempts before the managed app is either blocked or wiped.

Parameters.Name: SimplePinBlocked
Parameters.Description: Block simple PIN and require complex PIN to be set.

Parameters.Name: MinimumPinLength
Parameters.Description: Minimum pin length required for an app-level pin if PinRequired is set to True.

Parameters.Name: PinCharacterSet
Parameters.Description: Character set which may be used for an app-level pin if PinRequired is set to True. Possible values are: numeric, alphanumericAndSymbol.

Parameters.Name: AllowedDataStorageLocations
Parameters.Description: Data storage locations where a user may store managed data.

Parameters.Name: ContactSyncBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether contacts can be synced to the user's device.

Parameters.Name: PeriodBeforePinReset
Parameters.Description: TimePeriod before the all-level pin must be reset if PinRequired is set to True.

Parameters.Name: PrintBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether printing is allowed from managed apps.

Parameters.Name: FingerprintBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether use of the fingerprint reader is allowed in place of a pin if PinRequired is set to True.

Parameters.Name: FaceIdBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether use of the FaceID is allowed in place of a pin if PinRequired is set to True.

Parameters.Name: ManagedBrowser
Parameters.Description: Indicates in which managed browser(s) that internet links should be opened. When this property is configured, ManagedBrowserToOpenLinksRequired should be true. Possible values are: notConfigured, microsoftEdge.

Parameters.Name: MinimumRequiredAppVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions less than the specified version will block the managed app from accessing company data.

Parameters.Name: MinimumWarningAppVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions less than the specified version will result in warning message on the managed app from accessing company data.

Parameters.Name: MinimumRequiredOSVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions less than the specified version will block the managed app from accessing company data.

Parameters.Name: MinimumWarningOSVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions less than the specified version will result in warning message on the managed app from accessing company data.

Parameters.Name: MinimumRequiredSdkVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions less than the specified version will block the managed app from accessing company data.

Parameters.Name: MinimumWipeOSVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions less than or equal to the specified version will wipe the managed app and the associated company data.

Parameters.Name: MinimumWipeAppVersion
Parameters.Description: Versions less than or equal to the specified version will wipe the managed app and the associated company data.

Parameters.Name: AppActionIfDeviceComplianceRequired
Parameters.Description: Defines a managed app behavior, either block or wipe, when the device is either rooted or jailbroken, if DeviceComplianceRequired is set to true.

Parameters.Name: AppActionIfMaximumPinRetriesExceeded
Parameters.Description: Defines a managed app behavior, either block or wipe, based on maximum number of incorrect pin retry attempts.

Parameters.Name: PinRequiredInsteadOfBiometricTimeout

Parameters.Name: NotificationRestriction

Parameters.Name: TargetedAppManagementLevels

Parameters.Name: AppDataEncryptionType

Parameters.Name: ExemptedAppProtocols
Parameters.Description: Apps in this list will be exempt from the policy and will be able to receive data from managed apps.

Parameters.Name: AllowedIosDeviceModels

Parameters.Name: ProtectInboundDataFromUnknownSources
Parameters.Description: Protect incoming data from unknown source. This setting is only allowed to be True when AllowedInboundDataTransferSources is set to AllApps.

Description:
This resource configures an Intune Endpoint Protection Attack Surface Reduction Rules policy for a Windows 10 Device for Configuration Manager.
This policy setting enables setting the state (Block/Audit/Off/Warn) for each attack surface reduction (ASR) rule. Each ASR rule listed can be set to one of the following states (Block/Audit/Off/Warn). The ASR rule ID and state should be added under the Options for this setting. Each entry must be listed as a name value pair. The name defines a valid ASR rule ID, while the value contains the status ID indicating the status of the rule.

For more information about ASR rule ID and status ID, see Enable Attack Surface Reduction: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/threat-protection/windows-defender-exploit-guard/enable-attack-surface-reduction.
