Parameters.Name: EncryptionMethod
Parameters.Description: Select the encryption method for fixed drives. Possible values are: aesCbc128, aesCbc256, xtsAes128, xtsAes256.

Parameters.Name: RecoveryOptions
Parameters.Description: This policy setting allows you to control how BitLocker-protected fixed data drives are recovered in the absence of the required credentials. This policy setting is applied when you turn on BitLocker.

Parameters.Name: RequireEncryptionForWriteAccess
Parameters.Description: This policy setting determines whether BitLocker protection is required for fixed data drives to be writable on a computer.

Description: Intune Device Configuration Endpoint Protection Policy for Windows10

Parameters.Name: BlockDataRecoveryAgent
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether to block certificate-based data recovery agent.

Parameters.Name: EnableBitLockerAfterRecoveryInformationToStore
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to enable BitLocker until recovery information is stored in AD DS.

Parameters.Name: EnableRecoveryInformationSaveToStore
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to allow BitLocker recovery information to store in AD DS.

Parameters.Name: HideRecoveryOptions
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to allow showing recovery options in BitLocker Setup Wizard for fixed or system disk.

Parameters.Name: RecoveryInformationToStore
Parameters.Description: Configure what pieces of BitLocker recovery information are stored to AD DS. Possible values are: passwordAndKey, passwordOnly.

Parameters.Name: RecoveryKeyUsage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether users are allowed or required to generate a 256-bit recovery key for fixed or system disk. Possible values are: blocked, required, allowed, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: RecoveryPasswordUsage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether users are allowed or required to generate a 48-digit recovery password for fixed or system disk. Possible values are: blocked, required, allowed, notConfigured.

Description: Intune Device Configuration Endpoint Protection Policy for Windows10

Parameters.Name: BlockCrossOrganizationWriteAccess
Parameters.Description: This policy setting determines whether BitLocker protection is required for removable data drives to be writable on a computer.

Parameters.Name: EncryptionMethod
Parameters.Description: Select the encryption method for removable  drives. Possible values are: aesCbc128, aesCbc256, xtsAes128, xtsAes256.

Parameters.Name: RequireEncryptionForWriteAccess
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether to block write access to devices configured in another organization.  If requireEncryptionForWriteAccess is false, this value does not affect.

Description: Intune Device Configuration Endpoint Protection Policy for Windows10

Parameters.Name: EncryptionMethod
Parameters.Description: Select the encryption method for operating system drives. Possible values are: aesCbc128, aesCbc256, xtsAes128, xtsAes256.

Parameters.Name: MinimumPinLength
Parameters.Description: Indicates the minimum length of startup pin. Valid values 4 to 20

Parameters.Name: PrebootRecoveryEnableMessageAndUrl
Parameters.Description: Enable pre-boot recovery message and Url. If requireStartupAuthentication is false, this value does not affect.

Parameters.Name: PrebootRecoveryMessage
Parameters.Description: Defines a custom recovery message.

Parameters.Name: PrebootRecoveryUrl
Parameters.Description: Defines a custom recovery URL.

Parameters.Name: RecoveryOptions
Parameters.Description: Allows to recover BitLocker encrypted operating system drives in the absence of the required startup key information. This policy setting is applied when you turn on BitLocker.

Parameters.Name: StartupAuthenticationBlockWithoutTpmChip
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether to allow BitLocker without a compatible TPM (requires a password or a startup key on a USB flash drive).

Parameters.Name: StartupAuthenticationRequired
Parameters.Description: Require additional authentication at startup.

Parameters.Name: StartupAuthenticationTpmKeyUsage
Parameters.Description: Indicates if TPM startup key is allowed/required/disallowed. Possible values are: blocked, required, allowed, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: StartupAuthenticationTpmPinAndKeyUsage
Parameters.Description: Indicates if TPM startup pin key and key are allowed/required/disallowed. Possible values are: blocked, required, allowed, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: StartupAuthenticationTpmPinUsage
Parameters.Description: Indicates if TPM startup pin is allowed/required/disallowed. Possible values are: blocked, required, allowed, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: StartupAuthenticationTpmUsage
Parameters.Description: Indicates if TPM startup is allowed/required/disallowed. Possible values are: blocked, required, allowed, notConfigured.

Description: Intune Device Configuration Endpoint Protection Policy for Windows10

Parameters.Name: HighSeverity
Parameters.Description: Indicates a Defender action to take for high severity Malware threat detected. Possible values are: deviceDefault, clean, quarantine, remove, allow, userDefined, block.

Parameters.Name: LowSeverity
Parameters.Description: Indicates a Defender action to take for low severity Malware threat detected. Possible values are: deviceDefault, clean, quarantine, remove, allow, userDefined, block.

Parameters.Name: ModerateSeverity
Parameters.Description: Indicates a Defender action to take for moderate severity Malware threat detected. Possible values are: deviceDefault, clean, quarantine, remove, allow, userDefined, block.

Parameters.Name: SevereSeverity
Parameters.Description: Indicates a Defender action to take for severe severity Malware threat detected. Possible values are: deviceDefault, clean, quarantine, remove, allow, userDefined, block.

Description: Intune Device Configuration Endpoint Protection Policy for Windows10

Parameters.Name: AuthorizedApplicationRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to merge authorized application rules from group policy with those from local store instead of ignoring the local store rules. When AuthorizedApplicationRulesFromGroupPolicyNotMerged and AuthorizedApplicationRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged are both true, AuthorizedApplicationRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged takes priority.

Parameters.Name: AuthorizedApplicationRulesFromGroupPolicyNotMerged
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to prevent merging authorized application rules from group policy with those from local store instead of ignoring the local store rules. When AuthorizedApplicationRulesFromGroupPolicyNotMerged and AuthorizedApplicationRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged are both true, AuthorizedApplicationRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged takes priority.

Parameters.Name: ConnectionSecurityRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to merge connection security rules from group policy with those from local store instead of ignoring the local store rules. When ConnectionSecurityRulesFromGroupPolicyNotMerged and ConnectionSecurityRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged are both true, ConnectionSecurityRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged takes priority.

Parameters.Name: ConnectionSecurityRulesFromGroupPolicyNotMerged
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to prevent merging connection security rules from group policy with those from local store instead of ignoring the local store rules. When ConnectionSecurityRulesFromGroupPolicyNotMerged and ConnectionSecurityRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged are both true, ConnectionSecurityRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged takes priority.

Parameters.Name: FirewallEnabled
Parameters.Description: Configures the host device to allow or block the firewall and advanced security enforcement for the network profile. Possible values are: notConfigured, blocked, allowed.

Parameters.Name: GlobalPortRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to merge global port rules from group policy with those from local store instead of ignoring the local store rules. When GlobalPortRulesFromGroupPolicyNotMerged and GlobalPortRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged are both true, GlobalPortRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged takes priority.

Parameters.Name: GlobalPortRulesFromGroupPolicyNotMerged
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to prevent merging global port rules from group policy with those from local store instead of ignoring the local store rules. When GlobalPortRulesFromGroupPolicyNotMerged and GlobalPortRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged are both true, GlobalPortRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged takes priority.

Parameters.Name: InboundConnectionsBlocked
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to block all incoming connections by default. When InboundConnectionsRequired and InboundConnectionsBlocked are both true, InboundConnectionsBlocked takes priority.

Parameters.Name: InboundConnectionsRequired
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to allow all incoming connections by default. When InboundConnectionsRequired and InboundConnectionsBlocked are both true, InboundConnectionsBlocked takes priority.

Parameters.Name: InboundNotificationsBlocked
Parameters.Description: Prevents the firewall from displaying notifications when an application is blocked from listening on a port. When InboundNotificationsRequired and InboundNotificationsBlocked are both true, InboundNotificationsBlocked takes priority.

Parameters.Name: InboundNotificationsRequired
Parameters.Description: Allows the firewall to display notifications when an application is blocked from listening on a port. When InboundNotificationsRequired and InboundNotificationsBlocked are both true, InboundNotificationsBlocked takes priority.

Parameters.Name: IncomingTrafficBlocked
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to block all incoming traffic regardless of other policy settings. When IncomingTrafficRequired and IncomingTrafficBlocked are both true, IncomingTrafficBlocked takes priority.

Parameters.Name: IncomingTrafficRequired
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to allow incoming traffic pursuant to other policy settings. When IncomingTrafficRequired and IncomingTrafficBlocked are both true, IncomingTrafficBlocked takes priority.

Parameters.Name: OutboundConnectionsBlocked
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to block all outgoing connections by default. When OutboundConnectionsRequired and OutboundConnectionsBlocked are both true, OutboundConnectionsBlocked takes priority. This setting will get applied to Windows releases version 1809 and above.

Parameters.Name: OutboundConnectionsRequired
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to allow all outgoing connections by default. When OutboundConnectionsRequired and OutboundConnectionsBlocked are both true, OutboundConnectionsBlocked takes priority. This setting will get applied to Windows releases version 1809 and above.

Parameters.Name: PolicyRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to merge Firewall Rule policies from group policy with those from local store instead of ignoring the local store rules. When PolicyRulesFromGroupPolicyNotMerged and PolicyRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged are both true, PolicyRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged takes priority.

Parameters.Name: PolicyRulesFromGroupPolicyNotMerged
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to prevent merging Firewall Rule policies from group policy with those from local store instead of ignoring the local store rules. When PolicyRulesFromGroupPolicyNotMerged and PolicyRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged are both true, PolicyRulesFromGroupPolicyMerged takes priority.

Parameters.Name: SecuredPacketExemptionAllowed
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to allow the host computer to respond to unsolicited network traffic of that traffic is secured by IPSec even when stealthModeBlocked is set to true. When SecuredPacketExemptionBlocked and SecuredPacketExemptionAllowed are both true, SecuredPacketExemptionAllowed takes priority.

Parameters.Name: SecuredPacketExemptionBlocked
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to block the host computer to respond to unsolicited network traffic of that traffic is secured by IPSec even when stealthModeBlocked is set to true. When SecuredPacketExemptionBlocked and SecuredPacketExemptionAllowed are both true, SecuredPacketExemptionAllowed takes priority.

Parameters.Name: StealthModeBlocked
Parameters.Description: Prevent the server from operating in stealth mode. When StealthModeRequired and StealthModeBlocked are both true, StealthModeBlocked takes priority.

Parameters.Name: StealthModeRequired
Parameters.Description: Allow the server to operate in stealth mode. When StealthModeRequired and StealthModeBlocked are both true, StealthModeBlocked takes priority.

Parameters.Name: UnicastResponsesToMulticastBroadcastsBlocked
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to block unicast responses to multicast broadcast traffic. When UnicastResponsesToMulticastBroadcastsRequired and UnicastResponsesToMulticastBroadcastsBlocked are both true, UnicastResponsesToMulticastBroadcastsBlocked takes priority.

Parameters.Name: UnicastResponsesToMulticastBroadcastsRequired
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall to allow unicast responses to multicast broadcast traffic. When UnicastResponsesToMulticastBroadcastsRequired and UnicastResponsesToMulticastBroadcastsBlocked are both true, UnicastResponsesToMulticastBroadcastsBlocked takes priority.

Description: Intune Device Configuration Endpoint Protection Policy for Windows10

Parameters.Name: Action
Parameters.Description: The action the rule enforces. If not specified, the default is Allowed. Possible values are: notConfigured, blocked, allowed.

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: The description of the rule.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: The display name of the rule. Does not need to be unique.

Parameters.Name: EdgeTraversal
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether edge traversal is enabled or disabled for this rule. The EdgeTraversal setting indicates that specific inbound traffic is allowed to tunnel through NATs and other edge devices using the Teredo tunneling technology. In order for this setting to work correctly, the application or service with the inbound firewall rule needs to support IPv6. The primary application of this setting allows listeners on the host to be globally addressable through a Teredo IPv6 address. New rules have the EdgeTraversal property disabled by default. Possible values are: notConfigured, blocked, allowed.

Parameters.Name: FilePath
Parameters.Description: The full file path of an app that's affected by the firewall rule.

Parameters.Name: InterfaceTypes
Parameters.Description: The interface types of the rule. Possible values are: notConfigured, remoteAccess, wireless, lan.

Parameters.Name: LocalAddressRanges
Parameters.Description: List of local addresses covered by the rule. Default is any address. Valid tokens include:'' indicates any local address. If present, this must be the only token included.A subnet can be specified using either the subnet mask or network prefix notation. If neither a subnet mask nor a network prefix is specified, the subnet mask defaults to 255.255.255.255.A valid IPv6 address.An IPv4 address range in the format of 'start address - end address' with no spaces included.An IPv6 address range in the format of 'start address - end address' with no spaces included.

Parameters.Name: LocalPortRanges
Parameters.Description: List of local port ranges. For example, '100-120', '200', '300-320'. If not specified, the default is All.

Parameters.Name: LocalUserAuthorizations
Parameters.Description: Specifies the list of authorized local users for the app container. This is a string in Security Descriptor Definition Language (SDDL) format.

Parameters.Name: PackageFamilyName
Parameters.Description: The package family name of a Microsoft Store application that's affected by the firewall rule.

Parameters.Name: ProfileTypes
Parameters.Description: Specifies the profiles to which the rule belongs. If not specified, the default is All. Possible values are: notConfigured, domain, private, public.

Parameters.Name: Protocol
Parameters.Description: 0-255 number representing the IP protocol (TCP = 6, UDP = 17). If not specified, the default is All. Valid values 0 to 255

Parameters.Name: RemoteAddressRanges
Parameters.Description: List of tokens specifying the remote addresses covered by the rule. Tokens are case insensitive. Default is any address. Valid tokens include:'' indicates any remote address. If present, this must be the only token included.'Defaultgateway''DHCP''DNS''WINS''Intranet' (supported on Windows versions 1809+)'RmtIntranet' (supported on Windows versions 1809+)'Internet' (supported on Windows versions 1809+)'Ply2Renders' (supported on Windows versions 1809+)'LocalSubnet' indicates any local address on the local subnet.A subnet can be specified using either the subnet mask or network prefix notation. If neither a subnet mask nor a network prefix is specified, the subnet mask defaults to 255.255.255.255.A valid IPv6 address.An IPv4 address range in the format of 'start address - end address' with no spaces included.An IPv6 address range in the format of 'start address - end address' with no spaces included.

Parameters.Name: RemotePortRanges
Parameters.Description: List of remote port ranges. For example, '100-120', '200', '300-320'. If not specified, the default is All.

Parameters.Name: ServiceName
Parameters.Description: The name used in cases when a service, not an application, is sending or receiving traffic.

Parameters.Name: TrafficDirection
Parameters.Description: The traffic direction that the rule is enabled for. If not specified, the default is Out. Possible values are: notConfigured, out, in.

Description: Intune Device Configuration Endpoint Protection Policy for Windows10

Parameters.Name: LocalUsersOrGroups
Parameters.Description: Representing a collection of local users or groups which will be set on device if the state of this setting is Allowed. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

Parameters.Name: State
Parameters.Description: Representing the current state of this user rights setting. Possible values are: notConfigured, blocked, allowed.

Description: Intune Device Configuration Endpoint Protection Policy for Windows10

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: Admins description of this local user or group.

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: The name of this local user or group.

Parameters.Name: SecurityIdentifier
Parameters.Description: The security identifier of this local user or group (e.g. S-1-5-32-544).

Description: Intune Device Configuration Endpoint Protection Policy for Windows10

Parameters.Name: ApplicationGuardAllowCameraMicrophoneRedirection
Parameters.Description: Gets or sets whether applications inside Microsoft Defender Application Guard can access the devices camera and microphone.

Parameters.Name: ApplicationGuardAllowFileSaveOnHost
Parameters.Description: Allow users to download files from Edge in the application guard container and save them on the host file system

Parameters.Name: ApplicationGuardAllowPersistence
Parameters.Description: Allow persisting user generated data inside the App Guard Container (favorites, cookies, web passwords, etc.)

Parameters.Name: ApplicationGuardAllowPrintToLocalPrinters
Parameters.Description: Allow printing to Local Printers from Container

Parameters.Name: ApplicationGuardAllowPrintToNetworkPrinters
Parameters.Description: Allow printing to Network Printers from Container

Parameters.Name: ApplicationGuardAllowPrintToPDF
Parameters.Description: Allow printing to PDF from Container

Parameters.Name: ApplicationGuardAllowPrintToXPS
Parameters.Description: Allow printing to XPS from Container

Parameters.Name: ApplicationGuardAllowVirtualGPU
Parameters.Description: Allow application guard to use virtual GPU

Parameters.Name: ApplicationGuardBlockClipboardSharing
Parameters.Description: Block clipboard to share data from Host to Container, or from Container to Host, or both ways, or neither ways. Possible values are: notConfigured, blockBoth, blockHostToContainer, blockContainerToHost, blockNone.

Parameters.Name: ApplicationGuardBlockFileTransfer
Parameters.Description: Block clipboard to transfer image file, text file or neither of them. Possible values are: notConfigured, blockImageAndTextFile, blockImageFile, blockNone, blockTextFile.

Parameters.Name: ApplicationGuardBlockNonEnterpriseContent
Parameters.Description: Block enterprise sites to load non-enterprise content, such as third party plug-ins

Parameters.Name: ApplicationGuardCertificateThumbprints
Parameters.Description: Allows certain device level Root Certificates to be shared with the Microsoft Defender Application Guard container.

Parameters.Name: ApplicationGuardEnabled
Parameters.Description: Enable Windows Defender Application Guard

Parameters.Name: ApplicationGuardEnabledOptions
Parameters.Description: Enable Windows Defender Application Guard for newer Windows builds. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabledForEdge, enabledForOffice, enabledForEdgeAndOffice.

Parameters.Name: ApplicationGuardForceAuditing
Parameters.Description: Force auditing will persist Windows logs and events to meet security/compliance criteria (sample events are user login-logoff, use of privilege rights, software installation, system changes, etc.)

Parameters.Name: AppLockerApplicationControl
Parameters.Description: Enables the Admin to choose what types of app to allow on devices. Possible values are: notConfigured, enforceComponentsAndStoreApps, auditComponentsAndStoreApps, enforceComponentsStoreAppsAndSmartlocker, auditComponentsStoreAppsAndSmartlocker.

Parameters.Name: BitLockerAllowStandardUserEncryption
Parameters.Description: Allows the admin to allow standard users to enable encryption during Azure AD Join.

Parameters.Name: BitLockerDisableWarningForOtherDiskEncryption
Parameters.Description: Allows the Admin to disable the warning prompt for other disk encryption on the user machines.

Parameters.Name: BitLockerEnableStorageCardEncryptionOnMobile
Parameters.Description: Allows the admin to require encryption to be turned on using BitLocker. This policy is valid only for a mobile SKU.

Parameters.Name: BitLockerEncryptDevice
Parameters.Description: Allows the admin to require encryption to be turned on using BitLocker.

Parameters.Name: BitLockerFixedDrivePolicy
Parameters.Description: BitLocker Fixed Drive Policy.

Parameters.Name: BitLockerRecoveryPasswordRotation
Parameters.Description: This setting initiates a client-driven recovery password rotation after an OS drive recovery (either by using bootmgr or WinRE). Possible values are: notConfigured, disabled, enabledForAzureAd, enabledForAzureAdAndHybrid.

Parameters.Name: BitLockerRemovableDrivePolicy
Parameters.Description: BitLocker Removable Drive Policy.

Parameters.Name: BitLockerSystemDrivePolicy
Parameters.Description: BitLocker System Drive Policy.

Parameters.Name: DefenderAdditionalGuardedFolders
Parameters.Description: List of folder paths to be added to the list of protected folders

Parameters.Name: DefenderAdobeReaderLaunchChildProcess
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior of Adobe Reader from creating child processes. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderAdvancedRansomewareProtectionType
Parameters.Description: Value indicating use of advanced protection against ransomeware. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderAllowBehaviorMonitoring
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Behavior Monitoring functionality.

Parameters.Name: DefenderAllowCloudProtection
Parameters.Description: To best protect your PC, Windows Defender will send information to Microsoft about any problems it finds. Microsoft will analyze that information, learn more about problems affecting you and other customers, and offer improved solutions.

Parameters.Name: DefenderAllowEndUserAccess
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows user access to the Windows Defender UI. If disallowed, all Windows Defender notifications will also be suppressed.

Parameters.Name: DefenderAllowIntrusionPreventionSystem
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Intrusion Prevention functionality.

Parameters.Name: DefenderAllowOnAccessProtection
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender On Access Protection functionality.

Parameters.Name: DefenderAllowRealTimeMonitoring
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Realtime Monitoring functionality.

Parameters.Name: DefenderAllowScanArchiveFiles
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows scanning of archives.

Parameters.Name: DefenderAllowScanDownloads
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender IOAVP Protection functionality.

Parameters.Name: DefenderAllowScanNetworkFiles
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows a scanning of network files.

Parameters.Name: DefenderAllowScanRemovableDrivesDuringFullScan
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows a full scan of removable drives. During a quick scan, removable drives may still be scanned.

Parameters.Name: DefenderAllowScanScriptsLoadedInInternetExplorer
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Script Scanning functionality.

Parameters.Name: DefenderAttackSurfaceReductionExcludedPaths
Parameters.Description: List of exe files and folders to be excluded from attack surface reduction rules

Parameters.Name: DefenderBlockEndUserAccess
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows user access to the Windows Defender UI. If disallowed, all Windows Defender notifications will also be suppressed.

Parameters.Name: DefenderBlockPersistenceThroughWmiType
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior ofBlock persistence through WMI event subscription. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

Parameters.Name: DefenderCheckForSignaturesBeforeRunningScan
Parameters.Description: This policy setting allows you to manage whether a check for new virus and spyware definitions will occur before running a scan.

Parameters.Name: DefenderCloudBlockLevel
Parameters.Description: Added in Windows 10, version 1709. This policy setting determines how aggressive Windows Defender Antivirus will be in blocking and scanning suspicious files. Value type is integer. This feature requires the 'Join Microsoft MAPS' setting enabled in order to function. Possible values are: notConfigured, high, highPlus, zeroTolerance.

Parameters.Name: DefenderCloudExtendedTimeoutInSeconds
Parameters.Description: Added in Windows 10, version 1709. This feature allows Windows Defender Antivirus to block a suspicious file for up to 60 seconds, and scan it in the cloud to make sure it's safe. Value type is integer, range is 0 - 50. This feature depends on three other MAPS settings the must all be enabled- 'Configure the 'Block at First Sight' feature 'Join Microsoft MAPS' 'Send file samples when further analysis is required'. Valid values 0 to 50

Parameters.Name: DefenderDaysBeforeDeletingQuarantinedMalware
Parameters.Description: Time period (in days) that quarantine items will be stored on the system. Valid values 0 to 90

Parameters.Name: DefenderDetectedMalwareActions
Parameters.Description: Allows an administrator to specify any valid threat severity levels and the corresponding default action ID to take.

Parameters.Name: DefenderDisableBehaviorMonitoring
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Behavior Monitoring functionality.

Parameters.Name: DefenderDisableCatchupFullScan
Parameters.Description: This policy setting allows you to configure catch-up scans for scheduled full scans. A catch-up scan is a scan that is initiated because a regularly scheduled scan was missed. Usually these scheduled scans are missed because the computer was turned off at the scheduled time.

Parameters.Name: DefenderDisableCatchupQuickScan
Parameters.Description: This policy setting allows you to configure catch-up scans for scheduled quick scans. A catch-up scan is a scan that is initiated because a regularly scheduled scan was missed. Usually these scheduled scans are missed because the computer was turned off at the scheduled time.

Parameters.Name: DefenderDisableCloudProtection
Parameters.Description: To best protect your PC, Windows Defender will send information to Microsoft about any problems it finds. Microsoft will analyze that information, learn more about problems affecting you and other customers, and offer improved solutions.

Parameters.Name: DefenderDisableIntrusionPreventionSystem
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Intrusion Prevention functionality.

Parameters.Name: DefenderDisableOnAccessProtection
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender On Access Protection functionality.

Parameters.Name: DefenderDisableRealTimeMonitoring
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Realtime Monitoring functionality.

Parameters.Name: DefenderDisableScanArchiveFiles
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows scanning of archives.

Parameters.Name: DefenderDisableScanDownloads
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender IOAVP Protection functionality.

Parameters.Name: DefenderDisableScanNetworkFiles
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows a scanning of network files.

Parameters.Name: DefenderDisableScanRemovableDrivesDuringFullScan
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows a full scan of removable drives. During a quick scan, removable drives may still be scanned.

Parameters.Name: DefenderDisableScanScriptsLoadedInInternetExplorer
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Script Scanning functionality.

Parameters.Name: DefenderEmailContentExecution
Parameters.Description: Value indicating if execution of executable content (exe, dll, ps, js, vbs, etc) should be dropped from email (webmail/mail-client). Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderEmailContentExecutionType
Parameters.Description: Value indicating if execution of executable content (exe, dll, ps, js, vbs, etc) should be dropped from email (webmail/mail-client). Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

Parameters.Name: DefenderEnableLowCpuPriority
Parameters.Description: This policy setting allows you to enable or disable low CPU priority for scheduled scans.

Parameters.Name: DefenderEnableScanIncomingMail
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows scanning of email.

Parameters.Name: DefenderEnableScanMappedNetworkDrivesDuringFullScan
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows a full scan of mapped network drives.

Parameters.Name: DefenderExploitProtectionXml
Parameters.Description: Xml content containing information regarding exploit protection details.

Parameters.Name: DefenderExploitProtectionXmlFileName
Parameters.Description: Name of the file from which DefenderExploitProtectionXml was obtained.

Parameters.Name: DefenderFileExtensionsToExclude
Parameters.Description: File extensions to exclude from scans and real time protection.

Parameters.Name: DefenderFilesAndFoldersToExclude
Parameters.Description: Files and folder to exclude from scans and real time protection.

Parameters.Name: DefenderGuardedFoldersAllowedAppPaths
Parameters.Description: List of paths to exe that are allowed to access protected folders

Parameters.Name: DefenderGuardMyFoldersType
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior of protected folders. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, blockDiskModification, auditDiskModification.

Parameters.Name: DefenderNetworkProtectionType
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior of NetworkProtection. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderOfficeAppsExecutableContentCreationOrLaunch
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior of Office applications/macros creating or launching executable content. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderOfficeAppsExecutableContentCreationOrLaunchType
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior of Office applications/macros creating or launching executable content. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

Parameters.Name: DefenderOfficeAppsLaunchChildProcess
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior of Office application launching child processes. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderOfficeAppsLaunchChildProcessType
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior of Office application launching child processes. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

Parameters.Name: DefenderOfficeAppsOtherProcessInjection
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior of Office applications injecting into other processes. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderOfficeAppsOtherProcessInjectionType
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior ofOffice applications injecting into other processes. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

Parameters.Name: DefenderOfficeCommunicationAppsLaunchChildProcess
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior of Office communication applications, including Microsoft Outlook, from creating child processes. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderOfficeMacroCodeAllowWin32Imports
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior of Win32 imports from Macro code in Office. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderOfficeMacroCodeAllowWin32ImportsType
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior of Win32 imports from Macro code in Office. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

Parameters.Name: DefenderPotentiallyUnwantedAppAction
Parameters.Description: Added in Windows 10, version 1607. Specifies the level of detection for potentially unwanted applications (PUAs). Windows Defender alerts you when potentially unwanted software is being downloaded or attempts to install itself on your computer. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderPreventCredentialStealingType
Parameters.Description: Value indicating if credential stealing from the Windows local security authority subsystem is permitted. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderProcessCreation
Parameters.Description: Value indicating response to process creations originating from PSExec and WMI commands. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderProcessCreationType
Parameters.Description: Value indicating response to process creations originating from PSExec and WMI commands. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

Parameters.Name: DefenderProcessesToExclude
Parameters.Description: Processes to exclude from scans and real time protection.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScanDirection
Parameters.Description: Controls which sets of files should be monitored. Possible values are: monitorAllFiles, monitorIncomingFilesOnly, monitorOutgoingFilesOnly.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScanMaxCpuPercentage
Parameters.Description: Represents the average CPU load factor for the Windows Defender scan (in percent). The default value is 50. Valid values 0 to 100

Parameters.Name: DefenderScanType
Parameters.Description: Selects whether to perform a quick scan or full scan. Possible values are: userDefined, disabled, quick, full.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScheduledQuickScanTime
Parameters.Description: Selects the time of day that the Windows Defender quick scan should run. For example, a value of 0=12:00AM, a value of 60=1:00AM, a value of 120=2:00, and so on, up to a value of 1380=11:00PM. The default value is 120

Parameters.Name: DefenderScheduledScanDay
Parameters.Description: Selects the day that the Windows Defender scan should run. Possible values are: userDefined, everyday, sunday, monday, tuesday, wednesday, thursday, friday, saturday, noScheduledScan.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScheduledScanTime
Parameters.Description: Selects the time of day that the Windows Defender scan should run.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScriptDownloadedPayloadExecution
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior of js/vbs executing payload downloaded from Internet. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScriptDownloadedPayloadExecutionType
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior of js/vbs executing payload downloaded from Internet. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScriptObfuscatedMacroCode
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior of obfuscated js/vbs/ps/macro code. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScriptObfuscatedMacroCodeType
Parameters.Description: Value indicating the behavior of obfuscated js/vbs/ps/macro code. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterBlockExploitProtectionOverride
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block user from overriding Exploit Protection settings.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterDisableAccountUI
Parameters.Description: Used to disable the display of the account protection area.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterDisableAppBrowserUI
Parameters.Description: Used to disable the display of the app and browser protection area.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterDisableClearTpmUI
Parameters.Description: Used to disable the display of the Clear TPM button.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterDisableFamilyUI
Parameters.Description: Used to disable the display of the family options area.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterDisableHardwareUI
Parameters.Description: Used to disable the display of the hardware protection area.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterDisableHealthUI
Parameters.Description: Used to disable the display of the device performance and health area.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterDisableNetworkUI
Parameters.Description: Used to disable the display of the firewall and network protection area.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterDisableNotificationAreaUI
Parameters.Description: Used to disable the display of the notification area control. The user needs to either sign out and sign in or reboot the computer for this setting to take effect.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterDisableRansomwareUI
Parameters.Description: Used to disable the display of the ransomware protection area.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterDisableSecureBootUI
Parameters.Description: Used to disable the display of the secure boot area under Device security.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterDisableTroubleshootingUI
Parameters.Description: Used to disable the display of the security process troubleshooting under Device security.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterDisableVirusUI
Parameters.Description: Used to disable the display of the virus and threat protection area.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterDisableVulnerableTpmFirmwareUpdateUI
Parameters.Description: Used to disable the display of update TPM Firmware when a vulnerable firmware is detected.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterHelpEmail
Parameters.Description: The email address that is displayed to users.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterHelpPhone
Parameters.Description: The phone number or Skype ID that is displayed to users.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterHelpURL
Parameters.Description: The help portal URL this is displayed to users.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterITContactDisplay
Parameters.Description: Configure where to display IT contact information to end users. Possible values are: notConfigured, displayInAppAndInNotifications, displayOnlyInApp, displayOnlyInNotifications.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterNotificationsFromApp
Parameters.Description: Notifications to show from the displayed areas of app. Possible values are: notConfigured, blockNoncriticalNotifications, blockAllNotifications.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSecurityCenterOrganizationDisplayName
Parameters.Description: The company name that is displayed to the users.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSignatureUpdateIntervalInHours
Parameters.Description: Specifies the interval (in hours) that will be used to check for signatures, so instead of using the ScheduleDay and ScheduleTime the check for new signatures will be set according to the interval. Valid values 0 to 24

Parameters.Name: DefenderSubmitSamplesConsentType
Parameters.Description: Checks for the user consent level in Windows Defender to send data. Possible values are: sendSafeSamplesAutomatically, alwaysPrompt, neverSend, sendAllSamplesAutomatically.

Parameters.Name: DefenderUntrustedExecutable
Parameters.Description: Value indicating response to executables that don't meet a prevalence, age, or trusted list criteria. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderUntrustedExecutableType
Parameters.Description: Value indicating response to executables that don't meet a prevalence, age, or trusted list criteria. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

Parameters.Name: DefenderUntrustedUSBProcess
Parameters.Description: Value indicating response to untrusted and unsigned processes that run from USB. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderUntrustedUSBProcessType
Parameters.Description: Value indicating response to untrusted and unsigned processes that run from USB. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

Parameters.Name: DeviceGuardEnableSecureBootWithDMA
Parameters.Description: This property will be deprecated in May 2019 and will be replaced with property DeviceGuardSecureBootWithDMA. Specifies whether Platform Security Level is enabled at next reboot.

Parameters.Name: DeviceGuardEnableVirtualizationBasedSecurity
Parameters.Description: Turns On Virtualization Based Security(VBS).

Parameters.Name: DeviceGuardLaunchSystemGuard
Parameters.Description: Allows the IT admin to configure the launch of System Guard. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: DeviceGuardLocalSystemAuthorityCredentialGuardSettings
Parameters.Description: Turn on Credential Guard when Platform Security Level with Secure Boot and Virtualization Based Security are both enabled. Possible values are: notConfigured, enableWithUEFILock, enableWithoutUEFILock, disable.

Parameters.Name: DeviceGuardSecureBootWithDMA
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether Platform Security Level is enabled at next reboot. Possible values are: notConfigured, withoutDMA, withDMA.

Parameters.Name: DmaGuardDeviceEnumerationPolicy
Parameters.Description: This policy is intended to provide additional security against external DMA capable devices. It allows for more control over the enumeration of external DMA capable devices incompatible with DMA Remapping/device memory isolation and sandboxing. This policy only takes effect when Kernel DMA Protection is supported and enabled by the system firmware. Kernel DMA Protection is a platform feature that cannot be controlled via policy or by end user. It has to be supported by the system at the time of manufacturing. To check if the system supports Kernel DMA Protection, please check the Kernel DMA Protection field in the Summary page of MSINFO32.exe. Possible values are: deviceDefault, blockAll, allowAll.

Parameters.Name: FirewallBlockStatefulFTP
Parameters.Description: Blocks stateful FTP connections to the device

Parameters.Name: FirewallCertificateRevocationListCheckMethod
Parameters.Description: Specify how the certificate revocation list is to be enforced. Possible values are: deviceDefault, none, attempt, require.

Parameters.Name: FirewallIdleTimeoutForSecurityAssociationInSeconds
Parameters.Description: Configures the idle timeout for security associations, in seconds, from 300 to 3600 inclusive. This is the period after which security associations will expire and be deleted. Valid values 300 to 3600

Parameters.Name: FirewallIPSecExemptionsAllowDHCP
Parameters.Description: Configures IPSec exemptions to allow both IPv4 and IPv6 DHCP traffic

Parameters.Name: FirewallIPSecExemptionsAllowICMP
Parameters.Description: Configures IPSec exemptions to allow ICMP

Parameters.Name: FirewallIPSecExemptionsAllowNeighborDiscovery
Parameters.Description: Configures IPSec exemptions to allow neighbor discovery IPv6 ICMP type-codes

Parameters.Name: FirewallIPSecExemptionsAllowRouterDiscovery
Parameters.Description: Configures IPSec exemptions to allow router discovery IPv6 ICMP type-codes

Parameters.Name: FirewallIPSecExemptionsNone
Parameters.Description: Configures IPSec exemptions to no exemptions

Parameters.Name: FirewallMergeKeyingModuleSettings
Parameters.Description: If an authentication set is not fully supported by a keying module, direct the module to ignore only unsupported authentication suites rather than the entire set

Parameters.Name: FirewallPacketQueueingMethod
Parameters.Description: Configures how packet queueing should be applied in the tunnel gateway scenario. Possible values are: deviceDefault, disabled, queueInbound, queueOutbound, queueBoth.

Parameters.Name: FirewallPreSharedKeyEncodingMethod
Parameters.Description: Select the preshared key encoding to be used. Possible values are: deviceDefault, none, utF8.

Parameters.Name: FirewallProfileDomain
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall profile settings for domain networks

Parameters.Name: FirewallProfilePrivate
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall profile settings for private networks

Parameters.Name: FirewallProfilePublic
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall profile settings for public networks

Parameters.Name: FirewallRules
Parameters.Description: Configures the firewall rule settings. This collection can contain a maximum of 150 elements.

Parameters.Name: LanManagerAuthenticationLevel
Parameters.Description: This security setting determines which challenge/response authentication protocol is used for network logons. Possible values are: lmAndNltm, lmNtlmAndNtlmV2, lmAndNtlmOnly, lmAndNtlmV2, lmNtlmV2AndNotLm, lmNtlmV2AndNotLmOrNtm.

Parameters.Name: LanManagerWorkstationDisableInsecureGuestLogons
Parameters.Description: If enabled,the SMB client will allow insecure guest logons. If not configured, the SMB client will reject insecure guest logons.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsAdministratorAccountName
Parameters.Description: Define a different account name to be associated with the security identifier (SID) for the account 'Administrator'.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsAdministratorElevationPromptBehavior
Parameters.Description: Define the behavior of the elevation prompt for admins in Admin Approval Mode. Possible values are: notConfigured, elevateWithoutPrompting, promptForCredentialsOnTheSecureDesktop, promptForConsentOnTheSecureDesktop, promptForCredentials, promptForConsent, promptForConsentForNonWindowsBinaries.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsAllowAnonymousEnumerationOfSAMAccountsAndShares
Parameters.Description: This security setting determines whether to allows anonymous users to perform certain activities, such as enumerating the names of domain accounts and network shares.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsAllowPKU2UAuthenticationRequests
Parameters.Description: Block PKU2U authentication requests to this device to use online identities.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsAllowRemoteCallsToSecurityAccountsManager
Parameters.Description: Edit the default Security Descriptor Definition Language string to allow or deny users and groups to make remote calls to the SAM.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsAllowRemoteCallsToSecurityAccountsManagerHelperBool
Parameters.Description: UI helper boolean for LocalSecurityOptionsAllowRemoteCallsToSecurityAccountsManager entity

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsAllowSystemToBeShutDownWithoutHavingToLogOn
Parameters.Description: This security setting determines whether a computer can be shut down without having to log on to Windows.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsAllowUIAccessApplicationElevation
Parameters.Description: Allow UIAccess apps to prompt for elevation without using the secure desktop.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsAllowUIAccessApplicationsForSecureLocations
Parameters.Description: Allow UIAccess apps to prompt for elevation without using the secure desktop.Default is enabled

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsAllowUndockWithoutHavingToLogon
Parameters.Description: Prevent a portable computer from being undocked without having to log in.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsBlockMicrosoftAccounts
Parameters.Description: Prevent users from adding new Microsoft accounts to this computer.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsBlockRemoteLogonWithBlankPassword
Parameters.Description: Enable Local accounts that are not password protected to log on from locations other than the physical device.Default is enabled

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsBlockRemoteOpticalDriveAccess
Parameters.Description: Enabling this settings allows only interactively logged on user to access CD-ROM media.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsBlockUsersInstallingPrinterDrivers
Parameters.Description: Restrict installing printer drivers as part of connecting to a shared printer to admins only.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsClearVirtualMemoryPageFile
Parameters.Description: This security setting determines whether the virtual memory pagefile is cleared when the system is shut down.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsClientDigitallySignCommunicationsAlways
Parameters.Description: This security setting determines whether packet signing is required by the SMB client component.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsClientSendUnencryptedPasswordToThirdPartySMBServers
Parameters.Description: If this security setting is enabled, the Server Message Block (SMB) redirector is allowed to send plaintext passwords to non-Microsoft SMB servers that do not support password encryption during authentication.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsDetectApplicationInstallationsAndPromptForElevation
Parameters.Description: App installations requiring elevated privileges will prompt for admin credentials.Default is enabled

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsDisableAdministratorAccount
Parameters.Description: Determines whether the Local Administrator account is enabled or disabled.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsDisableClientDigitallySignCommunicationsIfServerAgrees
Parameters.Description: This security setting determines whether the SMB client attempts to negotiate SMB packet signing.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsDisableGuestAccount
Parameters.Description: Determines if the Guest account is enabled or disabled.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsDisableServerDigitallySignCommunicationsAlways
Parameters.Description: This security setting determines whether packet signing is required by the SMB server component.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsDisableServerDigitallySignCommunicationsIfClientAgrees
Parameters.Description: This security setting determines whether the SMB server will negotiate SMB packet signing with clients that request it.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsDoNotAllowAnonymousEnumerationOfSAMAccounts
Parameters.Description: This security setting determines what additional permissions will be granted for anonymous connections to the computer.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsDoNotRequireCtrlAltDel
Parameters.Description: Require CTRL+ALT+DEL to be pressed before a user can log on.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsDoNotStoreLANManagerHashValueOnNextPasswordChange
Parameters.Description: This security setting determines if, at the next password change, the LAN Manager (LM) hash value for the new password is stored. Its not stored by default.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsFormatAndEjectOfRemovableMediaAllowedUser
Parameters.Description: Define who is allowed to format and eject removable NTFS media. Possible values are: notConfigured, administrators, administratorsAndPowerUsers, administratorsAndInteractiveUsers.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsGuestAccountName
Parameters.Description: Define a different account name to be associated with the security identifier (SID) for the account 'Guest'.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsHideLastSignedInUser
Parameters.Description: Do not display the username of the last person who signed in on this device.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsHideUsernameAtSignIn
Parameters.Description: Do not display the username of the person signing in to this device after credentials are entered and before the devices desktop is shown.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsInformationDisplayedOnLockScreen
Parameters.Description: Configure the user information that is displayed when the session is locked. If not configured, user display name, domain and username are shown. Possible values are: notConfigured, administrators, administratorsAndPowerUsers, administratorsAndInteractiveUsers.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsInformationShownOnLockScreen
Parameters.Description: Configure the user information that is displayed when the session is locked. If not configured, user display name, domain and username are shown. Possible values are: notConfigured, userDisplayNameDomainUser, userDisplayNameOnly, doNotDisplayUser.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsLogOnMessageText
Parameters.Description: Set message text for users attempting to log in.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsLogOnMessageTitle
Parameters.Description: Set message title for users attempting to log in.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsMachineInactivityLimit
Parameters.Description: Define maximum minutes of inactivity on the interactive desktops login screen until the screen saver runs. Valid values 0 to 9999

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsMachineInactivityLimitInMinutes
Parameters.Description: Define maximum minutes of inactivity on the interactive desktops login screen until the screen saver runs. Valid values 0 to 9999

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsMinimumSessionSecurityForNtlmSspBasedClients
Parameters.Description: This security setting allows a client to require the negotiation of 128-bit encryption and/or NTLMv2 session security. Possible values are: none, requireNtmlV2SessionSecurity, require128BitEncryption, ntlmV2And128BitEncryption.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsMinimumSessionSecurityForNtlmSspBasedServers
Parameters.Description: This security setting allows a server to require the negotiation of 128-bit encryption and/or NTLMv2 session security. Possible values are: none, requireNtmlV2SessionSecurity, require128BitEncryption, ntlmV2And128BitEncryption.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsOnlyElevateSignedExecutables
Parameters.Description: Enforce PKI certification path validation for a given executable file before it is permitted to run.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsRestrictAnonymousAccessToNamedPipesAndShares
Parameters.Description: By default, this security setting restricts anonymous access to shares and pipes to the settings for named pipes that can be accessed anonymously and Shares that can be accessed anonymously

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsSmartCardRemovalBehavior
Parameters.Description: This security setting determines what happens when the smart card for a logged-on user is removed from the smart card reader. Possible values are: noAction, lockWorkstation, forceLogoff, disconnectRemoteDesktopSession.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsStandardUserElevationPromptBehavior
Parameters.Description: Define the behavior of the elevation prompt for standard users. Possible values are: notConfigured, automaticallyDenyElevationRequests, promptForCredentialsOnTheSecureDesktop, promptForCredentials.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsSwitchToSecureDesktopWhenPromptingForElevation
Parameters.Description: Enable all elevation requests to go to the interactive user's desktop rather than the secure desktop. Prompt behavior policy settings for admins and standard users are used.

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsUseAdminApprovalMode
Parameters.Description: Defines whether the built-in admin account uses Admin Approval Mode or runs all apps with full admin privileges.Default is enabled

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsUseAdminApprovalModeForAdministrators
Parameters.Description: Define whether Admin Approval Mode and all UAC policy settings are enabled, default is enabled

Parameters.Name: LocalSecurityOptionsVirtualizeFileAndRegistryWriteFailuresToPerUserLocations
Parameters.Description: Virtualize file and registry write failures to per user locations

Parameters.Name: SmartScreenBlockOverrideForFiles
Parameters.Description: Allows IT Admins to control whether users can can ignore SmartScreen warnings and run malicious files.

Parameters.Name: SmartScreenEnableInShell
Parameters.Description: Allows IT Admins to configure SmartScreen for Windows.

Parameters.Name: UserRightsAccessCredentialManagerAsTrustedCaller
Parameters.Description: This user right is used by Credential Manager during Backup/Restore. Users' saved credentials might be compromised if this privilege is given to other entities. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

Parameters.Name: UserRightsActAsPartOfTheOperatingSystem
Parameters.Description: This user right allows a process to impersonate any user without authentication. The process can therefore gain access to the same local resources as that user. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

Parameters.Name: UserRightsAllowAccessFromNetwork
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users and groups are allowed to connect to the computer over the network. State Allowed is supported.

Parameters.Name: UserRightsBackupData
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users can bypass file, directory, registry, and other persistent objects permissions when backing up files and directories. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

Parameters.Name: UserRightsBlockAccessFromNetwork
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users and groups are block from connecting to the computer over the network. State Block is supported.

Parameters.Name: UserRightsChangeSystemTime
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users and groups can change the time and date on the internal clock of the computer. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

Parameters.Name: UserRightsCreateGlobalObjects
Parameters.Description: This security setting determines whether users can create global objects that are available to all sessions. Users who can create global objects could affect processes that run under other users' sessions, which could lead to application failure or data corruption. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

Parameters.Name: UserRightsCreatePageFile
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users and groups can call an internal API to create and change the size of a page file. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

Parameters.Name: UserRightsCreatePermanentSharedObjects
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which accounts can be used by processes to create a directory object using the object manager. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

Parameters.Name: UserRightsCreateSymbolicLinks
Parameters.Description: This user right determines if the user can create a symbolic link from the computer to which they are logged on. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

Parameters.Name: UserRightsCreateToken
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users/groups can be used by processes to create a token that can then be used to get access to any local resources when the process uses an internal API to create an access token. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

Parameters.Name: UserRightsDebugPrograms
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users can attach a debugger to any process or to the kernel. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

Parameters.Name: UserRightsDelegation
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users can set the Trusted for Delegation setting on a user or computer object. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

Parameters.Name: UserRightsDenyLocalLogOn
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users cannot log on to the computer. States NotConfigured, Blocked are supported

Parameters.Name: UserRightsGenerateSecurityAudits
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which accounts can be used by a process to add entries to the security log. The security log is used to trace unauthorized system access.  Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

Parameters.Name: UserRightsImpersonateClient
Parameters.Description: Assigning this user right to a user allows programs running on behalf of that user to impersonate a client. Requiring this user right for this kind of impersonation prevents an unauthorized user from convincing a client to connect to a service that they have created and then impersonating that client, which can elevate the unauthorized user's permissions to administrative or system levels. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

Parameters.Name: UserRightsIncreaseSchedulingPriority
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which accounts can use a process with Write Property access to another process to increase the execution priority assigned to the other process. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

Parameters.Name: UserRightsLoadUnloadDrivers
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users can dynamically load and unload device drivers or other code in to kernel mode. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

Parameters.Name: UserRightsLocalLogOn
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users can log on to the computer. States NotConfigured, Allowed are supported

Parameters.Name: UserRightsLockMemory
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which accounts can use a process to keep data in physical memory, which prevents the system from paging the data to virtual memory on disk. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

Parameters.Name: UserRightsManageAuditingAndSecurityLogs
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users can specify object access auditing options for individual resources, such as files, Active Directory objects, and registry keys. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

Parameters.Name: UserRightsManageVolumes
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users and groups can run maintenance tasks on a volume, such as remote defragmentation. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

Parameters.Name: UserRightsModifyFirmwareEnvironment
Parameters.Description: This user right determines who can modify firmware environment values. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

Parameters.Name: UserRightsModifyObjectLabels
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which user accounts can modify the integrity label of objects, such as files, registry keys, or processes owned by other users. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

Parameters.Name: UserRightsProfileSingleProcess
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users can use performance monitoring tools to monitor the performance of system processes. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

Parameters.Name: UserRightsRemoteDesktopServicesLogOn
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users and groups are prohibited from logging on as a Remote Desktop Services client. Only states NotConfigured and Blocked are supported

Parameters.Name: UserRightsRemoteShutdown
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users are allowed to shut down a computer from a remote location on the network. Misuse of this user right can result in a denial of service. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

Parameters.Name: UserRightsRestoreData
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users can bypass file, directory, registry, and other persistent objects permissions when restoring backed up files and directories, and determines which users can set any valid security principal as the owner of an object. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

Parameters.Name: UserRightsTakeOwnership
Parameters.Description: This user right determines which users can take ownership of any securable object in the system, including Active Directory objects, files and folders, printers, registry keys, processes, and threads. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

Parameters.Name: WindowsDefenderTamperProtection
Parameters.Description: Configure windows defender TamperProtection settings. Possible values are: notConfigured, enable, disable.

Parameters.Name: XboxServicesAccessoryManagementServiceStartupMode
Parameters.Description: This setting determines whether the Accessory management service's start type is Automatic(2), Manual(3), Disabled(4). Default: Manual. Possible values are: manual, automatic, disabled.

Parameters.Name: XboxServicesEnableXboxGameSaveTask
Parameters.Description: This setting determines whether xbox game save is enabled (1) or disabled (0).

Parameters.Name: XboxServicesLiveAuthManagerServiceStartupMode
Parameters.Description: This setting determines whether Live Auth Manager service's start type is Automatic(2), Manual(3), Disabled(4). Default: Manual. Possible values are: manual, automatic, disabled.

Parameters.Name: XboxServicesLiveGameSaveServiceStartupMode
Parameters.Description: This setting determines whether Live Game save service's start type is Automatic(2), Manual(3), Disabled(4). Default: Manual. Possible values are: manual, automatic, disabled.

Parameters.Name: XboxServicesLiveNetworkingServiceStartupMode
Parameters.Description: This setting determines whether Networking service's start type is Automatic(2), Manual(3), Disabled(4). Default: Manual. Possible values are: manual, automatic, disabled.

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

Parameters.Name: SupportsScopeTags
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not the underlying Device Configuration supports the assignment of scope tags. Assigning to the ScopeTags property is not allowed when this value is false and entities will not be visible to scoped users. This occurs for Legacy policies created in Silverlight and can be resolved by deleting and recreating the policy in the Azure Portal. This property is read-only.

Parameters.Name: Id
Parameters.Description: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

Parameters.Name: RoleScopeTagIds
Parameters.Description: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

Parameters.Name: Assignments
Parameters.Description: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Present ensures the policy exists, absent ensures it is removed.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Admin

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ApplicationSecret
Parameters.Description: Secret of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity
Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Description: Intune Device Configuration Endpoint Protection Policy for Windows10

Parameters.Name: Bluetooth
Parameters.Description: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Bluetooth. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: BootFromBuiltInNetworkAdapters
Parameters.Description: Defines whether a user is allowed to boot from built-in network adapters. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: BootFromExternalMedia
Parameters.Description: Defines whether a user is allowed to boot from external media. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: Cameras
Parameters.Description: Defines whether built-in cameras are enabled. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: ChangeUefiSettingsPermission
Parameters.Description: Defines the permission level granted to users to change UEFI settings. Possible values are: notConfiguredOnly, none.

Parameters.Name: FrontCamera
Parameters.Description: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Front Camera. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: InfraredCamera
Parameters.Description: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Infrared camera. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: Microphone
Parameters.Description: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Microphone. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: MicrophonesAndSpeakers
Parameters.Description: Defines whether built-in microphones or speakers are enabled. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: NearFieldCommunication
Parameters.Description: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Near Field Communication. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: Radios
Parameters.Description: Defines whether built-in radios e.g. WIFI, NFC, Bluetooth, are enabled. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: RearCamera
Parameters.Description: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable rear camera. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: SdCard
Parameters.Description: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable SD Card Port. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: SimultaneousMultiThreading
Parameters.Description: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Simultaneous MultiThreading. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: UsbTypeAPort
Parameters.Description: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable USB Type A Port. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: VirtualizationOfCpuAndIO
Parameters.Description: Defines whether CPU and IO virtualization is enabled. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: WakeOnLAN
Parameters.Description: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Wake on LAN. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: WakeOnPower
Parameters.Description: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Wake On Power. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: WiFi
Parameters.Description: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable WiFi. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: WindowsPlatformBinaryTable
Parameters.Description: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Windows Platform Binary Table. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: WirelessWideAreaNetwork
Parameters.Description: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Wireless Wide Area Network. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: EnhancedAntiSpoofingForFacialFeaturesEnabled
Parameters.Description: Boolean value used to enable enhanced anti-spoofing for facial feature recognition on Windows Hello face authentication.

Parameters.Name: PinExpirationInDays
Parameters.Description: Integer value specifies the period (in days) that a PIN can be used before the system requires the user to change it. Valid values are 0 to 730 inclusive. Valid values 0 to 730

Parameters.Name: PinLowercaseCharactersUsage
Parameters.Description: This value configures the use of lowercase characters in the Windows Hello for Business PIN. Possible values are: blocked, required, allowed, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: PinMaximumLength
Parameters.Description: Integer value that sets the maximum number of characters allowed for the work PIN. Valid values are 4 to 127 inclusive and greater than or equal to the value set for the minimum PIN. Valid values 4 to 127

Parameters.Name: PinMinimumLength
Parameters.Description: Integer value that sets the minimum number of characters required for the Windows Hello for Business PIN. Valid values are 4 to 127 inclusive and less than or equal to the value set for the maximum PIN. Valid values 4 to 127

Parameters.Name: PinPreviousBlockCount
Parameters.Description: Controls the ability to prevent users from using past PINs. This must be set between 0 and 50, inclusive, and the current PIN of the user is included in that count. If set to 0, previous PINs are not stored. PIN history is not preserved through a PIN reset. Valid values 0 to 50

Parameters.Name: PinRecoveryEnabled
Parameters.Description: Boolean value that enables a user to change their PIN by using the Windows Hello for Business PIN recovery service.

Parameters.Name: PinSpecialCharactersUsage
Parameters.Description: Controls the ability to use special characters in the Windows Hello for Business PIN. Possible values are: blocked, required, allowed, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: PinUppercaseCharactersUsage
Parameters.Description: This value configures the use of uppercase characters in the Windows Hello for Business PIN. Possible values are: blocked, required, allowed, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: SecurityDeviceRequired
Parameters.Description: Controls whether to require a Trusted Platform Module (TPM) for provisioning Windows Hello for Business. A TPM provides an additional security benefit in that data stored on it cannot be used on other devices. If set to False, all devices can provision Windows Hello for Business even if there is not a usable TPM.

Parameters.Name: UnlockWithBiometricsEnabled
Parameters.Description: Controls the use of biometric gestures, such as face and fingerprint, as an alternative to the Windows Hello for Business PIN.  If set to False, biometric gestures are not allowed. Users must still configure a PIN as a backup in case of failures.

Parameters.Name: UseCertificatesForOnPremisesAuthEnabled
Parameters.Description: Boolean value that enables Windows Hello for Business to use certificates to authenticate on-premise resources.

Parameters.Name: UseSecurityKeyForSignin
Parameters.Description: Boolean value used to enable the Windows Hello security key as a logon credential.

Parameters.Name: WindowsHelloForBusinessBlocked
Parameters.Description: Boolean value that blocks Windows Hello for Business as a method for signing into Windows.

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseCloudResources
Parameters.Description: Contains a list of enterprise resource domains hosted in the cloud that need to be protected. Connections to these resources are considered enterprise data. If a proxy is paired with a cloud resource, traffic to the cloud resource will be routed through the enterprise network via the denoted proxy server (on Port 80). A proxy server used for this purpose must also be configured using the EnterpriseInternalProxyServers policy. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseInternalProxyServers
Parameters.Description: This is the comma-separated list of internal proxy servers. For example, '157.54.14.28, 157.54.11.118, 10.202.14.167, 157.53.14.163, 157.69.210.59'. These proxies have been configured by the admin to connect to specific resources on the Internet. They are considered to be enterprise network locations. The proxies are only leveraged in configuring the EnterpriseCloudResources policy to force traffic to the matched cloud resources through these proxies.

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseIPRanges
Parameters.Description: Sets the enterprise IP ranges that define the computers in the enterprise network. Data that comes from those computers will be considered part of the enterprise and protected. These locations will be considered a safe destination for enterprise data to be shared to. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseIPRangesAreAuthoritative
Parameters.Description: Boolean value that tells the client to accept the configured list and not to use heuristics to attempt to find other subnets. Default is false.

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseNetworkDomainNames
Parameters.Description: This is the list of domains that comprise the boundaries of the enterprise. Data from one of these domains that is sent to a device will be considered enterprise data and protected. These locations will be considered a safe destination for enterprise data to be shared to.

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseProxyServers
Parameters.Description: This is a list of proxy servers. Any server not on this list is considered non-enterprise.

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseProxyServersAreAuthoritative
Parameters.Description: Boolean value that tells the client to accept the configured list of proxies and not try to detect other work proxies. Default is false

Parameters.Name: NeutralDomainResources
Parameters.Description: List of domain names that can used for work or personal resource.

Description: This resource configures the settings of Android Device Administrator device restriction policy in your cloud-based organization.

Parameters.Name: Id
Parameters.Description: Id of the Intune policy.

Parameters.Description: Display name of the Intune policy.

Parameters.Description: Description of the Intune policy.
