Parameters.Description: If true, runs the task immediately if StartDateTime is in the past, else, runs at the next recurrence.

Parameters.Name: StartDateTime
Parameters.Description: The start time for the force restart.

Description: Intune Device Configuration Policy for Windows10

Parameters.Name: AccountsBlockAddingNonMicrosoftAccountEmail
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from adding email accounts to the device that are not associated with a Microsoft account.

Parameters.Name: ActivateAppsWithVoice
Parameters.Description: Specifies if Windows apps can be activated by voice. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: AntiTheftModeBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block the user from selecting an AntiTheft mode preference (Windows 10 Mobile only).

Parameters.Name: AppManagementMSIAllowUserControlOverInstall
Parameters.Description: This policy setting permits users to change installation options that typically are available only to system administrators.

Parameters.Name: AppManagementMSIAlwaysInstallWithElevatedPrivileges
Parameters.Description: This policy setting directs Windows Installer to use elevated permissions when it installs any program on the system.

Parameters.Name: AppManagementPackageFamilyNamesToLaunchAfterLogOn
Parameters.Description: List of semi-colon delimited Package Family Names of Windows apps. Listed Windows apps are to be launched after logon.

Parameters.Name: AppsAllowTrustedAppsSideloading
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether apps from AppX packages signed with a trusted certificate can be side loaded. Possible values are: notConfigured, blocked, allowed.

Parameters.Name: AppsBlockWindowsStoreOriginatedApps
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to disable the launch of all apps from Windows Store that came pre-installed or were downloaded.

Parameters.Name: AuthenticationAllowSecondaryDevice
Parameters.Description: Allows secondary authentication devices to work with Windows.

Parameters.Name: AuthenticationPreferredAzureADTenantDomainName
Parameters.Description: Specifies the preferred domain among available domains in the Azure AD tenant.

Parameters.Name: AuthenticationWebSignIn
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not Web Credential Provider will be enabled. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: BluetoothAllowedServices
Parameters.Description: Specify a list of allowed Bluetooth services and profiles in hex formatted strings.

Parameters.Name: BluetoothBlockAdvertising
Parameters.Description: Whether or not to Block the user from using bluetooth advertising.

Parameters.Name: BluetoothBlockDiscoverableMode
Parameters.Description: Whether or not to Block the user from using bluetooth discoverable mode.

Parameters.Name: BluetoothBlocked
Parameters.Description: Whether or not to Block the user from using bluetooth.

Parameters.Name: BluetoothBlockPrePairing
Parameters.Description: Whether or not to block specific bundled Bluetooth peripherals to automatically pair with the host device.

Parameters.Name: BluetoothBlockPromptedProximalConnections
Parameters.Description: Whether or not to block the users from using Swift Pair and other proximity based scenarios.

Parameters.Name: CameraBlocked
Parameters.Description: Whether or not to Block the user from accessing the camera of the device.

Parameters.Name: CellularBlockDataWhenRoaming
Parameters.Description: Whether or not to Block the user from using data over cellular while roaming.

Parameters.Name: CellularBlockVpn
Parameters.Description: Whether or not to Block the user from using VPN over cellular.

Parameters.Name: CellularBlockVpnWhenRoaming
Parameters.Description: Whether or not to Block the user from using VPN when roaming over cellular.

Parameters.Name: CellularData
Parameters.Description: Whether or not to allow the cellular data channel on the device. If not configured, the cellular data channel is allowed and the user can turn it off. Possible values are: blocked, required, allowed, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: CertificatesBlockManualRootCertificateInstallation
Parameters.Description: Whether or not to Block the user from doing manual root certificate installation.

Parameters.Name: ConfigureTimeZone
Parameters.Description: Specifies the time zone to be applied to the device. This is the standard Windows name for the target time zone.

Parameters.Name: ConnectedDevicesServiceBlocked
Parameters.Description: Whether or not to block Connected Devices Service which enables discovery and connection to other devices, remote messaging, remote app sessions and other cross-device experiences.

Parameters.Name: CopyPasteBlocked
Parameters.Description: Whether or not to Block the user from using copy paste.

Parameters.Name: CortanaBlocked
Parameters.Description: Whether or not to Block the user from using Cortana.

Parameters.Name: CryptographyAllowFipsAlgorithmPolicy
Parameters.Description: Specify whether to allow or disallow the Federal Information Processing Standard (FIPS) policy.

Parameters.Name: DataProtectionBlockDirectMemoryAccess
Parameters.Description: This policy setting allows you to block direct memory access (DMA) for all hot pluggable PCI downstream ports until a user logs into Windows.

Parameters.Name: DefenderBlockEndUserAccess
Parameters.Description: Whether or not to block end user access to Defender.

Parameters.Name: DefenderBlockOnAccessProtection
Parameters.Description: Allows or disallows Windows Defender On Access Protection functionality.

Parameters.Name: DefenderCloudBlockLevel
Parameters.Description: Specifies the level of cloud-delivered protection. Possible values are: notConfigured, high, highPlus, zeroTolerance.

Parameters.Name: DefenderCloudExtendedTimeout
Parameters.Description: Timeout extension for file scanning by the cloud. Valid values 0 to 50

Parameters.Name: DefenderCloudExtendedTimeoutInSeconds
Parameters.Description: Timeout extension for file scanning by the cloud. Valid values 0 to 50

Parameters.Name: DefenderDaysBeforeDeletingQuarantinedMalware
Parameters.Description: Number of days before deleting quarantined malware. Valid values 0 to 90

Parameters.Name: DefenderDetectedMalwareActions
Parameters.Description: Gets or sets Defenders actions to take on detected Malware per threat level.

Parameters.Name: DefenderDisableCatchupFullScan
Parameters.Description: When blocked, catch-up scans for scheduled full scans will be turned off.

Parameters.Name: DefenderDisableCatchupQuickScan
Parameters.Description: When blocked, catch-up scans for scheduled quick scans will be turned off.

Parameters.Name: DefenderFileExtensionsToExclude
Parameters.Description: File extensions to exclude from scans and real time protection.

Parameters.Name: DefenderFilesAndFoldersToExclude
Parameters.Description: Files and folder to exclude from scans and real time protection.

Parameters.Name: DefenderMonitorFileActivity
Parameters.Description: Value for monitoring file activity. Possible values are: userDefined, disable, monitorAllFiles, monitorIncomingFilesOnly, monitorOutgoingFilesOnly.

Parameters.Name: DefenderPotentiallyUnwantedAppAction
Parameters.Description: Gets or sets Defenders action to take on Potentially Unwanted Application (PUA), which includes software with behaviors of ad-injection, software bundling, persistent solicitation for payment or subscription, etc. Defender alerts user when PUA is being downloaded or attempts to install itself. Added in Windows 10 for desktop. Possible values are: deviceDefault, block, audit.

Parameters.Name: DefenderPotentiallyUnwantedAppActionSetting
Parameters.Description: Gets or sets Defenders action to take on Potentially Unwanted Application (PUA), which includes software with behaviors of ad-injection, software bundling, persistent solicitation for payment or subscription, etc. Defender alerts user when PUA is being downloaded or attempts to install itself. Added in Windows 10 for desktop. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

Parameters.Name: DefenderProcessesToExclude
Parameters.Description: Processes to exclude from scans and real time protection.

Parameters.Name: DefenderPromptForSampleSubmission
Parameters.Description: The configuration for how to prompt user for sample submission. Possible values are: userDefined, alwaysPrompt, promptBeforeSendingPersonalData, neverSendData, sendAllDataWithoutPrompting.

Parameters.Name: DefenderRequireBehaviorMonitoring
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to require behavior monitoring.

Parameters.Name: DefenderRequireCloudProtection
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to require cloud protection.

Parameters.Name: DefenderRequireNetworkInspectionSystem
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to require network inspection system.

Parameters.Name: DefenderRequireRealTimeMonitoring
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to require real time monitoring.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScanArchiveFiles
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to scan archive files.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScanDownloads
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to scan downloads.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScanIncomingMail
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to scan incoming mail messages.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScanMappedNetworkDrivesDuringFullScan
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to scan mapped network drives during full scan.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScanMaxCpu
Parameters.Description: Max CPU usage percentage during scan. Valid values 0 to 100

Parameters.Name: DefenderScanNetworkFiles
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to scan files opened from a network folder.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScanRemovableDrivesDuringFullScan
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to scan removable drives during full scan.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScanScriptsLoadedInInternetExplorer
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to scan scripts loaded in Internet Explorer browser.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScanType
Parameters.Description: The defender system scan type. Possible values are: userDefined, disabled, quick, full.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScheduledQuickScanTime
Parameters.Description: The time to perform a daily quick scan.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScheduledScanTime
Parameters.Description: The defender time for the system scan.

Parameters.Name: DefenderScheduleScanEnableLowCpuPriority
Parameters.Description: When enabled, low CPU priority will be used during scheduled scans.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSignatureUpdateIntervalInHours
Parameters.Description: The signature update interval in hours. Specify 0 not to check. Valid values 0 to 24

Parameters.Name: DefenderSubmitSamplesConsentType
Parameters.Description: Checks for the user consent level in Windows Defender to send data. Possible values are: sendSafeSamplesAutomatically, alwaysPrompt, neverSend, sendAllSamplesAutomatically.

Parameters.Name: DefenderSystemScanSchedule
Parameters.Description: Defender day of the week for the system scan. Possible values are: userDefined, everyday, sunday, monday, tuesday, wednesday, thursday, friday, saturday, noScheduledScan.

Parameters.Name: DeveloperUnlockSetting
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to allow developer unlock. Possible values are: notConfigured, blocked, allowed.

Parameters.Name: DeviceManagementBlockFactoryResetOnMobile
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from resetting their phone.

Parameters.Name: DeviceManagementBlockManualUnenroll
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from doing manual un-enrollment from device management.

Parameters.Name: DiagnosticsDataSubmissionMode
Parameters.Description: Gets or sets a value allowing the device to send diagnostic and usage telemetry data, such as Watson. Possible values are: userDefined, none, basic, enhanced, full.

Parameters.Name: DisplayAppListWithGdiDPIScalingTurnedOff
Parameters.Description: List of legacy applications that have GDI DPI Scaling turned off.

Parameters.Name: DisplayAppListWithGdiDPIScalingTurnedOn
Parameters.Description: List of legacy applications that have GDI DPI Scaling turned on.

Parameters.Name: EdgeAllowStartPagesModification
Parameters.Description: Allow users to change Start pages on Edge. Use the EdgeHomepageUrls to specify the Start pages that the user would see by default when they open Edge.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockAccessToAboutFlags
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to prevent access to about flags on Edge browser.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockAddressBarDropdown
Parameters.Description: Block the address bar dropdown functionality in Microsoft Edge. Disable this settings to minimize network connections from Microsoft Edge to Microsoft services.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockAutofill
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block auto fill.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockCompatibilityList
Parameters.Description: Block Microsoft compatibility list in Microsoft Edge. This list from Microsoft helps Edge properly display sites with known compatibility issues.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockDeveloperTools
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block developer tools in the Edge browser.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from using the Edge browser.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockEditFavorites
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from making changes to Favorites.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockExtensions
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block extensions in the Edge browser.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockFullScreenMode
Parameters.Description: Allow or prevent Edge from entering the full screen mode.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockInPrivateBrowsing
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block InPrivate browsing on corporate networks, in the Edge browser.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockJavaScript
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from using JavaScript.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockLiveTileDataCollection
Parameters.Description: Block the collection of information by Microsoft for live tile creation when users pin a site to Start from Microsoft Edge.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockPasswordManager
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block password manager.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockPopups
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block popups.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockPrelaunch
Parameters.Description: Decide whether Microsoft Edge is prelaunched at Windows startup.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockPrinting
Parameters.Description: Configure Edge to allow or block printing.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockSavingHistory
Parameters.Description: Configure Edge to allow browsing history to be saved or to never save browsing history.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockSearchEngineCustomization
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block the user from adding new search engine or changing the default search engine.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockSearchSuggestions
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block the user from using the search suggestions in the address bar.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockSendingDoNotTrackHeader
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from sending the do not track header.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockSendingIntranetTrafficToInternetExplorer
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to switch the intranet traffic from Edge to Internet Explorer. Note: the name of this property is misleading the property is obsolete, use EdgeSendIntranetTrafficToInternetExplorer instead.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockSideloadingExtensions
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether the user can sideload extensions.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockTabPreloading
Parameters.Description: Configure whether Edge preloads the new tab page at Windows startup.

Parameters.Name: EdgeBlockWebContentOnNewTabPage
Parameters.Description: Configure to load a blank page in Edge instead of the default New tab page and prevent users from changing it.

Parameters.Name: EdgeClearBrowsingDataOnExit
Parameters.Description: Clear browsing data on exiting Microsoft Edge.

Parameters.Name: EdgeCookiePolicy
Parameters.Description: Indicates which cookies to block in the Edge browser. Possible values are: userDefined, allow, blockThirdParty, blockAll.

Parameters.Name: EdgeDisableFirstRunPage
Parameters.Description: Block the Microsoft web page that opens on the first use of Microsoft Edge. This policy allows enterprises, like those enrolled in zero emissions configurations, to block this page.

Parameters.Name: EdgeEnterpriseModeSiteListLocation
Parameters.Description: Indicates the enterprise mode site list location. Could be a local file, local network or http location.

Parameters.Name: EdgeFavoritesBarVisibility
Parameters.Description: Get or set a value that specifies whether to set the favorites bar to always be visible or hidden on any page. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

Parameters.Name: EdgeFavoritesListLocation
Parameters.Description: The location of the favorites list to provision. Could be a local file, local network or http location.

Parameters.Name: EdgeFirstRunUrl
Parameters.Description: The first run URL for when Edge browser is opened for the first time.

Parameters.Name: EdgeHomeButtonConfiguration
Parameters.Description: Causes the Home button to either hide, load the default Start page, load a New tab page, or a custom URL

Parameters.Name: EdgeHomeButtonConfigurationEnabled
Parameters.Description: Enable the Home button configuration.

Parameters.Name: EdgeHomepageUrls
Parameters.Description: The list of URLs for homepages shodwn on MDM-enrolled devices on Edge browser.

Parameters.Name: EdgeKioskModeRestriction
Parameters.Description: Controls how the Microsoft Edge settings are restricted based on the configure kiosk mode. Possible values are: notConfigured, digitalSignage, normalMode, publicBrowsingSingleApp, publicBrowsingMultiApp.

Parameters.Name: EdgeKioskResetAfterIdleTimeInMinutes
Parameters.Description: Specifies the time in minutes from the last user activity before Microsoft Edge kiosk resets.  Valid values are 0-1440. The default is 5. 0 indicates no reset. Valid values 0 to 1440

Parameters.Name: EdgeNewTabPageURL
Parameters.Description: Specify the page opened when new tabs are created.

Parameters.Name: EdgeOpensWith
Parameters.Description: Specify what kind of pages are open at start. Possible values are: notConfigured, startPage, newTabPage, previousPages, specificPages.

Parameters.Name: EdgePreventCertificateErrorOverride
Parameters.Description: Allow or prevent users from overriding certificate errors.

Parameters.Name: EdgeRequiredExtensionPackageFamilyNames
Parameters.Description: Specify the list of package family names of browser extensions that are required and cannot be turned off by the user.

Parameters.Name: EdgeRequireSmartScreen
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Require the user to use the smart screen filter.

Parameters.Name: EdgeSearchEngine
Parameters.Description: Allows IT admins to set a default search engine for MDM-Controlled devices. Users can override this and change their default search engine provided the AllowSearchEngineCustomization policy is not set.

Parameters.Name: EdgeSendIntranetTrafficToInternetExplorer
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to switch the intranet traffic from Edge to Internet Explorer.

Parameters.Name: EdgeShowMessageWhenOpeningInternetExplorerSites
Parameters.Description: Controls the message displayed by Edge before switching to Internet Explorer. Possible values are: notConfigured, disabled, enabled, keepGoing.

Parameters.Name: EdgeSyncFavoritesWithInternetExplorer
Parameters.Description: Enable favorites sync between Internet Explorer and Microsoft Edge. Additions, deletions, modifications and order changes to favorites are shared between browsers.

Parameters.Name: EdgeTelemetryForMicrosoft365Analytics
Parameters.Description: Specifies what type of telemetry data (none, intranet, internet, both) is sent to Microsoft 365 Analytics. Possible values are: notConfigured, intranet, internet, intranetAndInternet.

Parameters.Name: EnableAutomaticRedeployment
Parameters.Description: Allow users with administrative rights to delete all user data and settings using CTRL + Win + R at the device lock screen so that the device can be automatically re-configured and re-enrolled into management.

Parameters.Name: EnergySaverOnBatteryThresholdPercentage
Parameters.Description: This setting allows you to specify battery charge level at which Energy Saver is turned on. While on battery, Energy Saver is automatically turned on at (and below) the specified battery charge level. Valid input range (0-100). Valid values 0 to 100

Parameters.Name: EnergySaverPluggedInThresholdPercentage
Parameters.Description: This setting allows you to specify battery charge level at which Energy Saver is turned on. While plugged in, Energy Saver is automatically turned on at (and below) the specified battery charge level. Valid input range (0-100). Valid values 0 to 100

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseCloudPrintDiscoveryEndPoint
Parameters.Description: Endpoint for discovering cloud printers.

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseCloudPrintDiscoveryMaxLimit
Parameters.Description: Maximum number of printers that should be queried from a discovery endpoint. This is a mobile only setting. Valid values 1 to 65535

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseCloudPrintMopriaDiscoveryResourceIdentifier
Parameters.Description: OAuth resource URI for printer discovery service as configured in Azure portal.

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseCloudPrintOAuthAuthority
Parameters.Description: Authentication endpoint for acquiring OAuth tokens.

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseCloudPrintOAuthClientIdentifier
Parameters.Description: GUID of a client application authorized to retrieve OAuth tokens from the OAuth Authority.

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseCloudPrintResourceIdentifier
Parameters.Description: OAuth resource URI for print service as configured in the Azure portal.

Parameters.Name: ExperienceBlockDeviceDiscovery
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to enable device discovery UX.

Parameters.Name: ExperienceBlockErrorDialogWhenNoSIM
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to allow the error dialog from displaying if no SIM card is detected.

Parameters.Name: ExperienceBlockTaskSwitcher
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to enable task switching on the device.

Parameters.Name: ExperienceDoNotSyncBrowserSettings
Parameters.Description: Allow or prevent the syncing of Microsoft Edge Browser settings. Option for IT admins to prevent syncing across devices, but allow user override. Possible values are: notConfigured, blockedWithUserOverride, blocked.

Parameters.Name: FindMyFiles
Parameters.Description: Controls if the user can configure search to Find My Files mode, which searches files in secondary hard drives and also outside of the user profile. Find My Files does not allow users to search files or locations to which they do not have access. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: GameDvrBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block DVR and broadcasting.

Parameters.Name: InkWorkspaceAccess
Parameters.Description: Controls the user access to the ink workspace, from the desktop and from above the lock screen. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: InkWorkspaceAccessState
Parameters.Description: Controls the user access to the ink workspace, from the desktop and from above the lock screen. Possible values are: notConfigured, blocked, allowed.

Parameters.Name: InkWorkspaceBlockSuggestedApps
Parameters.Description: Specify whether to show recommended app suggestions in the ink workspace.

Parameters.Name: InternetSharingBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from using internet sharing.

Parameters.Name: LocationServicesBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from location services.

Parameters.Name: LockScreenActivateAppsWithVoice
Parameters.Description: This policy setting specifies whether Windows apps can be activated by voice while the system is locked. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: LockScreenAllowTimeoutConfiguration
Parameters.Description: Specify whether to show a user-configurable setting to control the screen timeout while on the lock screen of Windows 10 Mobile devices. If this policy is set to Allow, the value set by lockScreenTimeoutInSeconds is ignored.

Parameters.Name: LockScreenBlockActionCenterNotifications
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block action center notifications over lock screen.

Parameters.Name: LockScreenBlockCortana
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not the user can interact with Cortana using speech while the system is locked.

Parameters.Name: LockScreenBlockToastNotifications
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether to allow toast notifications above the device lock screen.

Parameters.Name: LockScreenTimeoutInSeconds
Parameters.Description: Set the duration (in seconds) from the screen locking to the screen turning off for Windows 10 Mobile devices. Supported values are 11-1800. Valid values 11 to 1800

Parameters.Name: LogonBlockFastUserSwitching
Parameters.Description: Disables the ability to quickly switch between users that are logged on simultaneously without logging off.

Parameters.Name: MessagingBlockMMS
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block the MMS send/receive functionality on the device.

Parameters.Name: MessagingBlockRichCommunicationServices
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block the RCS send/receive functionality on the device.

Parameters.Name: MessagingBlockSync
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block text message back up and restore and Messaging Everywhere.

Parameters.Name: MicrosoftAccountBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block a Microsoft account.

Parameters.Name: MicrosoftAccountBlockSettingsSync
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block Microsoft account settings sync.

Parameters.Name: MicrosoftAccountSignInAssistantSettings
Parameters.Description: Controls the Microsoft Account Sign-In Assistant (wlidsvc) NT service. Possible values are: notConfigured, disabled.

Parameters.Name: NetworkProxyApplySettingsDeviceWide
Parameters.Description: If set, proxy settings will be applied to all processes and accounts in the device. Otherwise, it will be applied to the user account that's enrolled into MDM.

Parameters.Name: NetworkProxyAutomaticConfigurationUrl
Parameters.Description: Address to the proxy auto-config (PAC) script you want to use.

Parameters.Name: NetworkProxyDisableAutoDetect
Parameters.Description: Disable automatic detection of settings. If enabled, the system will try to find the path to a proxy auto-config (PAC) script.

Parameters.Name: NetworkProxyServer
Parameters.Description: Specifies manual proxy server settings.

Parameters.Name: NfcBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from using near field communication.

Parameters.Name: OneDriveDisableFileSync
Parameters.Description: Gets or sets a value allowing IT admins to prevent apps and features from working with files on OneDrive.

Parameters.Name: PasswordBlockSimple
Parameters.Description: Specify whether PINs or passwords such as '1111' or '1234' are allowed. For Windows 10 desktops, it also controls the use of picture passwords.

Parameters.Name: PasswordExpirationDays
Parameters.Description: The password expiration in days. Valid values 0 to 730

Parameters.Name: PasswordMinimumAgeInDays
Parameters.Description: This security setting determines the period of time (in days) that a password must be used before the user can change it. Valid values 0 to 998

Parameters.Name: PasswordMinimumCharacterSetCount
Parameters.Description: The number of character sets required in the password.

Parameters.Name: PasswordMinimumLength
Parameters.Description: The minimum password length. Valid values 4 to 16

Parameters.Name: PasswordMinutesOfInactivityBeforeScreenTimeout
Parameters.Description: The minutes of inactivity before the screen times out.

Parameters.Name: PasswordPreviousPasswordBlockCount
Parameters.Description: The number of previous passwords to prevent reuse of. Valid values 0 to 50

Parameters.Name: PasswordRequired
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to require the user to have a password.

Parameters.Name: PasswordRequiredType
Parameters.Description: The required password type. Possible values are: deviceDefault, alphanumeric, numeric.

Parameters.Name: PasswordRequireWhenResumeFromIdleState
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to require a password upon resuming from an idle state.

Parameters.Name: PasswordSignInFailureCountBeforeFactoryReset
Parameters.Description: The number of sign in failures before factory reset. Valid values 0 to 999

Parameters.Name: PersonalizationDesktopImageUrl
Parameters.Description: A http or https Url to a jpg, jpeg or png image that needs to be downloaded and used as the Desktop Image or a file Url to a local image on the file system that needs to used as the Desktop Image.

Parameters.Name: PersonalizationLockScreenImageUrl
Parameters.Description: A http or https Url to a jpg, jpeg or png image that needs to be downloaded and used as the Lock Screen Image or a file Url to a local image on the file system that needs to be used as the Lock Screen Image.

Parameters.Name: PowerButtonActionOnBattery
Parameters.Description: This setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user presses the Power button while on battery. Possible values are: notConfigured, noAction, sleep, hibernate, shutdown.

Parameters.Name: PowerButtonActionPluggedIn
Parameters.Description: This setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user presses the Power button while plugged in. Possible values are: notConfigured, noAction, sleep, hibernate, shutdown.

Parameters.Name: PowerHybridSleepOnBattery
Parameters.Description: This setting allows you to turn off hybrid sleep while on battery. If you set this setting to disable, a hiberfile is not generated when the system transitions to sleep (Stand By). If you set this setting to enable or do not configure this policy setting, users control this setting. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: PowerHybridSleepPluggedIn
Parameters.Description: This setting allows you to turn off hybrid sleep while plugged in. If you set this setting to disable, a hiberfile is not generated when the system transitions to sleep (Stand By). If you set this setting to enable or do not configure this policy setting, users control this setting. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: PowerLidCloseActionOnBattery
Parameters.Description: This setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user closes the lid on a mobile PC while on battery. Possible values are: notConfigured, noAction, sleep, hibernate, shutdown.

Parameters.Name: PowerLidCloseActionPluggedIn
Parameters.Description: This setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user closes the lid on a mobile PC while plugged in. Possible values are: notConfigured, noAction, sleep, hibernate, shutdown.

Parameters.Name: PowerSleepButtonActionOnBattery
Parameters.Description: This setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user presses the Sleep button while on battery. Possible values are: notConfigured, noAction, sleep, hibernate, shutdown.

Parameters.Name: PowerSleepButtonActionPluggedIn
Parameters.Description: This setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user presses the Sleep button while plugged in. Possible values are: notConfigured, noAction, sleep, hibernate, shutdown.

Parameters.Name: PrinterBlockAddition
Parameters.Description: Prevent user installation of additional printers from printers settings.

Parameters.Name: PrinterDefaultName
Parameters.Description: Name (network host name) of an installed printer.

Parameters.Name: PrinterNames
Parameters.Description: Automatically provision printers based on their names (network host names).

Parameters.Name: PrivacyAdvertisingId
Parameters.Description: Enables or disables the use of advertising ID. Added in Windows 10, version 1607. Possible values are: notConfigured, blocked, allowed.

Parameters.Name: PrivacyAutoAcceptPairingAndConsentPrompts
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to allow the automatic acceptance of the pairing and privacy user consent dialog when launching apps.

Parameters.Name: PrivacyBlockActivityFeed
Parameters.Description: Blocks the usage of cloud based speech services for Cortana, Dictation, or Store applications.

Parameters.Name: PrivacyBlockInputPersonalization
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block the usage of cloud based speech services for Cortana, Dictation, or Store applications.

Parameters.Name: PrivacyBlockPublishUserActivities
Parameters.Description: Blocks the shared experiences/discovery of recently used resources in task switcher etc.

Parameters.Name: PrivacyDisableLaunchExperience
Parameters.Description: This policy prevents the privacy experience from launching during user logon for new and upgraded users.

Parameters.Name: ResetProtectionModeBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from reset protection mode.

Parameters.Name: SafeSearchFilter
Parameters.Description: Specifies what filter level of safe search is required. Possible values are: userDefined, strict, moderate.

Parameters.Name: ScreenCaptureBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from taking Screenshots.

Parameters.Name: SearchBlockDiacritics
Parameters.Description: Specifies if search can use diacritics.

Parameters.Name: SearchBlockWebResults
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block the web search.

Parameters.Name: SearchDisableAutoLanguageDetection
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether to use automatic language detection when indexing content and properties.

Parameters.Name: SearchDisableIndexerBackoff
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to disable the search indexer backoff feature.

Parameters.Name: SearchDisableIndexingEncryptedItems
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block indexing of WIP-protected items to prevent them from appearing in search results for Cortana or Explorer.

Parameters.Name: SearchDisableIndexingRemovableDrive
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to allow users to add locations on removable drives to libraries and to be indexed.

Parameters.Name: SearchDisableLocation
Parameters.Description: Specifies if search can use location information.

Parameters.Name: SearchDisableUseLocation
Parameters.Description: Specifies if search can use location information.

Parameters.Name: SearchEnableAutomaticIndexSizeManangement
Parameters.Description: Specifies minimum amount of hard drive space on the same drive as the index location before indexing stops.

Parameters.Name: SearchEnableRemoteQueries
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block remote queries of this computers index.

Parameters.Name: SecurityBlockAzureADJoinedDevicesAutoEncryption
Parameters.Description: Specify whether to allow automatic device encryption during OOBE when the device is Azure AD joined (desktop only).

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockAccountsPage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block access to Accounts in Settings app.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockAddProvisioningPackage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block the user from installing provisioning packages.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockAppsPage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block access to Apps in Settings app.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockChangeLanguage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block the user from changing the language settings.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockChangePowerSleep
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block the user from changing power and sleep settings.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockChangeRegion
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block the user from changing the region settings.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockChangeSystemTime
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block the user from changing date and time settings.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockDevicesPage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block access to Devices in Settings app.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockEaseOfAccessPage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block access to Ease of Access in Settings app.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockEditDeviceName
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block the user from editing the device name.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockGamingPage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block access to Gaming in Settings app.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockNetworkInternetPage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block access to Network & Internet in Settings app.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockPersonalizationPage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block access to Personalization in Settings app.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockPrivacyPage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block access to Privacy in Settings app.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockRemoveProvisioningPackage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block the runtime configuration agent from removing provisioning packages.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockSettingsApp
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block access to Settings app.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockSystemPage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block access to System in Settings app.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockTimeLanguagePage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block access to Time & Language in Settings app.

Parameters.Name: SettingsBlockUpdateSecurityPage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block access to Update & Security in Settings app.

Parameters.Name: SharedUserAppDataAllowed
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block multiple users of the same app to share data.

Parameters.Name: SmartScreenAppInstallControl
Parameters.Description: Added in Windows 10, version 1703. Allows IT Admins to control whether users are allowed to install apps from places other than the Store. Possible values are: notConfigured, anywhere, storeOnly, recommendations, preferStore.

Parameters.Name: SmartScreenBlockPromptOverride
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not users can override SmartScreen Filter warnings about potentially malicious websites.

Parameters.Name: SmartScreenBlockPromptOverrideForFiles
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not users can override the SmartScreen Filter warnings about downloading unverified files

Parameters.Name: SmartScreenEnableAppInstallControl
Parameters.Description: This property will be deprecated in July 2019 and will be replaced by property SmartScreenAppInstallControl. Allows IT Admins to control whether users are allowed to install apps from places other than the Store.

Parameters.Name: StartBlockUnpinningAppsFromTaskbar
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block the user from unpinning apps from taskbar.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuAppListVisibility
Parameters.Description: Setting the value of this collapses the app list, removes the app list entirely, or disables the corresponding toggle in the Settings app. Possible values are: userDefined, collapse, remove, disableSettingsApp.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuHideChangeAccountSettings
Parameters.Description: Enabling this policy hides the change account setting from appearing in the user tile in the start menu.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuHideFrequentlyUsedApps
Parameters.Description: Enabling this policy hides the most used apps from appearing on the start menu and disables the corresponding toggle in the Settings app.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuHideHibernate
Parameters.Description: Enabling this policy hides hibernate from appearing in the power button in the start menu.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuHideLock
Parameters.Description: Enabling this policy hides lock from appearing in the user tile in the start menu.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuHidePowerButton
Parameters.Description: Enabling this policy hides the power button from appearing in the start menu.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuHideRecentJumpLists
Parameters.Description: Enabling this policy hides recent jump lists from appearing on the start menu/taskbar and disables the corresponding toggle in the Settings app.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuHideRecentlyAddedApps
Parameters.Description: Enabling this policy hides recently added apps from appearing on the start menu and disables the corresponding toggle in the Settings app.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuHideRestartOptions
Parameters.Description: Enabling this policy hides 'Restart/Update and Restart' from appearing in the power button in the start menu.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuHideShutDown
Parameters.Description: Enabling this policy hides shut down/update and shut down from appearing in the power button in the start menu.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuHideSignOut
Parameters.Description: Enabling this policy hides sign out from appearing in the user tile in the start menu.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuHideSleep
Parameters.Description: Enabling this policy hides sleep from appearing in the power button in the start menu.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuHideSwitchAccount
Parameters.Description: Enabling this policy hides switch account from appearing in the user tile in the start menu.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuHideUserTile
Parameters.Description: Enabling this policy hides the user tile from appearing in the start menu.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuLayoutEdgeAssetsXml
Parameters.Description: This policy setting allows you to import Edge assets to be used with startMenuLayoutXml policy. Start layout can contain secondary tile from Edge app which looks for Edge local asset file. Edge local asset would not exist and cause Edge secondary tile to appear empty in this case. This policy only gets applied when startMenuLayoutXml policy is modified. The value should be a UTF-8 Base64 encoded byte array.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuLayoutXml
Parameters.Description: Allows admins to override the default Start menu layout and prevents the user from changing it. The layout is modified by specifying an XML file based on a layout modification schema. XML needs to be in a UTF8 encoded byte array format.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuMode
Parameters.Description: Allows admins to decide how the Start menu is displayed. Possible values are: userDefined, fullScreen, nonFullScreen.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuPinnedFolderDocuments
Parameters.Description: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the Documents folder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuPinnedFolderDownloads
Parameters.Description: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the Downloads folder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuPinnedFolderFileExplorer
Parameters.Description: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the FileExplorer shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuPinnedFolderHomeGroup
Parameters.Description: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the HomeGroup folder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuPinnedFolderMusic
Parameters.Description: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the Music folder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuPinnedFolderNetwork
Parameters.Description: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the Network folder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuPinnedFolderPersonalFolder
Parameters.Description: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the PersonalFolder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuPinnedFolderPictures
Parameters.Description: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the Pictures folder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuPinnedFolderSettings
Parameters.Description: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the Settings folder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

Parameters.Name: StartMenuPinnedFolderVideos
Parameters.Description: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the Videos folder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

Parameters.Name: StorageBlockRemovableStorage
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from using removable storage.

Parameters.Name: StorageRequireMobileDeviceEncryption
Parameters.Description: Indicating whether or not to require encryption on a mobile device.

Parameters.Name: StorageRestrictAppDataToSystemVolume
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether application data is restricted to the system drive.

Parameters.Name: StorageRestrictAppInstallToSystemVolume
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether the installation of applications is restricted to the system drive.

Parameters.Name: SystemTelemetryProxyServer
Parameters.Description: Gets or sets the fully qualified domain name (FQDN) or IP address of a proxy server to forward Connected User Experiences and Telemetry requests.

Parameters.Name: TaskManagerBlockEndTask
Parameters.Description: Specify whether non-administrators can use Task Manager to end tasks.

Parameters.Name: TenantLockdownRequireNetworkDuringOutOfBoxExperience
Parameters.Description: Whether the device is required to connect to the network.

Parameters.Name: UninstallBuiltInApps
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to uninstall a fixed list of built-in Windows apps.

Parameters.Name: UsbBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from USB connection.

Parameters.Name: VoiceRecordingBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from voice recording.

Parameters.Name: WebRtcBlockLocalhostIpAddress
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not user's localhost IP address is displayed while making phone calls using the WebRTC

Parameters.Name: WiFiBlockAutomaticConnectHotspots
Parameters.Description: Indicating whether or not to block automatically connecting to Wi-Fi hotspots. Has no impact if Wi-Fi is blocked.

Parameters.Name: WiFiBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from using Wi-Fi.

Parameters.Name: WiFiBlockManualConfiguration
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from using Wi-Fi manual configuration.

Parameters.Name: WiFiScanInterval
Parameters.Description: Specify how often devices scan for Wi-Fi networks. Supported values are 1-500, where 100 = default, and 500 = low frequency. Valid values 1 to 500

Parameters.Name: Windows10AppsForceUpdateSchedule
Parameters.Description: Windows 10 force update schedule for Apps.

Parameters.Name: WindowsSpotlightBlockConsumerSpecificFeatures
Parameters.Description: Allows IT admins to block experiences that are typically for consumers only, such as Start suggestions, Membership notifications, Post-OOBE app install and redirect tiles.

Parameters.Name: WindowsSpotlightBlocked
Parameters.Description: Allows IT admins to turn off all Windows Spotlight features

Parameters.Name: WindowsSpotlightBlockOnActionCenter
Parameters.Description: Block suggestions from Microsoft that show after each OS clean install, upgrade or in an on-going basis to introduce users to what is new or changed

Parameters.Name: WindowsSpotlightBlockTailoredExperiences
Parameters.Description: Block personalized content in Windows spotlight based on users device usage.

Parameters.Name: WindowsSpotlightBlockThirdPartyNotifications
Parameters.Description: Block third party content delivered via Windows Spotlight

Parameters.Name: WindowsSpotlightBlockWelcomeExperience
Parameters.Description: Block Windows Spotlight Windows welcome experience

Parameters.Name: WindowsSpotlightBlockWindowsTips
Parameters.Description: Allows IT admins to turn off the popup of Windows Tips.

Parameters.Name: WindowsSpotlightConfigureOnLockScreen
Parameters.Description: Specifies the type of Spotlight. Possible values are: notConfigured, disabled, enabled.

Parameters.Name: WindowsStoreBlockAutoUpdate
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to block automatic update of apps from Windows Store.

Parameters.Name: WindowsStoreBlocked
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from using the Windows store.

Parameters.Name: WindowsStoreEnablePrivateStoreOnly
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to enable Private Store Only.

Parameters.Name: WirelessDisplayBlockProjectionToThisDevice
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to allow other devices from discovering this PC for projection.

Parameters.Name: WirelessDisplayBlockUserInputFromReceiver
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to allow user input from wireless display receiver.

Parameters.Name: WirelessDisplayRequirePinForPairing
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to require a PIN for new devices to initiate pairing.

Parameters.Description: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

Parameters.Name: SupportsScopeTags
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not the underlying Device Configuration supports the assignment of scope tags. Assigning to the ScopeTags property is not allowed when this value is false and entities will not be visible to scoped users. This occurs for Legacy policies created in Silverlight and can be resolved by deleting and recreating the policy in the Azure Portal. This property is read-only.

Parameters.Description: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

Parameters.Name: RoleScopeTagIds
Parameters.Description: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

Parameters.Description: Present ensures the policy exists, absent ensures it is removed.

Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.

Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificateStore
Parameters.Description: Target store certificate. Possible values are: user, machine.

Parameters.Name: HashAlgorithm
Parameters.Description: SCEP Hash Algorithm. Possible values are: sha1, sha2.

Parameters.Name: KeySize
Parameters.Description: SCEP Key Size. Possible values are: size1024, size2048, size4096.

Parameters.Name: KeyUsage
Parameters.Description: SCEP Key Usage. Possible values are: keyEncipherment, digitalSignature.

Parameters.Name: ScepServerUrls
Parameters.Description: SCEP Server Url(s).

Parameters.Name: SubjectAlternativeNameFormatString
Parameters.Description: Custom String that defines the AAD Attribute.

Parameters.Name: SubjectNameFormatString
Parameters.Description: Custom format to use with SubjectNameFormat = Custom. Example: CN={{UserName}},E={{EmailAddress}},OU=Enterprise Users,O=Contoso Corporation,L=Redmond,ST=WA,C=US

Parameters.Name: CustomSubjectAlternativeNames
Parameters.Description: Custom Subject Alternative Name Settings. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

Parameters.Name: ExtendedKeyUsages
Parameters.Description: Extended Key Usage (EKU) settings. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

Parameters.Name: CertificateValidityPeriodScale
Parameters.Description: Scale for the Certificate Validity Period. Possible values are: days, months, years.

Parameters.Name: CertificateValidityPeriodValue
Parameters.Description: Value for the Certificate Validity Period

Parameters.Name: KeyStorageProvider
Parameters.Description: Key Storage Provider (KSP). Possible values are: useTpmKspOtherwiseUseSoftwareKsp, useTpmKspOtherwiseFail, usePassportForWorkKspOtherwiseFail, useSoftwareKsp.

Parameters.Name: RenewalThresholdPercentage
Parameters.Description: Certificate renewal threshold percentage. Valid values 1 to 99

Parameters.Name: SubjectAlternativeNameType
Parameters.Description: Certificate Subject Alternative Name Type. Possible values are: none, emailAddress, userPrincipalName, customAzureADAttribute, domainNameService, universalResourceIdentifier.

Parameters.Name: SubjectNameFormat
Parameters.Description: Certificate Subject Name Format. Possible values are: commonName, commonNameIncludingEmail, commonNameAsEmail, custom, commonNameAsIMEI, commonNameAsSerialNumber, commonNameAsAadDeviceId, commonNameAsIntuneDeviceId, commonNameAsDurableDeviceId.

Parameters.Name: AllowPrinting
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to allow the app from printing during the test.

Parameters.Name: AllowScreenCapture
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to allow screen capture capability during a test.

Parameters.Name: AllowTextSuggestion
Parameters.Description: Indicates whether or not to allow text suggestions during the test.

Parameters.Name: AssessmentAppUserModelId
Parameters.Description: Specifies the application user model ID of the assessment app launched when a user signs in to a secure assessment with a local guest account. Important notice: this property must be set with localGuestAccountName in order to make the local guest account sign-in experience work properly for secure assessments.

Parameters.Name: ConfigurationAccount
Parameters.Description: The account used to configure the Windows device for taking the test. The user can be a domain account (domain/user), an AAD account (usernametenant.com) or a local account (username).

Parameters.Name: ConfigurationAccountType
Parameters.Description: The account type used to by ConfigurationAccount. Possible values are: azureADAccount, domainAccount, localAccount, localGuestAccount.

Parameters.Name: LaunchUri
Parameters.Description: Url link to an assessment that's automatically loaded when the secure assessment browser is launched. It has to be a valid Url (https://msdn.microsoft.com/).

Parameters.Name: LocalGuestAccountName
Parameters.Description: Specifies the display text for the local guest account shown on the sign-in screen. Typically is the name of an assessment. When the user clicks the local guest account on the sign-in screen, an assessment app is launched with a specified assessment URL. Secure assessments can only be configured with local guest account sign-in on devices running Windows 10, version 1903 or later. Important notice: this property must be set with assessmentAppUserModelID in order to make the local guest account sign-in experience work properly for secure assessments.

Parameters.Name: AccountDeletionPolicy
Parameters.Description: Configures when accounts are deleted. Possible values are: immediate, diskSpaceThreshold, diskSpaceThresholdOrInactiveThreshold.

Parameters.Name: CacheAccountsAboveDiskFreePercentage
Parameters.Description: Sets the percentage of available disk space a PC should have before it stops deleting cached shared PC accounts. Only applies when AccountDeletionPolicy is DiskSpaceThreshold or DiskSpaceThresholdOrInactiveThreshold. Valid values 0 to 100

Parameters.Name: InactiveThresholdDays
Parameters.Description: Specifies when the accounts will start being deleted when they have not been logged on during the specified period, given as number of days. Only applies when AccountDeletionPolicy is DiskSpaceThreshold or DiskSpaceThresholdOrInactiveThreshold.

Parameters.Name: RemoveAccountsBelowDiskFreePercentage
Parameters.Description: Sets the percentage of disk space remaining on a PC before cached accounts will be deleted to free disk space. Accounts that have been inactive the longest will be deleted first. Only applies when AccountDeletionPolicy is DiskSpaceThresholdOrInactiveThreshold. Valid values 0 to 100

Description: Intune Device Configuration Shared Multi Device Policy for Windows10

Parameters.Name: AccountManagerPolicy
Parameters.Description: Specifies how accounts are managed on a shared PC. Only applies when disableAccountManager is false.

Parameters.Name: AllowedAccounts
Parameters.Description: Indicates which type of accounts are allowed to use on a shared PC. Possible values are: notConfigured, guest, domain.

Parameters.Name: AllowLocalStorage
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether local storage is allowed on a shared PC.

Parameters.Name: DisableAccountManager
Parameters.Description: Disables the account manager for shared PC mode.

Parameters.Name: DisableEduPolicies
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the default shared PC education environment policies should be disabled. For Windows 10 RS2 and later, this policy will be applied without setting Enabled to true.

Parameters.Name: DisablePowerPolicies
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the default shared PC power policies should be disabled.

Parameters.Name: DisableSignInOnResume
Parameters.Description: Disables the requirement to sign in whenever the device wakes up from sleep mode.

Parameters.Name: Enabled
Parameters.Description: Enables shared PC mode and applies the shared pc policies.

Parameters.Name: FastFirstSignIn
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether to auto connect new non-admin Azure AD accounts to pre-configured candidate local accounts. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: IdleTimeBeforeSleepInSeconds
Parameters.Description: Specifies the time in seconds that a device must sit idle before the PC goes to sleep. Setting this value to 0 prevents the sleep timeout from occurring.

Parameters.Name: KioskAppDisplayName
Parameters.Description: Specifies the display text for the account shown on the sign-in screen which launches the app specified by SetKioskAppUserModelId. Only applies when KioskAppUserModelId is set.

Parameters.Name: KioskAppUserModelId
Parameters.Description: Specifies the application user model ID of the app to use with assigned access.

Parameters.Name: LocalStorage
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether local storage is allowed on a shared PC. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: MaintenanceStartTime
Parameters.Description: Specifies the daily start time of maintenance hour.

Parameters.Name: SetAccountManager
Parameters.Description: Disables the account manager for shared PC mode. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: SetEduPolicies
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the default shared PC education environment policies should be enabled/disabled/not configured. For Windows 10 RS2 and later, this policy will be applied without setting Enabled to true. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: SetPowerPolicies
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the default shared PC power policies should be enabled/disabled. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: SignInOnResume
Parameters.Description: Specifies the requirement to sign in whenever the device wakes up from sleep mode. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: AuthenticationBlockPeriodInMinutes
Parameters.Description: Specify the duration for which automatic authentication attempts will be blocked from occurring after a failed authentication attempt.

Parameters.Name: AuthenticationMethod
Parameters.Description: Specify the authentication method. Possible values are: certificate, usernameAndPassword, derivedCredential. Possible values are: certificate, usernameAndPassword, derivedCredential, unknownFutureValue.

Parameters.Name: AuthenticationPeriodInSeconds
Parameters.Description: Specify the number of seconds for the client to wait after an authentication attempt before failing. Valid range 1-3600.

Parameters.Name: AuthenticationRetryDelayPeriodInSeconds
Parameters.Description: Specify the number of seconds between a failed authentication and the next authentication attempt. Valid range 1-3600.

Parameters.Name: AuthenticationType
Parameters.Description: Specify whether to authenticate the user, the device, either, or to use guest authentication (none). If you're using certificate authentication, make sure the certificate type matches the authentication type. Possible values are: none, user, machine, machineOrUser, guest. Possible values are: none, user, machine, machineOrUser, guest, unknownFutureValue.

Parameters.Name: CacheCredentials
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, caches user credentials on the device so that users don't need to keep entering them each time they connect. When FALSE, do not cache credentials. Default value is FALSE.

Parameters.Name: DisableUserPromptForServerValidation
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, prevents the user from being prompted to authorize new servers for trusted certification authorities when EAP type is selected as PEAP. When FALSE, does not prevent the user from being prompted. Default value is FALSE.

Parameters.Name: EapolStartPeriodInSeconds
Parameters.Description: Specify the number of seconds to wait before sending an EAPOL (Extensible Authentication Protocol over LAN) Start message. Valid range 1-3600.

Parameters.Name: EapType
Parameters.Description: Extensible Authentication Protocol (EAP). Indicates the type of EAP protocol set on the Wi-Fi endpoint (router). Possible values are: eapTls, leap, eapSim, eapTtls, peap, eapFast, teap. Possible values are: eapTls, leap, eapSim, eapTtls, peap, eapFast, teap.

Parameters.Name: Enforce8021X
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, the automatic configuration service for wired networks requires the use of 802.1X for port authentication. When FALSE, 802.1X is not required. Default value is FALSE.

Parameters.Name: ForceFIPSCompliance
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, forces FIPS compliance. When FALSE, does not enable FIPS compliance. Default value is FALSE.

Parameters.Name: InnerAuthenticationProtocolForEAPTTLS
Parameters.Description: Specify inner authentication protocol for EAP TTLS. Possible values are: unencryptedPassword, challengeHandshakeAuthenticationProtocol, microsoftChap, microsoftChapVersionTwo. Possible values are: unencryptedPassword, challengeHandshakeAuthenticationProtocol, microsoftChap, microsoftChapVersionTwo.

Parameters.Name: MaximumAuthenticationFailures
Parameters.Description: Specify the maximum authentication failures allowed for a set of credentials. Valid range 1-100.

Parameters.Name: MaximumEAPOLStartMessages
Parameters.Description: Specify the maximum number of EAPOL (Extensible Authentication Protocol over LAN) Start messages to be sent before returning failure. Valid range 1-100.

Parameters.Name: OuterIdentityPrivacyTemporaryValue
Parameters.Description: Specify the string to replace usernames for privacy when using EAP TTLS or PEAP.

Parameters.Name: PerformServerValidation
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, enables verification of server's identity by validating the certificate when EAP type is selected as PEAP. When FALSE, the certificate is not validated. Default value is TRUE.

Parameters.Name: RequireCryptographicBinding
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, enables cryptographic binding when EAP type is selected as PEAP. When FALSE, does not enable cryptogrpahic binding. Default value is TRUE.

Parameters.Name: SecondaryAuthenticationMethod
Parameters.Description: Specify the secondary authentication method. Possible values are: certificate, usernameAndPassword, derivedCredential. Possible values are: certificate, usernameAndPassword, derivedCredential, unknownFutureValue.

When set to 1, other devices will be able to detect the device. To verify, open the Bluetooth control panel on the device. Description:
This resource configures the Intune device platform enrollment restrictions.

### Restrictions

After deploying the configuration, updating the policies is no longer possible. This is a restriction by the Microsoft Graph API. Any further updates to the policies have to be done using the Intune Portal. 

**Be aware**: To deploy a Android platform restriction policy, two individual configurations must exist:

* The first one contains the key for `AndroidRestriction`
* The second one contains the key for `AndroidForWorkRestriction`

Parameters.Description: Ios restrictions based on platform, platform operating system version, and device ownership.

Parameters.Description: Mac restrictions based on platform, platform operating system version, and device ownership.

Parameters.Description: Mac restrictions based on platform, platform operating system version, and device ownership.

Parameters.Description: Windows Home Sku restrictions based on platform, platform operating system version, and device ownership.

Parameters.Name: WindowsMobileRestriction
Parameters.Description: Windows mobile restrictions based on platform, platform operating system version, and device ownership.

Parameters.Name: WindowsRestriction
Parameters.Description: Windows restrictions based on platform, platform operating system version, and device ownership.

After deploying the configuration, updating the policies is no longer possible. This is a restriction by the Microsoft Graph API. Any further updates to the policies have to be done using the Intune Portal.

* The first one contains the key for `AndroidRestriction`
* The second one contains the key for `AndroidForWorkRestriction`

Parameters.Description: The case-sensitive realm name for this profile.

Parameters.Description: A list of hosts or domain names for which the app extension performs SSO.

Parameters.Name: BlockAutomaticLogin
Parameters.Description: Enables or disables Keychain usage.

Parameters.Name: CacheName
Parameters.Description: The Generic Security Services name of the Kerberos cache to use for this profile.

Parameters.Name: CredentialBundleIdAccessControlList
Parameters.Description: A list of app Bundle IDs allowed to access the Kerberos Ticket Granting Ticket.

Parameters.Name: DomainRealms
Parameters.Description: A list of realms for custom domain-realm mapping. Realms are case sensitive.

Parameters.Name: IsDefaultRealm
Parameters.Description: When true, this profile's realm will be selected as the default. Necessary if multiple Kerberos-type profiles are configured.

Parameters.Name: PasswordBlockModification
Parameters.Description: Enables or disables password changes.

Parameters.Name: PasswordExpirationDays
Parameters.Description: Overrides the default password expiration in days. For most domains, this value is calculated automatically.

Parameters.Name: PasswordExpirationNotificationDays
Parameters.Description: The number of days until the user is notified that their password will expire (default is 15).

Parameters.Name: UserPrincipalName
Parameters.Description: The principal user name to use for this profile. The realm name does not need to be included.

Parameters.Name: PasswordRequireActiveDirectoryComplexity
Parameters.Description: Enables or disables whether passwords must meet Active Directory's complexity requirements.

Parameters.Name: PasswordPreviousPasswordBlockCount
Parameters.Description: The number of previous passwords to block.

Parameters.Name: PasswordMinimumLength
Parameters.Description: The minimum length of a password.

Parameters.Name: PasswordMinimumAgeDays
Parameters.Description: The minimum number of days until a user can change their password again.

Parameters.Name: PasswordRequirementsDescription
Parameters.Description: A description of the password complexity requirements.

Parameters.Name: RequireUserPresence
Parameters.Description: Whether to require authentication via Touch ID, Face ID, or a passcode to access the keychain entry.

Parameters.Name: ActiveDirectorySiteCode
Parameters.Description: The Active Directory site.

Parameters.Name: PasswordEnableLocalSync
Parameters.Description: Enables or disables password syncing. This won't affect users logged in with a mobile account on macOS.

Parameters.Name: BlockActiveDirectorySiteAutoDiscovery
Parameters.Description: Enables or disables whether the Kerberos extension can automatically determine its site name.

Parameters.Name: PasswordChangeUrl
Parameters.Description: The URL that the user will be sent to when they initiate a password change.

Parameters.Name: SignInHelpText
Parameters.Description: Text displayed to the user at the Kerberos sign-in window. Available for devices running iOS and iPadOS versions 14 and later.

Parameters.Name: ManagedAppsInBundleIdACLIncluded
Parameters.Description: When set to True, the Kerberos extension allows managed apps, and any apps entered with the app bundle ID to access the credential. When set to False, the Kerberos extension allows all apps to access the credential. Available for devices running iOS and iPadOS versions 14 and later.

Parameters.Name: EnableSharedDeviceMode
Parameters.Description: Enables or disables shared device mode.

Parameters.Name: BundleIdAccessControlList
Parameters.Description: An optional list of additional bundle IDs allowed to use the AAD extension for single sign-on.

Parameters.Name: Configurations
Parameters.Description: Gets or sets a list of typed key-value pairs used to configure Credential-type profiles. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

Parameters.Name: ExtensionIdentifier
Parameters.Description: Gets or sets the bundle ID of the app extension that performs SSO for the specified URLs.

Parameters.Name: TeamIdentifier
Parameters.Description: Gets or sets the team ID of the app extension that performs SSO for the specified URLs.

Parameters.Name: urlPrefixes
Parameters.Description: One or more URL prefixes of identity providers on whose behalf the app extension performs single sign-on. URLs must begin with http:// or https://. All URL prefixes must be unique for all profiles.

Description:
This resource configures Android Enterprise enrollment settings for device management within Microsoft Intune.
Note: Currently the bind API to enroll is waiting for the product team to make changes so the API can be called outside of an Intune portal. Until those changes are made, we can only unbind (disconnect/unenroll). For that reason we have commented out certain parameters that cannot be set. This will be uncommented once those changes are made.

Parameters.Description: The type of the schedule.

Parameters.Name: Date
Parameters.Description: The date when to run the schedule. Only applicable when the odataType is a run once schedule. Format: 2024-01-01

Parameters.Name: Interval
Parameters.Description: The interval of the schedule. Must be 1 in case of a run once schedule.

Parameters.Name: Time
Parameters.Description: The time when to run the schedule. Only applicable when the dataType is not an hourly schedule. Format: 01:00:00

Parameters.Name: UseUtc
Parameters.Description: If to use UTC as the time source. Only applicable when the dataType is not an hourly schedule.

Description:
This resource configures an Intune Endpoint Protection Exploit Protection policy for a Windows 10 Device.
Enables the IT admin to push out a configuration representing the desired system and application mitigation options to all the devices in the organization. The configuration is represented by an XML.
For more information Exploit Protection, see
- Enable Exploit Protection on Devices and Import: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/security/defender-endpoint/enable-exploit-protection,
- Export, and deploy Exploit Protection configurations: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/threat-protection/windows-defender-exploit-guard/import-export-exploit-protection-emet-xml

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: Policy description

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: Policy name

Parameters.Name: RoleScopeTagIds
Parameters.Description: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

Parameters.Name: Id
Parameters.Description: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

Parameters.Name: CRLcheck
Parameters.Description: Certificate revocation list verification (0: Disables CRL checking, 1: Specifies that CRL checking is attempted and that certificate validation fails only if the certificate is revoked. Other failures that are encountered during CRL checking (such as the revocation URL being unreachable) do not cause certificate validation to fail., 2: Means that checking is required and that certificate validation fails if any error is encountered during CRL processing)

Parameters.Name: DisableStatefulFtp
Parameters.Description: Disable Stateful Ftp (false: Stateful FTP enabled, true: Stateful FTP disabled)

Parameters.Name: EnablePacketQueue
Parameters.Description: Enable Packet Queue (0: Indicates that all queuing is to be disabled, 1: Specifies that inbound encrypted packets are to be queued, 2: Specifies that packets are to be queued after decryption is performed for forwarding)

Parameters.Name: IPsecExempt
Parameters.Description: IPsec Exceptions (0: FW_GLOBAL_CONFIG_IPSEC_EXEMPT_NONE:  No IPsec exemptions., 1: FW_GLOBAL_CONFIG_IPSEC_EXEMPT_NEIGHBOR_DISC:  Exempt neighbor discover IPv6 ICMP type-codes from IPsec., 2: FW_GLOBAL_CONFIG_IPSEC_EXEMPT_ICMP:  Exempt ICMP from IPsec., 4: FW_GLOBAL_CONFIG_IPSEC_EXEMPT_ROUTER_DISC:  Exempt router discover IPv6 ICMP type-codes from IPsec., 8: FW_GLOBAL_CONFIG_IPSEC_EXEMPT_DHCP:  Exempt both IPv4 and IPv6 DHCP traffic from IPsec.)

Parameters.Name: OpportunisticallyMatchAuthSetPerKM
Parameters.Description: Opportunistically Match Auth Set Per KM (false: FALSE, true: TRUE)

Parameters.Name: PresharedKeyEncoding
Parameters.Description: Preshared Key Encoding (0: FW_GLOBAL_CONFIG_PRESHARED_KEY_ENCODING_NONE:  Preshared key is not encoded. Instead, it is kept in its wide-character format. This symbolic constant has a value of 0., 1: FW_GLOBAL_CONFIG_PRESHARED_KEY_ENCODING_UTF_8:  Encode the preshared key using UTF-8. This symbolic constant has a value of 1.)

Description: Intune Firewall Policy for Windows10

Parameters.Name: Enabled
Parameters.Description: Enabled - Depends on FirewallRuleName (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: Name - Depends on FirewallRuleName

Parameters.Name: Direction
Parameters.Description: Direction - Depends on FirewallRuleName (in: The rule applies to inbound traffic., out: The rule applies to outbound traffic.)

Parameters.Name: Priority
Parameters.Description: Priority - Depends on FirewallRuleName

Parameters.Name: Profiles
Parameters.Description: Profiles - Depends on FirewallRuleName (1: FW_PROFILE_TYPE_DOMAIN:  This value represents the profile for networks that are connected to domains., 2: FW_PROFILE_TYPE_STANDARD:  This value represents the standard profile for networks. These networks are classified as private by the administrators in the server host. The classification happens the first time the host connects to the network. Usually these networks are behind Network Address Translation (NAT) devices, routers, and other edge devices, and they are in a private location, such as a home or an office. AND FW_PROFILE_TYPE_PRIVATE:  This value represents the profile for private networks, which is represented by the same value as that used for FW_PROFILE_TYPE_STANDARD., 4: FW_PROFILE_TYPE_PUBLIC:  This value represents the profile for public networks. These networks are classified as public by the administrators in the server host. The classification happens the first time the host connects to the network. Usually these networks are those at airports, coffee shops, and other public places where the peers in the network or the network administrator are not trusted., 2147483647: FW_PROFILE_TYPE_ALL:  This value represents all these network sets and any future network sets.)

Parameters.Name: VMCreatorId
Parameters.Description: Target - Depends on FirewallRuleName (wsl: WSL)

Parameters.Name: Action
Parameters.Description: Action - Depends on FirewallRuleName (0: Block, 1: Allow)

Parameters.Name: LocalAddressRanges
Parameters.Description: Local Address Ranges - Depends on FirewallRuleName

Parameters.Name: RemoteAddressRanges
Parameters.Description: Remote Address Ranges - Depends on FirewallRuleName

Parameters.Name: RemotePortRanges
Parameters.Description: Remote Port Ranges - Depends on FirewallRuleName

Parameters.Name: Protocol
Parameters.Description: Protocol - Depends on FirewallRuleName

Parameters.Name: LocalPortRanges
Parameters.Description: Local Port Ranges - Depends on FirewallRuleName

Parameters.Name: UpdateVersion
