Description: This resource configures an Intune mobile app of OfficeSuiteApp type for Windows devices.

Parameters.Name: Id
Parameters.Description: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: The DisplayName of the Mobile Threat Defense Connector partner. NOTE: Hard coded for convenience, not returned by the Graph API.

Parameters.Name: AllowPartnerToCollectIosApplicationMetadata
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, indicates the Mobile Threat Defense partner may collect metadata about installed applications from Intune for IOS devices. When FALSE, indicates the Mobile Threat Defense partner may not collect metadata about installed applications from Intune for IOS devices. Default value is FALSE.

Parameters.Name: AllowPartnerToCollectIosPersonalApplicationMetadata
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, indicates the Mobile Threat Defense partner may collect metadata about personally installed applications from Intune for IOS devices. When FALSE, indicates the Mobile Threat Defense partner may not collect metadata about personally installed applications from Intune for IOS devices. Default value is FALSE.

Parameters.Name: AndroidDeviceBlockedOnMissingPartnerData
Parameters.Description: For Android, set whether Intune must receive data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner prior to marking a device compliant.

Parameters.Name: AndroidEnabled
Parameters.Description: For Android, set whether data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner should be used during compliance evaluations.

Parameters.Name: AndroidMobileApplicationManagementEnabled
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, indicates that data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner can be used during Mobile Application Management (MAM) evaluations for Android devices. When FALSE, indicates that data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner should not be used during Mobile Application Management (MAM) evaluations for Android devices. Only one partner per platform may be enabled for Mobile Application Management (MAM) evaluation. Default value is FALSE.

Parameters.Name: IosDeviceBlockedOnMissingPartnerData
Parameters.Description: For IOS, set whether Intune must receive data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner prior to marking a device compliant.

Parameters.Name: IosEnabled
Parameters.Description: For IOS, get or set whether data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner should be used during compliance evaluations.

Parameters.Name: IosMobileApplicationManagementEnabled
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, indicates that data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner can be used during Mobile Application Management (MAM) evaluations for IOS devices. When FALSE, indicates that data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner should not be used during Mobile Application Management (MAM) evaluations for IOS devices. Only one partner per platform may be enabled for Mobile Application Management (MAM) evaluation. Default value is FALSE.

Parameters.Name: LastHeartbeatDateTime
Parameters.Description: DateTime of last Heartbeat received from the Mobile Threat Defense partner.

Parameters.Name: MicrosoftDefenderForEndpointAttachEnabled
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, indicates that configuration profile management via Microsoft Defender for Endpoint is enabled. When FALSE, inidicates that configuration profile management via Microsoft Defender for Endpoint is disabled. Default value is FALSE.

Parameters.Name: PartnerState
Parameters.Description: Partner state of this tenant.

Parameters.Name: PartnerUnresponsivenessThresholdInDays
Parameters.Description: Get or Set days the per tenant tolerance to unresponsiveness for this partner integration.

Parameters.Name: PartnerUnsupportedOSVersionBlocked
Parameters.Description: Get or set whether to block devices on the enabled platforms that do not meet the minimum version requirements of the Mobile Threat Defense partner.

Parameters.Name: WindowsDeviceBlockedOnMissingPartnerData
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, indicates that Intune must receive data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner prior to marking a device compliant for Windows. When FALSE, indicates that Intune may make a device compliant without receiving data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner for Windows. Default value is FALSE.

Parameters.Name: WindowsEnabled
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, indicates that data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner can be used during compliance evaluations for Windows. When FALSE, it indicates that data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner should not be used during compliance evaluations for Windows. Default value is FALSE.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Present ensures the instance exists, absent ensures it is removed.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the workload's Admin

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ApplicationSecret
Parameters.Description: Secret of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity
Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Parameters.Description: Allow Smart Screen (0: Turned off. Do not protect users from potential threats and prevent users from turning it on., 1: Turned on. Protect users from potential threats and prevent users from turning it off.)

Parameters.Name: AllowBluetooth
Parameters.Description: Allow Bluetooth (0: Disallow Bluetooth. If this is set to 0, the radio in the Bluetooth control panel will be grayed out and the user will not be able to turn Bluetooth on., 1: Reserved. If this is set to 1, the radio in the Bluetooth control panel will be functional and the user will be able to turn Bluetooth on., 2: Allow Bluetooth. If this is set to 2, the radio in the Bluetooth control panel will be functional and the user will be able to turn Bluetooth on.)

Parameters.Name: interfaceTypeMatch

Parameters.Name: dnsServerAddressMatch
Parameters.Description: DNS Search Server Address.

Parameters.Description: Display name of the Intune policy.

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: Description of the Intune policy.

Parameters.Name: RoleScopeTagIds
Parameters.Description: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

Parameters.Name: connectionName
Parameters.Description: Connection name displayed to the user.

Parameters.Name: connectionType
Parameters.Description: Connection type. Possible values are: ciscoAnyConnect, pulseSecure, f5EdgeClient, dellSonicWallMobileConnect, checkPointCapsuleVpn, customVpn, ciscoIPSec, citrix, ciscoAnyConnectV2, paloAltoGlobalProtect, zscalerPrivateAccess, f5Access2018, citrixSso, paloAltoGlobalProtectV2, ikEv2, alwaysOn, microsoftTunnel, netMotionMobility, microsoftProtect.

Parameters.Name: enableSplitTunneling
Parameters.Description: Send all network traffic through VPN.

Parameters.Name: authenticationMethod
Parameters.Description: Authentication method for this VPN connection.

Parameters.Name: safariDomains
Parameters.Description: Safari domains when this VPN per App setting is enabled. In addition to the apps associated with this VPN, Safari domains specified here will also be able to trigger this VPN connection.

Parameters.Name: associatedDomains
Parameters.Description: Associated Domains. These domains will be linked with the VPN configuration.

Parameters.Name: excludedDomains
Parameters.Description: Domains that are accessed through the public internet instead of through VPN, even when per-app VPN is activated.

Parameters.Name: proxyServer
Parameters.Description: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Parameters.Name: optInToDeviceIdSharing
Parameters.Description: Opt-In to sharing the device's Id to third-party vpn clients for use during network access control validation.

Parameters.Name: excludeList
Parameters.Description: Not documented on https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/resources/intune-deviceconfig-applevpnconfiguration?view=graph-rest-beta.

Parameters.Name: server
Parameters.Description: VPN Server on the network. Make sure end users can access this network location.

Parameters.Name: customData
Parameters.Description: Use this field to enable functionality not supported by Intune, but available in your VPN solution. Contact your VPN vendor to learn how to add these key/value pairs. This collection can contain a maximum of 25 elements

Parameters.Name: customKeyValueData
Parameters.Description: Use this field to enable functionality not supported by Intune, but available in your VPN solution. Contact your VPN vendor to learn how to add these key/value pairs. This collection can contain a maximum of 25 elements

Parameters.Name: onDemandRules
Parameters.Description: On-Demand Rules. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

Parameters.Name: targetedMobileApps
Parameters.Description: Not documented on https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/resources/intune-deviceconfig-applevpnconfiguration?view=graph-rest-beta.

Parameters.Description: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Parameters.Name: Ensure

Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Intune Admin

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ApplicationSecret
Parameters.Description: Secret of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity
Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: version
Parameters.Description: Version of the device configuration. Inherited from deviceConfiguration.

Parameters.Name: loginGroupOrDomain
Parameters.Description: Login group or domain when connection type is set to Dell SonicWALL Mobile Connection. Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration.

Parameters.Name: role
Parameters.Description: Role when connection type is set to Pulse Secure. Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration.

Parameters.Name: realm
Parameters.Description: Realm when connection type is set to Pulse Secure. Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration.

Parameters.Name: identifier
Parameters.Description: Identifier provided by VPN vendor when connection type is set to Custom VPN. For example: Cisco AnyConnect uses an identifier of the form com.cisco.anyconnect.applevpn.plugin Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration.

Parameters.Name: enablePerApp
Parameters.Description: Setting this to true creates Per-App VPN payload which can later be associated with Apps that can trigger this VPN conneciton on the end user's iOS device. Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration.

Parameters.Name: providerType
Parameters.Description: Provider type for per-app VPN. Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration. Possible values are: notConfigured, appProxy, packetTunnel.

Parameters.Name: disableOnDemandUserOverride
Parameters.Description: Toggle to prevent user from disabling automatic VPN in the Settings app Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration.

Parameters.Name: disconnectOnIdle
Parameters.Description: Whether to disconnect after on-demand connection idles Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration

Parameters.Name: disconnectOnIdleTimerInSeconds
Parameters.Description: The length of time in seconds to wait before disconnecting an on-demand connection. Valid values 0 to 65535 Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration.

Parameters.Name: microsoftTunnelSiteId
Parameters.Description: Microsoft Tunnel site ID.

Parameters.Name: cloudName
Parameters.Description: Zscaler only. Zscaler cloud which the user is assigned to.

Parameters.Name: strictEnforcement
Parameters.Description: Zscaler only. Blocks network traffic until the user signs into Zscaler app. True means traffic is blocked.

Parameters.Name: userDomain
Parameters.Description: Zscaler only. Enter a static domain to pre-populate the login field with in the Zscaler app. If this is left empty, the user's Azure Active Directory domain will be used instead.

Parameters.Name: AzureRightsManagementServicesAllowed
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether to allow Azure RMS encryption for WIP

Parameters.Name: DataRecoveryCertificate
Parameters.Description: Specifies a recovery certificate that can be used for data recovery of encrypted files. This is the same as the data recovery agent(DRA) certificate for encrypting file system(EFS)

Parameters.Name: EnforcementLevel
Parameters.Description: WIP enforcement level.See the Enum definition for supported values. Possible values are: noProtection, encryptAndAuditOnly, encryptAuditAndPrompt, encryptAuditAndBlock.

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseDomain
Parameters.Description: Primary enterprise domain

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseInternalProxyServers
Parameters.Description: This is the comma-separated list of internal proxy servers. For example, '157.54.14.28, 157.54.11.118, 10.202.14.167, 157.53.14.163, 157.69.210.59'. These proxies have been configured by the admin to connect to specific resources on the Internet. They are considered to be enterprise network locations. The proxies are only leveraged in configuring the EnterpriseProxiedDomains policy to force traffic to the matched domains through these proxies

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseIPRanges
Parameters.Description: Sets the enterprise IP ranges that define the computers in the enterprise network. Data that comes from those computers will be considered part of the enterprise and protected. These locations will be considered a safe destination for enterprise data to be shared to

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseIPRangesAreAuthoritative
Parameters.Description: Boolean value that tells the client to accept the configured list and not to use heuristics to attempt to find other subnets. Default is false

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseNetworkDomainNames
Parameters.Description: This is the list of domains that comprise the boundaries of the enterprise. Data from one of these domains that is sent to a device will be considered enterprise data and protected These locations will be considered a safe destination for enterprise data to be shared to

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseProtectedDomainNames
Parameters.Description: List of enterprise domains to be protected

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseProxiedDomains
Parameters.Description: Contains a list of Enterprise resource domains hosted in the cloud that need to be protected. Connections to these resources are considered enterprise data. If a proxy is paired with a cloud resource, traffic to the cloud resource will be routed through the enterprise network via the denoted proxy server (on Port 80). A proxy server used for this purpose must also be configured using the EnterpriseInternalProxyServers policy

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseProxyServers
Parameters.Description: This is a list of proxy servers. Any server not on this list is considered non-enterprise

Parameters.Name: EnterpriseProxyServersAreAuthoritative
Parameters.Description: Boolean value that tells the client to accept the configured list of proxies and not try to detect other work proxies. Default is false

Parameters.Name: ExemptApps
Parameters.Description: Exempt applications can also access enterprise data, but the data handled by those applications are not protected. This is because some critical enterprise applications may have compatibility problems with encrypted data.

Parameters.Name: IconsVisible
Parameters.Description: Determines whether overlays are added to icons for WIP protected files in Explorer and enterprise only app tiles in the Start menu. Starting in Windows 10, version 1703 this setting also configures the visibility of the WIP icon in the title bar of a WIP-protected app

Parameters.Name: IndexingEncryptedStoresOrItemsBlocked
Parameters.Description: This switch is for the Windows Search Indexer, to allow or disallow indexing of items

Parameters.Name: NeutralDomainResources
Parameters.Description: List of domain names that can used for work or personal resource

Parameters.Name: ProtectedApps
Parameters.Description: Protected applications can access enterprise data and the data handled by those applications are protected with encryption

Parameters.Name: ProtectionUnderLockConfigRequired
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the protection under lock feature (also known as encrypt under pin) should be configured

Parameters.Name: RevokeOnUnenrollDisabled
Parameters.Description: This policy controls whether to revoke the WIP keys when a device unenrolls from the management service. If set to 1 (Don't revoke keys), the keys will not be revoked and the user will continue to have access to protected files after unenrollment. If the keys are not revoked, there will be no revoked file cleanup subsequently.

Parameters.Name: RightsManagementServicesTemplateId
Parameters.Description: TemplateID GUID to use for RMS encryption. The RMS template allows the IT admin to configure the details about who has access to RMS-protected file and how long they have access

Parameters.Name: SmbAutoEncryptedFileExtensions
Parameters.Description: Specifies a list of file extensions, so that files with these extensions are encrypted when copying from an SMB share within the corporate boundary

Parameters.Description: The policy's description.

Parameters.Name: OfferEndDateTimeInUTC
Parameters.Description: The feature update's ending  of release date and time to be set, update, and displayed for a feature Update profile for example: 2020-06-09T10:00:00Z.

Parameters.Name: OfferIntervalInDays
Parameters.Description: The number of day(s) between each set of offers to be set, updated, and displayed for a feature update profile, for example: if OfferStartDateTimeInUTC is 2020-06-09T10:00:00Z, and OfferIntervalInDays is 1, then the next two sets of offers will be made consecutively on 2020-06-10T10:00:00Z (next day at the same specified time) and 2020-06-11T10:00:00Z (next next day at the same specified time) with 1 day in between each set of offers.

Parameters.Name: OfferStartDateTimeInUTC
Parameters.Description: The feature update's starting date and time to be set, update, and displayed for a feature Update profile for example: 2020-06-09T10:00:00Z.

Description:
Intune Windows Update For Business Feature Update Profile for Windows10

## RolloutSettings

The RolloutSettings for this resource have the following constraints and notes:

* When creating a policy:
    * If only a start date is specified, then the start date must be at least today.
        * If the desired state date is before the current date, it will be adjusted to the current date.
    * If a start and end date is specified, the start date must be the current date + 2 days, and
      the end date must be at least one day after the start date.
        * If the start date is before the current date + 2 days, it will be adjusted to this date.
* When updating a policy:
    * If only a start date is specified, then the start date must either be the date from the current
      configuration or the current date (or later).
        * If the desired state date is before the current date, it will be adjusted to the current date.
    * If a start and end date is specified, the start date must be the current date + 2 days, and
      the end date must be at least one day after the start date.
        * If the start date is before the current date + 2 days, it will be adjusted to this date.
* When testing a policy:
    * If the policy is missing and the start and end date are before the current date, it will return true.
    * If the start date is different but before the current start date or time, it will return true.

Parameters.Name: Id
Parameters.Description: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: The display name of the profile.

Description:
Intune Windows Update For Business Feature Update Profile for Windows10

## RolloutSettings

The RolloutSettings for this resource have the following constraints and notes:

* When creating a policy:
    * If only a start date is specified, then the start date must be at least today.
        * If the desired state date is before the current date, it will be adjusted to the current date.
    * If a start and end date is specified, the start date must be the current date + 2 days, and
      the end date must be at least one day after the start date.
        * If the start date is before the current date + 2 days, it will be adjusted to this date.
* When updating a policy:
    * If only a start date is specified, then the start date must either be the date from the current
      configuration or the current date (or later).
        * If the desired state date is before the current date, it will be adjusted to the current date.
    * If a start and end date is specified, the start date must be the current date + 2 days, and
      the end date must be at least one day after the start date.
        * If the start date is before the current date + 2 days, it will be adjusted to this date.
* When testing a policy:
    * If the policy is missing and the start and end date are before the current date, it will return true.
    * If the start date is different but before the current start date or time, it will return true.

Parameters.Description: The release date to identify a quality update. Format is yyyy-MM-ddT00:00:00Z.

Description: Intune Windows Update For Business Ring Update Profile for Windows 10

Parameters.Name: Id
Parameters.Description: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

Parameters.Name: RoleScopeTagIds
Parameters.Description: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

Parameters.Name: AllowWindows11Upgrade
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, allows eligible Windows 10 devices to upgrade to Windows 11. When FALSE, implies the device stays on the existing operating system. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: AutomaticUpdateMode
Parameters.Description: The Automatic Update Mode. Possible values are: UserDefined, NotifyDownload, AutoInstallAtMaintenanceTime, AutoInstallAndRebootAtMaintenanceTime, AutoInstallAndRebootAtScheduledTime, AutoInstallAndRebootWithoutEndUserControl, WindowsDefault. UserDefined is the default value, no intent. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: userDefined, notifyDownload, autoInstallAtMaintenanceTime, autoInstallAndRebootAtMaintenanceTime, autoInstallAndRebootAtScheduledTime, autoInstallAndRebootWithoutEndUserControl, windowsDefault.

Parameters.Name: AutoRestartNotificationDismissal
Parameters.Description: Specify the method by which the auto-restart required notification is dismissed. Possible values are: NotConfigured, Automatic, User. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: notConfigured, automatic, user, unknownFutureValue.

Parameters.Name: BusinessReadyUpdatesOnly
Parameters.Description: Determines which branch devices will receive their updates from. Possible values are: UserDefined, All, BusinessReadyOnly, WindowsInsiderBuildFast, WindowsInsiderBuildSlow, WindowsInsiderBuildRelease. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: userDefined, all, businessReadyOnly, windowsInsiderBuildFast, windowsInsiderBuildSlow, windowsInsiderBuildRelease.

Parameters.Name: DeadlineForFeatureUpdatesInDays
Parameters.Description: Number of days before feature updates are installed automatically with valid range from 0 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: DeadlineForQualityUpdatesInDays
Parameters.Description: Number of days before quality updates are installed automatically with valid range from 0 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: DeadlineGracePeriodInDays
Parameters.Description: Number of days after deadline until restarts occur automatically with valid range from 0 to 7 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: DeliveryOptimizationMode
Parameters.Description: The Delivery Optimization Mode. Possible values are: UserDefined, HttpOnly, HttpWithPeeringNat, HttpWithPeeringPrivateGroup, HttpWithInternetPeering, SimpleDownload, BypassMode. UserDefined allows the user to set. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: userDefined, httpOnly, httpWithPeeringNat, httpWithPeeringPrivateGroup, httpWithInternetPeering, simpleDownload, bypassMode.

Parameters.Name: DriversExcluded
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, excludes Windows update Drivers. When FALSE, does not exclude Windows update Drivers. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: EngagedRestartDeadlineInDays
Parameters.Description: Deadline in days before automatically scheduling and executing a pending restart outside of active hours, with valid range from 2 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: EngagedRestartSnoozeScheduleInDays
Parameters.Description: Number of days a user can snooze Engaged Restart reminder notifications with valid range from 1 to 3 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: EngagedRestartTransitionScheduleInDays
Parameters.Description: Number of days before transitioning from Auto Restarts scheduled outside of active hours to Engaged Restart, which requires the user to schedule, with valid range from 0 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: FeatureUpdatesDeferralPeriodInDays
Parameters.Description: Defer Feature Updates by these many days with valid range from 0 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: FeatureUpdatesPaused
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, assigned devices are paused from receiving feature updates for up to 35 days from the time you pause the ring. When FALSE, does not pause Feature Updates. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.s

Parameters.Name: FeatureUpdatesPauseExpiryDateTime
Parameters.Description: The Feature Updates Pause Expiry datetime. This value is 35 days from the time admin paused or extended the pause for the ring. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: FeatureUpdatesPauseStartDate
Parameters.Description: The Feature Updates Pause start date. This value is the time when the admin paused or extended the pause for the ring. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. This property is read-only.

Parameters.Name: FeatureUpdatesRollbackStartDateTime
Parameters.Description: The Feature Updates Rollback Start datetime.This value is the time when the admin rolled back the Feature update for the ring.Returned by default.Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: FeatureUpdatesRollbackWindowInDays
Parameters.Description: The number of days after a Feature Update for which a rollback is valid with valid range from 2 to 60 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: InstallationSchedule
Parameters.Description: The Installation Schedule. Possible values are: ActiveHoursStart, ActiveHoursEnd, ScheduledInstallDay, ScheduledInstallTime. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: MicrosoftUpdateServiceAllowed
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, allows Microsoft Update Service. When FALSE, does not allow Microsoft Update Service. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: PostponeRebootUntilAfterDeadline
Parameters.Description: When TRUE the device should wait until deadline for rebooting outside of active hours. When FALSE the device should not wait until deadline for rebooting outside of active hours. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: PrereleaseFeatures
Parameters.Description: The Pre-Release Features. Possible values are: UserDefined, SettingsOnly, SettingsAndExperimentations, NotAllowed. UserDefined is the default value, no intent. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: userDefined, settingsOnly, settingsAndExperimentations, notAllowed.

Parameters.Name: QualityUpdatesDeferralPeriodInDays
Parameters.Description: Defer Quality Updates by these many days with valid range from 0 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: QualityUpdatesPaused
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, assigned devices are paused from receiving quality updates for up to 35 days from the time you pause the ring. When FALSE, does not pause Quality Updates. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: QualityUpdatesPauseExpiryDateTime
Parameters.Description: The Quality Updates Pause Expiry datetime. This value is 35 days from the time admin paused or extended the pause for the ring. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: QualityUpdatesPauseStartDate
Parameters.Description: The Quality Updates Pause start date. This value is the time when the admin paused or extended the pause for the ring. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. This property is read-only.

Parameters.Name: QualityUpdatesRollbackStartDateTime
Parameters.Description: The Quality Updates Rollback Start datetime. This value is the time when the admin rolled back the Quality update for the ring. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: ScheduleImminentRestartWarningInMinutes
Parameters.Description: Specify the period for auto-restart imminent warning notifications. Supported values: 15, 30 or 60 (minutes). Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: ScheduleRestartWarningInHours
Parameters.Description: Specify the period for auto-restart warning reminder notifications. Supported values: 2, 4, 8, 12 or 24 (hours). Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: SkipChecksBeforeRestart
Parameters.Description: When TRUE, skips all checks before restart: Battery level = 40%, User presence, Display Needed, Presentation mode, Full screen mode, phone call state, game mode etc. When FALSE, does not skip all checks before restart. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Parameters.Name: UpdateNotificationLevel
Parameters.Description: Specifies what Windows Update notifications users see. Possible values are: NotConfigured, DefaultNotifications, RestartWarningsOnly, DisableAllNotifications. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: notConfigured, defaultNotifications, restartWarningsOnly, disableAllNotifications, unknownFutureValue.

Parameters.Name: UpdateWeeks
Parameters.Description: Schedule the update installation on the weeks of the month. Possible values are: UserDefined, FirstWeek, SecondWeek, ThirdWeek, FourthWeek, EveryWeek. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: userDefined, firstWeek, secondWeek, thirdWeek, fourthWeek, everyWeek, unknownFutureValue.

Parameters.Name: UserPauseAccess
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether to enable end user's access to pause software updates. Possible values are: NotConfigured, Enabled, Disabled. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Parameters.Name: UserWindowsUpdateScanAccess
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether to disable user's access to scan Windows Update. Possible values are: NotConfigured, Enabled, Disabled. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Description:
This resource configures the tenant settings so that the tenant runs in a fully
configurable mode. Usually tenants are created in a mode, that limits options to
customize the tenant. This setting is reflected by the Organization Config proptery
`IsDehydrated: True`.
Running this resource will set the property to false.

Currently there is no setting available to undo Enable-OrganizationCustomization.

Description:
This resource configures an Audit ConfigurationPolicy
in Security and Compliance Center.

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies the unique name for the sensitivity label. The maximum length is 64 characters. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if this label policy should exist or not.

Parameters.Name: Comment
Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

Parameters.Name: ApplySensitivityLabel
Parameters.Description: The ApplySensitivityLabel parameter specifies the label to use for the auto label policy.

Parameters.Name: ExchangeSender
Parameters.Description: The ExchangeSender parameter specifies which senders to include in the policy.

Parameters.Name: ExchangeSenderException
Parameters.Description: The ExchangeSenderException parameter specifies which senders to exclude in the policy.

Parameters.Name: ExchangeSenderMemberOf
Parameters.Description: The ExchangeSenderMemberOf parameter specifies the distribution groups, mail-enabled security groups, or dynamic distribution groups to include in the auto-labeling policy.

Parameters.Name: ExchangeSenderMemberOfException
Parameters.Description: he ExchangeSenderMemberOf parameter specifies the distribution groups, mail-enabled security groups, or dynamic distribution groups to exclude from the auto-labeling policy.

Parameters.Name: ExchangeLocation
Parameters.Description: The ExchangeSender parameter specifies which senders to include in the policy.

Parameters.Name: AddExchangeLocation
Parameters.Description: This AddExchangeLocation parameter specifies new Exchange locations to be added to the policy without affecting the existing ones.

Parameters.Name: RemoveExchangeLocation
Parameters.Description: The RemoveExchangeLocation parameter removes locations on Exchange from the policy.

Parameters.Name: Mode
Parameters.Description: The Mode parameter specifies the action and notification level of the auto-labeling policy.

Parameters.Name: OneDriveLocation
Parameters.Description: The OneDriveLocation parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to include. You identify the site by its URL value, or you can use the value.

Parameters.Name: AddOneDriveLocation
Parameters.Description: The AddOneDriveLocation parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to add to the list of included sites when you aren't using the value All for the OneDriveLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: RemoveOneDriveLocation
Parameters.Description: The RemoveOneDriveLocation parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to remove from the list of included sites when you aren't using the value All for the OneDriveLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: AddOneDriveLocationException
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to exclude when you use the value All for the OneDriveLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: RemoveOneDriveLocationException
Parameters.Description: This RemoveOneDriveLocationException parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to remove from the list of excluded sites when you use the value All for the OneDriveLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: OneDriveLocationException
Parameters.Description: The AddOneDriveLocationException parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to add to the list of excluded sites when you use the value All for the OneDriveLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: Priority
Parameters.Description: The Priority parameter specifies the priority of the policy. The highest priority policy will take action over lower priority policies if two policies are applicable for a file.

Parameters.Name: SharePointLocation
Parameters.Description: The SharePointLocation parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to include. You identify the site by its URL value, or you can use the value All to include all sites.

Parameters.Name: SharePointLocationException
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to exclude when you use the value All for the SharePointLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: AddSharePointLocationException
Parameters.Description: The AddSharePointLocation parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to add to the list of included sites when you aren't using the value All for the SharePointLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: RemoveSharePointLocationException
Parameters.Description: The RemoveSharePointLocationException parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to remove from the list of excluded sites when you use the value All for the SharePointLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: AddSharePointLocation
Parameters.Description: The AddSharePointLocation parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to add to the list of included sites when you aren't using the value All for the SharePointLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: RemoveSharePointLocation
Parameters.Description: The RemoveSharePointLocation parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to remove from the list of included sites when you aren't using the value All for the SharePointLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Exchange Global Admin

Parameters.Description: Path to certificate used in service principal usually a PFX file.

Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Description:
This resource configures a Auto Sensitivity Label
Rule in Security and Compliance Center.

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: Name of the Rule.

Parameters.Name: Policy
Parameters.Description: Name of the associated Policy.

Parameters.Name: AccessScope
Parameters.Description: The AccessScope parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that's based on the access scope of the content. The rule is applied to content that matches the specified access scope. Valid values are: InOrganization, NotInOrganization, None

Parameters.Name: AnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords
Parameters.Description: The AnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words or phrases in recipient email addresses. You can specify multiple words or phrases separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: AnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The AnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in recipient email addresses by using regular expressions.

Parameters.Name: Comment
Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

Parameters.Name: ContentContainsSensitiveInformation
Parameters.Description: The ContentContainsSensitiveInformation parameter specifies a condition for the rule that's based on a sensitive information type match in content. The rule is applied to content that contains the specified sensitive information type.

Parameters.Name: ContentExtensionMatchesWords
Parameters.Description: The ContentExtensionMatchesWords parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words in file name extensions. You can specify multiple words separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: Disabled
Parameters.Description: The Disabled parameter specifies whether the auto-labeling policy rule is enabled or disabled.

Parameters.Name: DocumentIsPasswordProtected
Parameters.Description: The DocumentIsPasswordProtected parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for password protected files (because the contents of the file can't be inspected). Password detection only works for Office documents and .zip files.

Parameters.Name: DocumentIsUnsupported
Parameters.Description: The DocumentIsUnsupported parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for files that can't be scanned.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfAccessScope
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfAccessScopeAccessScope parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that's based on the access scope of the content. The rule isn't applied to content that matches the specified access scope. Valid values are: InOrganization, NotInOrganization, None

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words or phrases in recipient email addresses. You can specify multiple words separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in recipient email addresses by using regular expressions.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfContentContainsSensitiveInformation
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfContentContainsSensitiveInformation parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that's based on a sensitive information type match in content. The rule isn't applied to content that contains the specified sensitive information type.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfContentExtensionMatchesWords
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfContentExtensionMatchesWords parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words in file name extensions. You can specify multiple words separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfDocumentIsPasswordProtected
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfDocumentIsPasswordProtected parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for password protected files (because the contents of the file can't be inspected). Password detection only works for Office documents and .zip files.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfDocumentIsUnsupported
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfDocumentIsUnsupported parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for files that can't be scanned.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfFrom
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfFrom parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages from specific senders. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the sender.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfFromAddressContainsWords
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfFromAddressContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words or phrases in the sender's email address. You can specify multiple words or phrases separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfFromAddressMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfFromAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in the sender's email address by using regular expressions.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfFromMemberOf
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfFromMemberOf parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages sent by group members. You identify the group members by their email addresses. You can enter multiple values separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfHeaderMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The HeaderMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in a header field by using regular expressions.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfProcessingLimitExceeded
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfProcessingLimitExceeded parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for files where scanning couldn't complete.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for recipients with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSenderDomainIs
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSenderDomainIs parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages from senders with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple values separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSenderIPRanges
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSenderIpRanges parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for senders whose IP addresses matches the specified value, or fall within the specified ranges.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSentTo
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSentTo parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSentToMemberOf
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSentToMemberOf parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages sent to members of distribution groups, dynamic distribution groups, or mail-enabled security groups. You identify the groups by email address. You can specify multiple values separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSubjectMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSubjectMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field of messages by using regular expressions.

Parameters.Name: FromAddressContainsWords
Parameters.Description: The FromAddressContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words or phrases in the sender's email address. You can specify multiple words or phrases separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: FromAddressMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The FromAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in the sender's email address by using regular expressions.

Parameters.Name: HeaderMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The HeaderMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in a header field by using regular expressions.

Parameters.Name: ProcessingLimitExceeded
Parameters.Description: The ProcessingLimitExceeded parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for files where scanning couldn't complete. You can use this condition to create rules that work together to identify and process messages where the content couldn't be fully scanned.

Parameters.Name: RecipientDomainIs
Parameters.Description: The RecipientDomainIs parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for recipients with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ReportSeverityLevel
Parameters.Description: The ReportSeverityLevel parameter specifies the severity level of the incident report for content detections based on the rule. Valid values are: None, Low, Medium, High

Parameters.Name: RuleErrorAction
Parameters.Description: The RuleErrorAction parameter specifies what to do if an error is encountered during the evaluation of the rule. Valid values are: Ignore, RetryThenBlock, *blank*

Parameters.Name: SenderDomainIs
Parameters.Description: The SenderDomainIs parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages from senders with email address in the specified domains.

Parameters.Name: SenderIPRanges
Parameters.Description: The SenderIpRanges parameter specifies a condition for the auto-sensitivity policy rule that looks for senders whose IP addresses matches the specified value, or fall within the specified ranges.

Parameters.Name: SentTo
Parameters.Description: The SentTo parameter specifies a condition for the auto-sensitivity policy rule that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

Parameters.Name: SentToMemberOf
Parameters.Description: The SentToMemberOf parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages sent to members of distribution groups, dynamic distribution groups, or mail-enabled security groups. You identify the groups by email address.

Parameters.Name: SubjectMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The SubjectMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field of messages by using regular expressions.

Parameters.Name: Workload
Parameters.Description: Workload the rule is associated with. Value can be: Exchange, SharePoint, OneDriveForBusiness

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if this rule should exist or not.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Exchange Global Admin

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Description: Username can be made up to anything but password will be used for CertificatePassword

Parameters.Description: Path to certificate used in service principal usually a PFX file.

Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: Comment

Parameters.Name: Enabled

Parameters.Name: ExchangeLocation

Parameters.Name: PublicFolderLocation
Parameters.Description: The PublicFolderLocation parameter specifies that you want to include all public folders in the case hold policy. You use the value All for this parameter.

Parameters.Name: SharePointLocation
Parameters.Description: The SharePointLocation parameter specifies the SharePoint Online and OneDrive for Business sites to include. You identify a site by its URL value.

in Security and Compliance Center.

Parameters.Name: Policy
Parameters.Description: The Policy parameter specifies the case hold policy that contains the rule. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the policy.

Parameters.Name: Comment
Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

Parameters.Name: ContentMatchQuery
Parameters.Description: The ContentMatchQuery parameter specifies a content search filter. Use this parameter to create a query-based hold so only the content that matches the specified search query is placed on hold. This parameter uses a text search string or a query that's formatted by using the Keyword Query Language (KQL).

Parameters.Name: Disabled
Parameters.Description: The Disabled parameter specifies whether the case hold rule is enabled or disabled.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Present ensures the rule exists, absent ensures it is removed

Description: This resource configures an eDiscovery Case in Security and Compliance.

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the complaiance tag.

Parameters.Name: Case
Parameters.Description: Compliance Case (eDiscovery) that this Search is associated with

Parameters.Name: AllowNotFoundExchangeLocationsEnabled
Parameters.Description: The AllowNotFoundExchangeLocationsEnabled parameter specifies whether to include mailboxes other than regular user mailboxes in the compliance search.

Parameters.Name: ContentMatchQuery
Parameters.Description: The ContentMatchQuery parameter specifies a content search filter. This parameter uses a text search string or a query that's formatted by using the Keyword Query Language (KQL).

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: The Description parameter specifies an optional description for the compliance search. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Parameters.Name: ExchangeLocation
Parameters.Description: The ExchangeLocation parameter specifies the mailboxes to include.

Parameters.Name: ExchangeLocationExclusion
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies the mailboxes to exclude when you use the value All for the ExchangeLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: HoldNames
Parameters.Description: The HoldNames parameter specifies that the content locations that have been placed on hold in the specified eDiscovery case will be searched. You use the value All for this parameter. You also need to specify the name of an eDiscovery case by using the Case parameter.

Parameters.Name: IncludeUserAppContent
Parameters.Description: The IncludeUserAppContent parameter specifies that you want to search the cloud-based storage location for users who don't have a regular Office 365 user account in your organization. These types of users include users without an Exchange Online license who use Office applications, Office 365 guest users, and on-premises users whose identity is synchronized with your Office 365 organization.

Parameters.Name: Language
Parameters.Description: The Language parameter specifies the language for the compliance search. Valid input for this parameter is a supported culture code value from the Microsoft .NET Framework CultureInfo class. For example, da-DK for Danish or ja-JP for Japanese.

Parameters.Name: PublicFolderLocation
Parameters.Description: The PublicFolderLocation parameter specifies that you want to include all public folders in the search. You use the value All for this parameter.

Parameters.Name: SharePointLocation
Parameters.Description: The SharePointLocation parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to include. You identify the site by its URL value, or you can use the value All to include all sites.

Parameters.Name: SharePointLocationExclusion
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to exclude when you use the value All for the SharePointLocation parameter. You identify the site by its URL value.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if this search should exist or not.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Global Admin Account

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificatePassword
Parameters.Description: Username can be made up to anything but password will be used for CertificatePassword

Parameters.Name: CertificatePath
Parameters.Description: Path to certificate used in service principal usually a PFX file.

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Description: This resource configures an Compliance Search (eDiscovery) in Security and Compliance.

Parameters.Name: Action
Parameters.Description: The Action parameter specifies what type of action to define. Accepted values are Export, Retention and Purge.

Parameters.Name: SearchName
Parameters.Description: The SearchName parameter specifies the name of the existing content search to associate with the content search action. You can specify multiple content searches separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: FileTypeExclusionsForUnindexedItems
Parameters.Description: The FileTypeExclusionsForUnindexedItems specifies the file types to exclude because they can't be indexed. You can specify multiple values separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: EnableDedupe
Parameters.Description: The EnableDedupe parameter eliminates duplication of messages when you export content search results.

Parameters.Name: IncludeCredential
Parameters.Description: The IncludeCredential switch specifies whether to include the credential in the results.

Parameters.Name: IncludeSharePointDocumentVersions
Parameters.Description: The IncludeSharePointDocumentVersions parameter specifies whether to export previous versions of the document when you use the Export switch.

Parameters.Name: PurgeType
Parameters.Description: The PurgeType parameter specifies how to remove items when the action is Purge.

Parameters.Name: RetryOnError
Parameters.Description: The RetryOnError switch specifies whether to retry the action on any items that failed without re-running the entire action all over again.

Parameters.Name: ActionScope
Parameters.Description: The ActionScope parameter specifies the items to include when the action is Export.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if this action should exist or not.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Exchange Global Admin

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificatePassword
Parameters.Description: Username can be made up to anything but password will be used for CertificatePassword

Parameters.Name: CertificatePath
Parameters.Description: Path to certificate used in service principal usually a PFX file.

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: FilePlanPropertyCitation

Parameters.Name: FilePlanPropertyReferenceId

Description: This resource configures a Compliance Tag in Security and Compliance.

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the complaiance tag.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if this rule should exist or not.

Parameters.Name: Comment
Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

Parameters.Name: EventType
Parameters.Description: The EventType parameter specifies the retention rule that's associated with the label.

Parameters.Name: IsRecordLabel
Parameters.Description: The IsRecordLabel parameter specifies whether the label is a record label.

Parameters.Name: Notes
Parameters.Description: The Notes parameter specifies an optional note. If you specify a value that contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks, for example: 'This is a user note'

Parameters.Name: Regulatory
Parameters.Description: Regulatory description

Parameters.Name: FilePlanProperty
Parameters.Description: The FilePlanProperty parameter specifies the file plan properties to include in the label.

Parameters.Name: ReviewerEmail
Parameters.Description: The ReviewerEmail parameter specifies the email address of a reviewer for Delete and KeepAndDelete retention actions. You can specify multiple email addresses separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: RetentionDuration
Parameters.Description: The RetentionDuration parameter specifies the hold duration for the retention rule. Valid values are: An integer - The hold duration in days, Unlimited - The content is held indefinitely.

Parameters.Name: RetentionAction
Parameters.Description: The RetentionAction parameter specifies the action for the label. Valid values are: Delete, Keep or KeepAndDelete.

Parameters.Name: RetentionType
Parameters.Description: The RetentionType parameter specifies whether the retention duration is calculated from the content creation date, tagged date, or last modification date. Valid values are: CreationAgeInDays, EventAgeInDays,ModificationAgeInDays, or TaggedAgeInDays.

Parameters.Description: Username can be made up to anything but password will be used for CertificatePassword

Parameters.Description: Path to certificate used in service principal usually a PFX file.

Description: This resource configures a Device Conditional Access Policy in Security and Compliance.

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: Name for the rule.

Parameters.Name: Policy
Parameters.Description: Name of the associated policy.

Parameters.Name: TargetGroups
Parameters.Description: The display names of the graoups targeted by the policy.

Parameters.Name: AccountName
Parameters.Description: The AccountName parameter specifies the account name.

Parameters.Name: AccountUserName
Parameters.Description: The AccountUserName parameter specifies the account user name.

Parameters.Name: AllowAppStore
Parameters.Description: The AllowAppStore parameter specifies whether to allow access to the app store on devices.

Parameters.Name: AllowAssistantWhileLocked
Parameters.Description: The AllowAssistantWhileLocked parameter specifies whether to allow the use of the voice assistant while devices are locked.

Parameters.Name: AllowConvenienceLogon
Parameters.Description: The AllowConvenienceLogon parameter specifies whether to allow convenience logons on devices.

Parameters.Name: AllowDiagnosticSubmission
Parameters.Description: The AllowDiagnosticSubmission parameter specifies whether to allow diagnostic submissions from devices.

Parameters.Name: AllowiCloudBackup
Parameters.Description: The AllowiCloudBackup parameter specifies whether to allow Apple iCloud Backup from devices.

Parameters.Name: AllowiCloudDocSync
Parameters.Description: The AllowiCloudDocSync parameter specifies whether to allow Apple iCloud Documents & Data sync on devices.

Parameters.Name: AllowiCloudPhotoSync
Parameters.Description: The AllowiCloudPhotoSync parameter specifies whether to allow Apple iCloud Photos sync on devices.

Parameters.Name: AllowJailbroken
Parameters.Description: The AllowJailbroken parameter specifies whether to allow access to your organization by jailbroken or rooted devices.

Parameters.Name: AllowPassbookWhileLocked
Parameters.Description: The AllowPassbookWhileLocked parameter specifies whether to allow the use of Apple Passbook while devices are locked.

Parameters.Name: AllowScreenshot
Parameters.Description: The AllowScreenshot parameter specifies whether to allow screenshots on devices.

Parameters.Name: AllowSimplePassword
Parameters.Description: The AllowSimplePassword parameter specifies whether to allow simple or non-complex passwords on devices.

Parameters.Name: AllowVideoConferencing
Parameters.Description: The AllowVideoConferencing parameter specifies whether to allow video conferencing on devices.

Parameters.Name: AllowVoiceAssistant
Parameters.Description: The AllowVoiceAssistant parameter specifies whether to allow using the voice assistant on devices.

Parameters.Name: AllowVoiceDialing
Parameters.Description: The AllowVoiceDialing parameter specifies whether to allow voice-activated telephone dialing.

Parameters.Name: AntiVirusSignatureStatus
Parameters.Description: The AntiVirusSignatureStatus parameter specifies the antivirus signature status.

Parameters.Name: AntiVirusStatus
Parameters.Description: The AntiVirusStatus parameter specifies the antivirus status.

Parameters.Name: AppsRating
Parameters.Description: The AppsRating parameter species the maximum or most restrictive rating of apps that are allowed on devices.

Parameters.Name: AutoUpdateStatus
Parameters.Description: The AutoUpdateStatus parameter specifies the update settings for devices.

Parameters.Name: BluetoothEnabled
Parameters.Description: The BluetoothEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable Bluetooth on devices.

Parameters.Name: CameraEnabled
Parameters.Description: The BluetoothEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable Bluetooth on devices.

Parameters.Name: EmailAddress
Parameters.Description: The EmailAddress parameter specifies the email address.

Parameters.Name: EnableRemovableStorage
Parameters.Description: The EnableRemovableStorage parameter specifies whether removable storage can be used by devices.

Parameters.Name: ExchangeActiveSyncHost
Parameters.Description: The ExchangeActiveSyncHost parameter specifies the Exchange ActiveSync host.

Parameters.Name: FirewallStatus
Parameters.Description: The FirewallStatus parameter specifies the acceptable firewall status values on devices.

Parameters.Name: ForceAppStorePassword
Parameters.Description: The ForceAppStorePassword parameter specifies whether to require a password to use the app store on devices.

Parameters.Name: ForceEncryptedBackup
Parameters.Description: The ForceEncryptedBackup parameter specifies whether to force encrypted backups for devices.

Parameters.Name: MaxPasswordAttemptsBeforeWipe
Parameters.Description: The MaxPasswordAttemptsBeforeWipe parameter specifies the number of incorrect password attempts that cause devices to be automatically wiped.

Parameters.Name: MaxPasswordGracePeriod
Parameters.Description: The MaxPasswordGracePeriod parameter specifies the length of time users are allowed to reset expired passwords on devices.

Parameters.Name: MoviesRating
Parameters.Description: The MoviesRating parameter species the maximum or most restrictive rating of movies that are allowed on devices. You specify the country/region rating system to use with the RegionRatings parameter.

Parameters.Name: PasswordComplexity
Parameters.Description: The PasswordComplexity parameter specifies the password complexity.

Parameters.Name: PasswordExpirationDays
Parameters.Description: The PasswordExpirationDays parameter specifies the number of days that the same password can be used on devices before users are required to change their passwords.

Parameters.Name: PasswordHistoryCount
Parameters.Description: The PasswordHistoryCount parameter specifies the minimum number of unique new passwords that are required on devices before an old password can be reused.

Parameters.Name: PasswordMinComplexChars
Parameters.Description: The PasswordMinComplexChars parameter specifies the minimum number of complex characters that are required for device passwords. A complex character isn't a letter.

Parameters.Name: PasswordMinimumLength
Parameters.Description: The PasswordMinimumLength parameter specifies the minimum number of characters that are required for device passwords.

Parameters.Name: PasswordQuality
Parameters.Description: The PasswordQuality parameter specifies the minimum password quality rating that's required for device passwords. Password quality is a numeric scale that indicates the security and complexity of the password. A higher quality value indicates a more secure password.

Parameters.Name: PasswordRequired
Parameters.Description: The PasswordRequired parameter specifies whether a password is required to access devices.

Parameters.Name: PasswordTimeout
Parameters.Description: The PasswordTimeout parameter specifies the length of time that devices can be inactive before a password is required to reactivate them.

Parameters.Name: PhoneMemoryEncrypted
Parameters.Description: The PhoneMemoryEncrypted parameter specifies whether to encrypt the memory on devices.

Parameters.Name: RegionRatings
Parameters.Description: The RegionRatings parameter specifies the rating system (country/region) to use for movie and television ratings with the MoviesRating and TVShowsRating parameters.

Parameters.Name: RequireEmailProfile
Parameters.Description: The RequireEmailProfile parameter specifies whether an email profile is required on devices.

Parameters.Name: SmartScreenEnabled
Parameters.Description: The SmartScreenEnabled parameter specifies whether to requireWindows SmartScreen on devices.

Parameters.Name: SystemSecurityTLS
Parameters.Description: The SystemSecurityTLS parameter specifies whether TLS encryption is used on devices.

Parameters.Name: TVShowsRating
Parameters.Description: The TVShowsRating parameter species the maximum or most restrictive rating of television shows that are allowed on devices. You specify the country/region rating system to use with the RegionRatings parameter.

Parameters.Name: UserAccountControlStatus
Parameters.Description: The UserAccountControlStatus parameter specifies how User Account Control messages are presented on devices.

Parameters.Name: WLANEnabled
Parameters.Description: The WLANEnabled parameter specifies whether Wi-Fi is enabled devices.

Parameters.Name: WorkFoldersSyncUrl
Parameters.Description: The WorkFoldersSyncUrl parameter specifies the URL that's used to synchronize company data on devices.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Present ensures the instance exists, absent ensures it is removed.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the workload's Admin

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity
Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.
