Parameters.Description: The BluetoothEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable Bluetooth on devices.

Parameters.Name: EmailAddress
Parameters.Description: The EmailAddress parameter specifies the email address.

Parameters.Name: EnableRemovableStorage
Parameters.Description: The EnableRemovableStorage parameter specifies whether removable storage can be used by devices.

Parameters.Name: ExchangeActiveSyncHost
Parameters.Description: The ExchangeActiveSyncHost parameter specifies the Exchange ActiveSync host.

Parameters.Name: FirewallStatus
Parameters.Description: The FirewallStatus parameter specifies the acceptable firewall status values on devices.

Parameters.Name: ForceAppStorePassword
Parameters.Description: The ForceAppStorePassword parameter specifies whether to require a password to use the app store on devices.

Parameters.Name: ForceEncryptedBackup
Parameters.Description: The ForceEncryptedBackup parameter specifies whether to force encrypted backups for devices.

Parameters.Name: MaxPasswordAttemptsBeforeWipe
Parameters.Description: The MaxPasswordAttemptsBeforeWipe parameter specifies the number of incorrect password attempts that cause devices to be automatically wiped.

Parameters.Name: MaxPasswordGracePeriod
Parameters.Description: The MaxPasswordGracePeriod parameter specifies the length of time users are allowed to reset expired passwords on devices.

Parameters.Name: MoviesRating
Parameters.Description: The MoviesRating parameter species the maximum or most restrictive rating of movies that are allowed on devices. You specify the country/region rating system to use with the RegionRatings parameter.

Parameters.Name: PasswordComplexity
Parameters.Description: The PasswordComplexity parameter specifies the password complexity.

Parameters.Name: PasswordExpirationDays
Parameters.Description: The PasswordExpirationDays parameter specifies the number of days that the same password can be used on devices before users are required to change their passwords.

Parameters.Name: PasswordHistoryCount
Parameters.Description: The PasswordHistoryCount parameter specifies the minimum number of unique new passwords that are required on devices before an old password can be reused.

Parameters.Name: PasswordMinComplexChars
Parameters.Description: The PasswordMinComplexChars parameter specifies the minimum number of complex characters that are required for device passwords. A complex character isn't a letter.

Parameters.Name: PasswordMinimumLength
Parameters.Description: The PasswordMinimumLength parameter specifies the minimum number of characters that are required for device passwords.

Parameters.Name: PasswordQuality
Parameters.Description: The PasswordQuality parameter specifies the minimum password quality rating that's required for device passwords. Password quality is a numeric scale that indicates the security and complexity of the password. A higher quality value indicates a more secure password.

Parameters.Name: PasswordRequired
Parameters.Description: The PasswordRequired parameter specifies whether a password is required to access devices.

Parameters.Name: PasswordTimeout
Parameters.Description: The PasswordTimeout parameter specifies the length of time that devices can be inactive before a password is required to reactivate them.

Parameters.Name: PhoneMemoryEncrypted
Parameters.Description: The PhoneMemoryEncrypted parameter specifies whether to encrypt the memory on devices.

Parameters.Name: RegionRatings
Parameters.Description: The RegionRatings parameter specifies the rating system (country/region) to use for movie and television ratings with the MoviesRating and TVShowsRating parameters.

Parameters.Name: RequireEmailProfile
Parameters.Description: The RequireEmailProfile parameter specifies whether an email profile is required on devices.

Parameters.Name: SmartScreenEnabled
Parameters.Description: The SmartScreenEnabled parameter specifies whether to requireWindows SmartScreen on devices.

Parameters.Name: SystemSecurityTLS
Parameters.Description: The SystemSecurityTLS parameter specifies whether TLS encryption is used on devices.

Parameters.Name: TVShowsRating
Parameters.Description: The TVShowsRating parameter species the maximum or most restrictive rating of television shows that are allowed on devices. You specify the country/region rating system to use with the RegionRatings parameter.

Parameters.Name: UserAccountControlStatus
Parameters.Description: The UserAccountControlStatus parameter specifies how User Account Control messages are presented on devices.

Parameters.Name: WLANEnabled
Parameters.Description: The WLANEnabled parameter specifies whether Wi-Fi is enabled devices.

Parameters.Name: WorkFoldersSyncUrl
Parameters.Description: The WorkFoldersSyncUrl parameter specifies the URL that's used to synchronize company data on devices.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Present ensures the instance exists, absent ensures it is removed.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the workload's Admin

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity
Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Description: This resource configures a Device Configuration Rule in Purview.

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the DLP policy. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Parameters.Name: Comment
Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

Parameters.Name: EndpointDlpLocation
Parameters.Description: The EndpointDLPLocation parameter specifies the user accounts to include in the DLP policy for Endpoint DLP when they are logged on to an onboarded device. You identify the account by name or email address. You can use the value All to include all user accounts.

Parameters.Name: EndpointDlpLocationException
Parameters.Description: The EndpointDlpLocationException parameter specifies the user accounts to exclude from Endpoint DLP when you use the value All for the EndpointDlpLocation parameter. You identify the account by name or email address.

Parameters.Name: OnPremisesScannerDlpLocation
Parameters.Description: The OnPremisesScannerDlpLocation parameter specifies the on-premises file shares and SharePoint document libraries and folders to include in the DLP policy. You can use the value All to include all on-premises file shares and SharePoint document libraries and folders.

Parameters.Name: OnPremisesScannerDlpLocationException
Parameters.Description: The OnPremisesScannerDlpLocationException parameter specifies the on-premises file shares and SharePoint document libraries and folders to exclude from the DLP policy if you use the value All for the OnPremisesScannerDlpLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: PowerBIDlpLocation
Parameters.Description: The PowerBIDlpLocation parameter specifies the Power BI workspace IDs to include in the DLP policy. Only workspaces hosted in Premium Gen2 capacities are permitted. You can use the value All to include all supported workspaces.

Parameters.Name: PowerBIDlpLocationException
Parameters.Description: The PowerBIDlpLocationException parameter specifies the Power BI workspace IDs to exclude from the DLP policy when you use the value All for the PowerBIDlpLocation parameter. Only workspaces hosted in Premium Gen2 capacities are permitted.

Parameters.Name: ThirdPartyAppDlpLocation
Parameters.Description: The ThirdPartyAppDlpLocation parameter specifies the non-Microsoft cloud apps to include in the DLP policy. You can use the value All to include all connected apps.

Parameters.Name: ThirdPartyAppDlpLocationException
Parameters.Description: The ThirdPartyAppDlpLocationException parameter specifies the non-Microsoft cloud apps to exclude from the DLP policy when you use the value All for the ThirdPartyAppDlpLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: ExchangeLocation
Parameters.Description: The ExchangeLocation parameter specifies Exchange Online mailboxes to include in the DLP policy. You can only use the value All for this parameter to include all mailboxes.

Parameters.Name: ExchangeSenderMemberOf
Parameters.Description: Exchange members to include.

Parameters.Name: ExchangeSenderMemberOfException
Parameters.Description: Exchange members to exclude.

Parameters.Name: Mode
Parameters.Description: The Mode parameter specifies the action and notification level of the DLP policy. Valid values are: Enable, TestWithNotifications, TestWithoutNotifications, Disable and PendingDeletion.

Parameters.Name: OneDriveLocation
Parameters.Description: The OneDriveLocation parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to include. You identify the site by its URL value, or you can use the value All to include all sites.

Parameters.Name: OneDriveLocationException
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to exclude when you use the value All for the OneDriveLocation parameter. You identify the site by its URL value.

Parameters.Name: Priority
Parameters.Description: Priority for the Policy.

Parameters.Name: SharePointLocation
Parameters.Description: The SharePointLocation parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to include. You identify the site by its URL value, or you can use the value All to include all sites.

Parameters.Name: SharePointLocationException
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to exclude when you use the value All for the SharePointLocation parameter. You identify the site by its URL value.

Parameters.Name: TeamsLocation
Parameters.Description: Teams locations to include

Parameters.Name: TeamsLocationException
Parameters.Description: Teams locations to exclude.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if this policy should exist or not.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Exchange Global Admin

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificatePassword
Parameters.Description: Username can be made up to anything but password will be used for CertificatePassword

Parameters.Name: CertificatePath
Parameters.Description: Path to certificate used in service principal usually a PFX file.

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Description:
This resource configures a Data Loss Prevention Compliance
Policy in Security and Compliance Center.

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: Name of the Rule.

Parameters.Name: Policy
Parameters.Description: Name of the associated DLP Compliance Policy.

Parameters.Name: AccessScope
Parameters.Description: The AccessScope parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that's based on the access scope of the content. The rule is applied to content that matches the specified access scope.

Parameters.Name: BlockAccess
Parameters.Description: The BlockAccess parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that blocks access to the source item when the conditions of the rule are met. $true: Blocks further access to the source item that matched the rule. The owner, author, and site owner can still access the item. $false: Allows access to the source item that matched the rule. This is the default value.

Parameters.Name: BlockAccessScope
Parameters.Description: The BlockAccessScope parameter specifies the scope of the block access action.

Parameters.Name: Comment
Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment. If you specify a value that contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Parameters.Name: AdvancedRule
Parameters.Description: The AdvancedRule parameter uses complex rule syntax that supports multiple AND, OR, and NOT operators and nested groups

Parameters.Name: ContentContainsSensitiveInformation
Parameters.Description: The ContentContainsSensitiveInformation parameter specifies a condition for the rule that's based on a sensitive information type match in content. The rule is applied to content that contains the specified sensitive information type.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfContentContainsSensitiveInformation
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfContentContainsSensitiveInformation parameter specifies an exception for the rule that's based on a sensitive information type match in content. The rule isn't applied to content that contains the specified sensitive information type.

Parameters.Name: ContentPropertyContainsWords
Parameters.Description: The ContentPropertyContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that's based on a property match in content. The rule is applied to content that contains the specified property.

Parameters.Name: Disabled
Parameters.Description: The Disabled parameter specifies whether the DLP rule is disabled.

Parameters.Name: GenerateAlert
Parameters.Description: The GenerateAlert parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that notifies the specified users when the conditions of the rule are met.

Parameters.Name: GenerateIncidentReport
Parameters.Description: The GenerateIncidentReport parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that sends an incident report to the specified users when the conditions of the rule are met.

Parameters.Name: IncidentReportContent
Parameters.Description: The IncidentReportContent parameter specifies the content to include in the report when you use the GenerateIncidentReport parameter.

Parameters.Name: NotifyAllowOverride
Parameters.Description: The NotifyAllowOverride parameter specifies the notification override options when the conditions of the rule are met.

Parameters.Name: NotifyEmailCustomText
Parameters.Description: The NotifyEmailCustomText parameter specifies the custom text in the email notification message that's sent to recipients when the conditions of the rule are met.

Parameters.Name: NotifyPolicyTipCustomText
Parameters.Description: The NotifyPolicyTipCustomText parameter specifies the custom text in the Policy Tip notification message that's shown to recipients when the conditions of the rule are met. The maximum length is 256 characters. HTML tags and tokens (variables) aren't supported.

Parameters.Name: NotifyUser
Parameters.Description: The NotifyUser parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that notifies the specified users when the conditions of the rule are met.

Parameters.Name: ReportSeverityLevel
Parameters.Description: The ReportSeverityLevel parameter specifies the severity level of the incident report for content detections based on the rule.

Parameters.Name: RuleErrorAction
Parameters.Description: The RuleErrorAction parameter specifies what to do if an error is encountered during the evaluation of the rule.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if this rule should exist or not.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Exchange Global Admin

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificatePassword
Parameters.Description: Username can be made up to anything but password will be used for CertificatePassword

Parameters.Name: CertificatePath
Parameters.Description: Path to certificate used in service principal usually a PFX file.

Parameters.Name: AnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords
Parameters.Description: The AnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in recipient email addresses.

Parameters.Name: AnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The AnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in recipient email addresses by using regular expressions.

Parameters.Name: RemoveRMSTemplate
Parameters.Description: The RemoveRMSTemplate parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that removes Office 365 Message Encryption from messages and their attachments.

Parameters.Name: StopPolicyProcessing
Parameters.Description: The StopPolicyProcessing parameter specifies an action that stops processing more DLP policy rules.

Parameters.Name: DocumentIsUnsupported
Parameters.Description: The DocumentIsUnsupported parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for files that can't be scanned.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfDocumentIsUnsupported
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfDocumentIsUnsupported parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for files that can't be scanned.

Parameters.Name: HasSenderOverride
Parameters.Description: The SenderOverride parameter specifies a condition for the rule that looks for messages where the sender chose to override a DLP policy.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfHasSenderOverride
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfHasSenderOverride parameter specifies an exception for the rule that looks for messages where the sender chose to override a DLP policy.

Parameters.Name: ProcessingLimitExceeded
Parameters.Description: The ProcessingLimitExceeded parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for files where scanning couldn't complete.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfProcessingLimitExceeded
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfProcessingLimitExceeded parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for files where scanning couldn't complete.

Parameters.Name: DocumentIsPasswordProtected
Parameters.Description: The DocumentIsPasswordProtected parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for password protected files (because the contents of the file can't be inspected). Password detection only works for Office documents and .zip files.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfDocumentIsPasswordProtected
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfDocumentIsPasswordProtected parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for password protected files (because the contents of the file can't be inspected). Password detection only works for Office documents and .zip files.

Parameters.Name: MessageTypeMatches
Parameters.Description: The MessageTypeMatches parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for types of SMIME message patterns.

Parameters.Name: FromScope
Parameters.Description: The FromScope parameter specifies whether messages from inside or outside the organisation are in scope for the DLP rule.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfFromScope
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfFromScope parameter specifies whether messages from inside or outside the organisation are in scope for the DLP rule.

Parameters.Name: SubjectContainsWords
Parameters.Description: The SubjectContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the Subject field of messages. You can specify multiple words or phrases separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: SubjectMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The SubjectMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field of messages by using regular expressions.

Parameters.Name: SubjectOrBodyContainsWords
Parameters.Description: The SubjectOrBodyContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the rule that looks for words in the Subject field or body of messages.

Parameters.Name: SubjectOrBodyMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The SubjectOrBodyMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field or body of messages.

Parameters.Name: ContentCharacterSetContainsWords
Parameters.Description: The ContentCharacterSetContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the rule that looks for character set names in messages. You can specify multiple values separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: DocumentNameMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The DocumentNameMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the name of message attachments by using regular expressions.

Parameters.Name: DocumentNameMatchesWords
Parameters.Description: The DocumentNameMatchesWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the name of message attachments.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords
Parameters.Description: he ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in recipient email addresses.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in recipient email addresses by using regular expressions.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfContentCharacterSetContainsWords
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfContentCharacterSetContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the rule that looks for character set names in messages.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfContentPropertyContainsWords
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfContentPropertyContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that's based on a property match in content.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfDocumentNameMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfDocumentNameMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the name of message attachments by using regular expressions.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfDocumentNameMatchesWords
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfDocumentNameMatchesWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the name of message attachments.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfFromAddressContainsWords
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfFromAddressContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the sender's email address.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfFromAddressMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfFromAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the sender's email address by using regular expressions.

Parameters.Name: FromAddressContainsWords
Parameters.Description: The FromAddressContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the sender's email address.

Parameters.Name: FromAddressMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The FromAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the sender's email address by using regular expressions.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfMessageTypeMatches
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfMessageTypeMatches parameter specifies an exception for the rule that looks for messages of the specified type.

Parameters.Name: RecipientDomainIs
Parameters.Description: The RecipientDomainIs parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for recipients with email addresses in the specified domains.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for recipients with email addresses in the specified domains.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSenderDomainIs
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSenderDomainIs parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for messages from senders with email address in the specified domains.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSenderIPRanges
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSenderIpRanges parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for senders whose IP addresses matches the specified value, or fall within the specified ranges.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSentTo
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSentTo parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for recipients in messages. You identify the recipients by email address.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSubjectContainsWords
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSubjectContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the Subject field of messages.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSubjectMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSubjectMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field of messages by using regular expressions.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSubjectOrBodyContainsWords
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSubjectOrBodyContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the rule that looks for words in the Subject field or body of messages.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSubjectOrBodyMatchesPatterns
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSubjectOrBodyMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field or body of messages.

Parameters.Name: DocumentContainsWords
Parameters.Description: The DocumentContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words in message attachments. Only supported attachment types are checked.

Parameters.Name: SentToMemberOf
Parameters.Description: The SentToMemberOf parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for messages sent to members of distribution groups, dynamic distribution groups, or mail-enabled security groups.

Parameters.Name: ContentIsNotLabeled
Parameters.Description: The ContentIsNotLabeled parameter specifies if the content is labeled. A True or False condition.

Parameters.Name: SetHeader
Parameters.Description: The SetHeader The SetHeader parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that adds or modifies a header field and value in the message header. You can specify multiple header name and value pairs separated by commas

Parameters.Name: ContentExtensionMatchesWords
Parameters.Description: The ContentExtensionMatchesWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words in file name extensions. You can specify multiple words separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: ExceptIfContentExtensionMatchesWords
Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfContentExtensionMatchesWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for words in file name extensions. You can specify multiple words separated by commas.

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: IRASettingsEnabled
Parameters.Description: When turned on, we'll scan sources in your org (such as the Microsoft 365 audit log) to detect the same activities used by insider risk policies. Scans run daily and provide real-time insights that can help you set up and refine policies to ensure you're detecting the most relevant activities.

Parameters.Name: Anonymization
Parameters.Description: For users who perform activities matching your insider risk policies, decide whether to show their actual names or use pseudonymized versions to mask their identities.

Parameters.Name: DLPUserRiskSync
Parameters.Description: When turned on, admins with the correct permissions will be able to review user risk details from Insider Risk Management within other solutions such as Data Loss Prevention (DLP), Communication Compliance, and user entity pages in Microsoft Defender.

Parameters.Name: OptInIRMDataExport
Parameters.Description: When turned on, admins with the correct permissions will be able to review user risk details from Insider Risk Management within other solutions such as Data Loss Prevention (DLP), Communication Compliance, and user entity pages in Microsoft Defender.

Description: This resource configures a Sensitivity label policy in Security and Compliance.

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies the unique name for the sensitivity label. The maximum length is 64 characters. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if this label policy should exist or not.

Parameters.Name: Comment
Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

Parameters.Name: AdvancedSettings
Parameters.Description: The AdvancedSettings parameter enables client-specific features and capabilities on the sensitivity label. The settings that you configure with this parameter only affect apps that are designed for the setting.

Parameters.Name: ExchangeLocation
Parameters.Description: The ExchangeLocation parameter specifies the mailboxes to include in the policy.

Parameters.Name: ExchangeLocationException
Parameters.Description: The ExchangeLocationException parameter specifies the mailboxes to exclude when you use the value All for the ExchangeLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: ModernGroupLocation
Parameters.Description: The ModernGroupLocation parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to include in the policy.

Parameters.Name: ModernGroupLocationException
Parameters.Description: The ModernGroupLocationException parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to exclude when you're using the value All for the ModernGroupLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: Labels
Parameters.Description: The Labels parameter specifies the sensitivity labels that are associated with the policy. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the label.

Parameters.Name: AddExchangeLocation
Parameters.Description: The AddExchangeLocation parameter specifies the mailboxes to add in the existing policy.

Parameters.Name: AddExchangeLocationException
Parameters.Description: The AddExchangeLocationException parameter specifies the mailboxes to add to exclusions when you use the value All for the ExchangeLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: AddModernGroupLocation
Parameters.Description: The AddModernGroupLocation parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to add to include the policy.

Parameters.Name: AddModernGroupLocationException
Parameters.Description: The AddModernGroupLocationException parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to add to exclusions when you're using the value All for the ModernGroupLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: AddLabels
Parameters.Description: The AddLabels parameter specifies the sensitivity labels to add to the policy. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the label.

Parameters.Name: RemoveExchangeLocation
Parameters.Description: The RemoveExchangeLocation parameter specifies the mailboxes to remove from the policy.

Parameters.Name: RemoveExchangeLocationException
Parameters.Description: The RemoveExchangeLocationException parameter specifies the mailboxes to remove when you use the value All for the ExchangeLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: RemoveModernGroupLocation
Parameters.Description: The RemoveModernGroupLocation parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to remove from the policy.

Parameters.Name: RemoveModernGroupLocationException
Parameters.Description: The RemoveModernGroupLocationException parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to remove from excluded values when you're using the value All for the ModernGroupLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: RemoveLabels
Parameters.Description: The RemoveLabels parameter specifies the sensitivity labels that are removed from the policy. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the label.

Parameters.Name: Credential
Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Exchange Global Admin

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.

Parameters.Name: TenantId
Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint
Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.

Parameters.Name: CertificatePassword
Parameters.Description: Username can be made up to anything but password will be used for CertificatePassword

Parameters.Name: CertificatePath
Parameters.Description: Path to certificate used in service principal usually a PFX file.

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Parameters.Name: AlertBy
Parameters.Description: Specifies the scope for aggregated alert policies

Parameters.Name: AlertFor
Parameters.Description: This parameter is reserved for internal Microsoft use

Parameters.Name: AggregationType
Parameters.Description: Specifies how the alert policy triggers alerts for multiple occurrences of monitored activity

Parameters.Name: Category
Parameters.Description: Specifies a category for the alert policy

Parameters.Name: Comment
Parameters.Description: Specifies an optional comment

Parameters.Name: Disabled
Parameters.Description: Enables or disables the alert policy

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if this alert should exist or not.

Parameters.Name: Filter
Parameters.Description: The Filter parameter uses OPATH syntax to filter the results by the specified properties and values

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: Specifies the unique name for the alert policy

Parameters.Name: NotificationCulture
Parameters.Description: Specifies the language or locale that's used for notifications. For example, da-DK for Danish

Parameters.Name: NotificationEnabled
Parameters.Description: NotificationEnabled true or false

Parameters.Name: NotifyUserOnFilterMatch
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether to trigger an alert for a single event when the alert policy is configured for aggregated activity

Parameters.Name: NotifyUserSuppressionExpiryDate
Parameters.Description: Specifies whether to temporarily suspend notifications for the alert policy. Until the specified date-time, no notifications are sent for detected activities.

Parameters.Name: NotifyUserThrottleThreshold
Parameters.Description: Specifies the maximum number of notifications for the alert policy within the time period specified by the NotifyUserThrottleWindow parameter. Once the maximum number of notifications has been reached in the time period, no more notifications are sent for the alert.

Parameters.Name: NotifyUserThrottleWindow
Parameters.Description: Specifies the time interval in minutes that's used by the NotifyUserThrottleThreshold parameter

Parameters.Name: NotifyUser
Parameters.Description: Specifies the SMTP address of the user who receives notification messages for the alert policy. You can specify multiple values separated by commas

Parameters.Name: Operation

Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

Parameters.Description: Location of the dynamic scope for this policy.

Parameters.Description: Determines if the policy is enabled or not.

Parameters.Name: ExchangeLocation
Parameters.Description: The ExchangeLocation parameter specifies the mailboxes to include.

Parameters.Name: ExchangeLocationException
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies the mailboxes to remove from the list of excluded mailboxes when you use the value All for the ExchangeLocation parameter

Parameters.Name: ModernGroupLocation
Parameters.Description: The ModernGroupLocation parameter specifies the Office 365 groups to include in the policy.

Parameters.Name: ModernGroupLocationException
Parameters.Description: The ModernGroupLocationException parameter specifies the Office 365 groups to exclude when you're using the value All for the ModernGroupLocation parameter.

Parameters.Name: OneDriveLocation
Parameters.Description: The OneDriveLocation parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to include. You identify the site by its URL value, or you can use the value All to include all sites.

Parameters.Name: OneDriveLocationException
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to exclude when you use the value All for the OneDriveLocation parameter. You identify the site by its URL value.

Parameters.Name: PublicFolderLocation
Parameters.Description: The PublicFolderLocation parameter specifies that you want to include all public folders in the retention policy. You use the value All for this parameter.

Parameters.Name: RestrictiveRetention
Parameters.Description: The RestrictiveRetention parameter specifies whether Preservation Lock is enabled for the policy.

Parameters.Name: SharePointLocation
Parameters.Description: The SharePointLocation parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to include. You identify the site by its URL value, or you can use the value All to include all sites.

Parameters.Name: SharePointLocationException
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to exclude when you use the value All for the SharePointLocation parameter. You identify the site by its URL value.

Parameters.Name: SkypeLocation
Parameters.Description: The SkypeLocation parameter specifies the Skype for Business Online users to include in the policy.

Parameters.Name: SkypeLocationException
Parameters.Description: This parameter is reserved for internal Microsoft use.

Parameters.Name: TeamsChannelLocation
Parameters.Description: The TeamsChannelLocation parameter specifies the Teams Channel to include in the policy.

Parameters.Name: TeamsChannelLocationException
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to exclude when you use the value All for the TeamsChannelLocation parameter. You identify the site by its URL value.

Parameters.Name: TeamsChatLocation
Parameters.Description: The TeamsChatLocation parameter specifies the Teams Chat to include in the policy.

Parameters.Name: TeamsChatLocationException
Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to exclude when you use the value All for the TeamsChatLocation parameter. You identify the site by its URL value.

Description: This resource configures a Retention Compliance Policy in Security and Compliance.

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the retention rule.

Parameters.Name: Policy
Parameters.Description: The Policy parameter specifies the policy to contain the rule.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if this rule should exist or not.

Parameters.Name: Comment
Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

Parameters.Name: ExpirationDateOption
Parameters.Description: The ExpirationDateOption parameter specifies whether the expiration date is calculated from the content creation date or last modification date. Valid values are: CreationAgeInDays and ModificationAgeInDays.

Parameters.Name: ExcludedItemClasses
Parameters.Description: The ExcludedItemClasses parameter specifies the types of messages to exclude from the rule. You can use this parameter only to exclude items from a hold policy, which excludes the specified item class from being held. Using this parameter won't exclude items from deletion policies. Typically, you use this parameter to exclude voicemail messages, IM conversations, and other Skype for Business Online content from being held by a hold policy.

Parameters.Name: ContentMatchQuery
Parameters.Description: The ContentMatchQuery parameter specifies a content search filter.

Parameters.Name: RetentionComplianceAction
Parameters.Description: The RetentionComplianceAction parameter specifies the retention action for the rule. Valid values are: Delete, Keep and KeepAndDelete.

Parameters.Name: RetentionDuration
Parameters.Description: The RetentionDuration parameter specifies the hold duration for the retention rule. Valid values are: An integer - The hold duration in days, Unlimited - The content is held indefinitely.

Parameters.Name: RetentionDurationDisplayHint
Parameters.Description: The RetentionDurationDisplayHint parameter specifies the units that are used to display the retention duration in the Security and Compliance Center. Valid values are: Days, Months or Years.

Description: This resource configures Sensitivity labels in Security and Compliance.

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies the unique name for the sensitivity label. The maximum length is 64 characters. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Specify if this rule should exist or not.

Parameters.Name: Comment
Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

Parameters.Name: AdvancedSettings
Parameters.Description: The AdvancedSettings parameter enables client-specific features and capabilities on the sensitivity label. The settings that you configure with this parameter only affect apps that are designed for the setting.

Parameters.Name: DisplayName
Parameters.Description: The DisplayName parameter specifies the display name for the sensitivity label. The display name appears in the Microsoft Office and is used by Outlook users to select the appropriate sensitivity label before they send a message.

Parameters.Name: LocaleSettings
Parameters.Description: The LocaleSettings parameter specifies one or more localized label name or label Tooltips in different languages. Regions include all region codes supported in Office Client applications.

Parameters.Name: ParentId
Parameters.Description: The ParentId parameter specifies the parent label that you want this label to be under (a sublabel). You can use any value that uniquely identifies the parent sensitivity label for example name.

Parameters.Name: Priority
Parameters.Description: The Priority parameter specifies a priority value for the sensitivity label that determines the order of label processing. A lower integer value indicates a higher priority.

Parameters.Name: Tooltip
Parameters.Description: The ToolTip parameter specifies the default tooltip and sensitivity label description that's seen by users. It the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingFooterAlignment
Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterAlignment parameter specifies the footer alignment.

Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingFooterEnabled
Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable the sensitivity label.

Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingFooterFontColor
Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterFontColor parameter specifies the color of the footer text. This parameter accepts a hexadecimal color code value in the format #xxxxxx. The default value is #000000.

Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingFooterFontSize
Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterFontSize parameter specifies the font size (in points) of the footer text.

Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingFooterMargin
Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterMargin parameter specifies the size (in points) of the footer margin.

Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingFooterText
Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterText parameter specifies the footer text. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingHeaderAlignment
Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderAlignment parameter specifies the header alignment.

Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingHeaderEnabled
Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderEnabled parameter enables or disables the Apply Content Marking Header action for the label.

Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingHeaderFontColor
Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderFontColor parameter specifies the color of the header text. This parameter accepts a hexadecimal color code value in the format #xxxxxx. The default value is #000000.

Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingHeaderFontSize
Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderFontSize parameter specifies the font size (in points) of the header text.

Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingHeaderMargin
Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderMargin parameter specifies the size (in points) of the header margin.

Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingHeaderText
Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderText parameter specifies the header text. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Parameters.Name: ApplyWaterMarkingEnabled
Parameters.Description: The ApplyWaterMarkingEnabled parameter enables or disables the Apply Watermarking Header action for the label.

Parameters.Name: ApplyWaterMarkingFontColor
Parameters.Description: The ApplyWaterMarkingFontColor parameter specifies the color of the watermark text. This parameter accepts a hexadecimal color code value in the format #xxxxxx.

Parameters.Name: ApplyWaterMarkingFontSize
Parameters.Description: The ApplyWaterMarkingFontSize parameter specifies the font size (in points) of the watermark text.

Parameters.Name: ApplyWaterMarkingLayout
Parameters.Description: The ApplyWaterMarkingAlignment parameter specifies the watermark alignment.

Parameters.Name: ApplyWaterMarkingText
Parameters.Description: The ApplyWaterMarkingText parameter specifies the watermark text. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Parameters.Name: ContentType
Parameters.Description: The ContentType parameter specifies where the sensitivity label can be applied.

Parameters.Name: EncryptionContentExpiredOnDateInDaysOrNever
Parameters.Description: The EncryptionContentExpiredOnDateInDaysOrNever parameter specifies when the encrypted content expires. Valid values are integer or never.

Parameters.Name: EncryptionDoNotForward
Parameters.Description: The EncryptionDoNotForward parameter specifies whether the Do Not Forward template is applied.

Parameters.Name: EncryptionEncryptOnly
Parameters.Description: The EncryptionEncryptOnly parameter specifies whether the encrypt-only template is applied.

Parameters.Name: EncryptionEnabled
Parameters.Description: The EncryptionEnabled parameter specifies whether encryption in enabled.

Parameters.Name: EncryptionOfflineAccessDays
Parameters.Description: The EncryptionOfflineAccessDays parameter specifies the number of days that offline access is allowed.

Parameters.Name: EncryptionPromptUser
Parameters.Description: The EncryptionPromptUser parameter specifies whether to set the label with user defined permission in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.

Parameters.Name: EncryptionProtectionType
Parameters.Description: The EncryptionProtectionType parameter specifies the protection type for encryption.

Parameters.Name: EncryptionRightsDefinitions
Parameters.Description: The EncryptionRightsDefinitions parameter specifies the rights users have when accessing protected. This parameter uses the syntax Identity1:Rights1,Rights2;Identity2:Rights3,Rights4. For example, john@contoso.com:VIEW,EDIT;microsoft.com:VIEW.

Parameters.Name: EncryptionRightsUrl
Parameters.Description: The EncryptionRightsUrl parameter specifies the URL for hold your own key (HYOK) protection.

Parameters.Name: SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowAccessToGuestUsers
Parameters.Description: The SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowAccessToGuestUsers parameter enables or disables access to guest users.

Parameters.Name: SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowEmailFromGuestUsers
Parameters.Description: The SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowEmailFromGuestUsers parameter enables or disables email from guest users.

Parameters.Name: SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowFullAccess
Parameters.Description: The SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowFullAccess parameter enables or disables full access.

Parameters.Name: SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowLimitedAccess
Parameters.Description: The SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowLimitedAccess parameter enables or disables limited access.

Parameters.Name: SiteAndGroupProtectionBlockAccess
Parameters.Description: The SiteAndGroupProtectionBlockAccess parameter blocks access.

Parameters.Name: SiteAndGroupProtectionEnabled
Parameters.Description: The SiteAndGroupProtectionEnabled parameter enables or disables the Site and Group Protection action for the labels.

Parameters.Name: SiteAndGroupProtectionPrivacy
Parameters.Description: The SiteAndGroupProtectionPrivacy parameter specifies the privacy level for the label.

Parameters.Name: SiteAndGroupExternalSharingControlType
Parameters.Description: The SiteAndGroupExternalSharingControlType parameter specifies the external user sharing setting for the label.

Parameters.Name: AutoLabelingSettings
Parameters.Description: The AutoLabelingSettings parameter specifies the conditions for label to be automatically applied to files and emails.

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: The Name of the Managed Property

Parameters.Name: Type
Parameters.Description: The Type of the Managed Property

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: Description of the Managed Property

Parameters.Name: Searchable
Parameters.Description: Enables querying against the content of the managed property.  The content of this managed property is included in the full-text index. For example, if the property is 'author', a simple query for 'Smith' returns items containing the word 'Smith' and items whose author property contains 'Smith'.

Parameters.Name: FullTextIndex
Parameters.Description: Defines which full-text index the Managed Property is stored in.

Parameters.Name: FullTextContext
Parameters.Description: Defines the context of a managed property within its full-text index.

Parameters.Name: Queryable
Parameters.Description: Enables querying against the specific Managed Property. The Managed Property field name must be included in the query, either specified in the query itself or included in the query programmatically. If the Managed Property is 'author', the query must contain 'author:Smith'.

Parameters.Name: Retrievable
Parameters.Description: Enables the content of this managed property to be returned in search results. Enable this setting for managed properties that are relevant to present in search results.

Parameters.Name: AllowMultipleValues
Parameters.Description: Allow multiple values of the same type in this managed property. For example, if this is the 'author' managed property, and a document has multiple authors, each author name will be stored as a separate value in this managed property.

Parameters.Name: Refinable
Parameters.Description: Yes: Enables using the property as a refiner for search results in the front end. You must manually configure the refiner in the web part. Yes - latent: Enables switching refinable to active later, without having to do a full re-crawl when you switch. Both options require a full crawl to take effect.

Parameters.Name: Sortable
Parameters.Description: Yes: Enables sorting the result set based on the property before the result set is returned. Use for example for large result sets that cannot be sorted and retrieved at the same time. Yes - latent: Enables switching sortable to active later, without having to do a full re-crawl when you switch. Both options require a full crawl to take effect.

Parameters.Name: Safe
Parameters.Description: Enables this managed property to be returned for queries executed by anonymous users. Enable this setting for managed properties that do not contain sensitive information and are appropriate for anonymous users to view.

Parameters.Name: Aliases
Parameters.Description: Define an alias for a managed property if you want to use the alias instead of the managed property name in queries and in search results. Use the original managed property and not the alias to map to a crawled property. Use an alias if you don't want to or don't have permission to create a new managed property.

Parameters.Name: TokenNormalization
Parameters.Description: Enable to return results independent of letter casing and diacritics(for example accented characters) used in the query.

Parameters.Name: CompleteMatching
Parameters.Description: By default, search returns partial matches between queries against this managed property and its content. Select Complete Matching for search to return exact matches instead. If a managed property 'Title' contains 'Contoso Sites', only the query Title: 'Contoso Sites' will give a result.

Parameters.Name: LanguageNeutralTokenization
Parameters.Description: By default, search depends on language when it breaks queries and content into parts (tokenization). Select language neutral tokenization if you have multilingual content and this managed property contains tags that are based on metadata term sets or other identifiers.

Parameters.Name: FinerQueryTokenization
Parameters.Description: By default, search tokenizes queries coarser than content. If a managed property 'ID' contains the string '1-23-456#7', and you query ID:'1-23', you might not get a partial match because search didn't break the query into small enough parts. Consider selecting finer query tokenization if the content of this managed property contains separators such as dots and dashes. Finer query tokenization makes queries against this managed property slower.

Parameters.Name: MappedCrawledProperties
Parameters.Description: Names of the crawled properties that are mapped to this managed property

Parameters.Name: CompanyNameExtraction
Parameters.Description: Enables the system to extract company name entities from the managed property when crawling new or updated items. Afterwards, the extracted entities can be used to set up refiners in the web part.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Present ensures the Search Managed Property exists.

Parameters.Description: Credentials of the account to authenticate with.

Description:
This resource allows users to configure and monitor the sharing settings for
your SPO tenant sharing settings

Parameters.Name: Url
Parameters.Description: The URL of the site collection.

Parameters.Name: Title
Parameters.Description: The title of the site collection.

Parameters.Name: Owner
Parameters.Description: Specifies the owner of the site.

Parameters.Name: TimeZoneId
Parameters.Description: TimeZone ID of the site collection.

Parameters.Name: Template
Parameters.Description: Specifies with template of site to create.

Parameters.Name: HubUrl
Parameters.Description: The URL of the Hub site the site collection needs to get connected to.

Parameters.Name: DisableFlows
Parameters.Description: Disables Microsoft Flow for this site.

Parameters.Name: SharingCapability
Parameters.Description: Specifies what the sharing capabilities are for the site. Possible values: Disabled, ExternalUserSharingOnly, ExternalUserAndGuestSharing, ExistingExternalUserSharingOnly.

Parameters.Name: StorageMaximumLevel
Parameters.Description: Specifies the storage quota for this site collection in megabytes. This value must not exceed the company's available quota.

Parameters.Name: StorageWarningLevel
Parameters.Description: Specifies the warning level for the storage quota in megabytes. This value must not exceed the values set for the StorageMaximumLevel parameter.

Parameters.Name: AllowSelfServiceUpgrade
Parameters.Description: Specifies if the site administrator can upgrade the site collection.

Parameters.Name: CommentsOnSitePagesDisabled
Parameters.Description: Specifies if comments on site pages are enabled or disabled.

Parameters.Name: DefaultLinkPermission
Parameters.Description: Specifies the default link permission for the site collection. None - Respect the organization default link permission. View - Sets the default link permission for the site to 'view' permissions. Edit - Sets the default link permission for the site to 'edit' permissions.

Parameters.Name: DefaultSharingLinkType
Parameters.Description: Specifies the default link type for the site collection. None - Respect the organization default sharing link type. AnonymousAccess - Sets the default sharing link for this site to an Anonymous Access or Anyone link. Internal - Sets the default sharing link for this site to the 'organization' link or company shareable link. Direct - Sets the default sharing link for this site to the 'Specific people' link.

Parameters.Name: DisableAppViews
Parameters.Description: Disables App Views.

Parameters.Name: DisableCompanyWideSharingLinks
Parameters.Description: Disables Company wide sharing links.

Parameters.Name: LocaleId
Parameters.Description: Specifies the language of the new site collection. Defaults to the current language of the web connected to.

Parameters.Name: DenyAddAndCustomizePages
Parameters.Description: Determines whether the Add And Customize Pages right is denied on the site collection. For more information about permission levels, see User permissions and permission levels in SharePoint.

Parameters.Name: RestrictedToRegion
Parameters.Description: Defines geo-restriction settings for this site

Parameters.Name: SharingAllowedDomainList
Parameters.Description: Specifies a list of email domains that is allowed for sharing with the external collaborators. Use the space character as the delimiter.

Parameters.Name: SharingBlockedDomainList
Parameters.Description: Specifies a list of email domains that is blocked for sharing with the external collaborators.

Parameters.Name: SharingDomainRestrictionMode
Parameters.Description: Specifies the external sharing mode for domains.

Parameters.Name: ShowPeoplePickerSuggestionsForGuestUsers
Parameters.Description: To enable the option to search for existing guest users at Site Collection Level, set this parameter to $true.

Parameters.Name: AnonymousLinkExpirationInDays
Parameters.Description: Specifies that all anonymous/anyone links that have been created (or will be created) will expire after the set number of days. Only applies if OverrideTenantAnonymousLinkExpirationPolicy is set to true. To remove the expiration requirement, set the value to zero (0)

Parameters.Name: SocialBarOnSitePagesDisabled
Parameters.Description: Disables or enables the Social Bar for Site Collection.

Parameters.Name: OverrideTenantAnonymousLinkExpirationPolicy
Parameters.Description: False - Respect the organization-level policy for anonymous or anyone link expiration. True - Override the organization-level policy for anonymous or anyone link expiration (can be more or less restrictive)

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Present ensures the site collection exists, absent ensures it is removed

Description:
This resource allows users to configure and monitor the themes for
their SPO tenant.

To configure your SPO themes using this configuration resource requires you to
provide a color palette which at the end defines the colors of your theme.

You can use the SPO Theme builder under:

[https://aka.ms/spthemebuilder](https://aka.ms/spthemebuilder)

to create and preview your color palette.

Once you are satisfied with the color of your theme you can copy
the JSON Output and put it directly into your configuration.

More details on the theme schema can be found
[here](https://aka.ms/AboutSPOThemes)

Parameters.Name: Name
Parameters.Description: The name of the theme, which appears in the theme picker UI and is also used by administrators and developers to refer to the theme in PowerShell cmdlets or calls to the SharePoint REST API.

Parameters.Name: IsInverted
Parameters.Description: This value should be false for light themes and true for dark themes; it controls whether SharePoint uses dark or light theme colors to render text on colored backgrounds.

Parameters.Name: Palette
Parameters.Description: Specifies the color scheme which composes your theme.

Parameters.Name: Ensure
Parameters.Description: Only accepted value is 'Present'.

[https://aka.ms/spthemebuilder](https://aka.ms/spthemebuilder)

to create and preview your color palette.

Once you are satisfied with the color of your theme you can copy
the JSON Output and put it directly into your configuration.

More details on the theme schema can be found
[here](https://aka.ms/AboutSPOThemes)

Description: Manages the Teams App Permission Policies.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: Unique identifier to be assigned to the new Teams app setup policy. Use the 'Global' Identity if you wish to assign this policy to the entire tenant.

Parameters.Description: Enables administrators to provide explanatory text to accompany a Teams app setup policy.

Parameters.Name: AppPresetList
Parameters.Description: Choose which apps and messaging extensions you want to be installed in your users' personal Teams environment and in meetings they create. Users can install other available apps from the Teams app store.

Parameters.Name: AppPresetMeetingList
Parameters.Description: Choose which apps and meeting extensions you want to be installed in your users' personal Teams environment and in meetings they create. Users can install other available apps from the Teams app store.

Parameters.Name: PinnedAppBarApps
Parameters.Description: Pinning an app displays the app in the app bar in Teams client. Admins can pin apps and they can allow users to pin apps. Pinning is used to highlight apps that are needed the most by users and promote ease of access.

Parameters.Name: PinnedMessageBarApps
Parameters.Description: Apps are pinned in messaging extensions and into the ellipsis menu.

Parameters.Name: AllowUserPinning
Parameters.Description: If you turn this on, the user's existing app pins will be added to the list of pinned apps set in this policy. Users can rearrange, add, and remove pins as they choose. If you turn this off, the user's existing app pins will be removed and replaced with the apps defined in this policy.

Parameters.Name: AllowSideLoading
Parameters.Description: This is also known as side loading. This setting determines if a user can upload a custom app package in the Teams app. Turning it on lets you create or develop a custom app to be used personally or across your organization without having to submit it to the Teams app store. Uploading a custom app also lets you test an app before you distribute it more widely by only assigning it to a single user or group of users.

Description: Creates a new Teams call hold policy in your tenant. The Teams call hold policy is used to customize the call hold experience for Teams clients.

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: Identity of the Teams Calling Policy.

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: Description of the Teams Calling Policy.

Parameters.Name: AllowPrivateCalling
Parameters.Description: Controls all calling capabilities in Teams. Turning this off will turn off all calling functionality in Teams. If you use Skype for Business for calling, this policy will not affect calling functionality in Skype for Business.

Parameters.Name: AllowVoicemail
Parameters.Description: Enables inbound calls to be routed to voice mail. Valid options are: AlwaysEnabled, AlwaysDisabled, UserOverride.

Parameters.Name: AllowCallGroups
Parameters.Description: Enables inbound calls to be routed to call groups.

Parameters.Name: AllowDelegation
Parameters.Description: Enables inbound calls to be routed to delegates; allows delegates to make outbound calls on behalf of the users for whom they have delegated permissions.

Parameters.Name: AllowCallForwardingToUser
Parameters.Description: Enables call forwarding or simultaneous ringing of inbound calls to other users in your tenant.

Parameters.Name: AllowCallForwardingToPhone
Parameters.Description: Enables call forwarding or simultaneous ringing of inbound calls to any phone number.

Parameters.Name: AllowCallRedirect
Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter provides the ability to configure call redirection capabilities on Teams phones.

Parameters.Name: AllowSIPDevicesCalling
Parameters.Description: Determines whether the user is allowed to use SIP devices for calling on behalf of a Teams client.

Parameters.Name: AllowWebPSTNCalling
Parameters.Description: Allows PSTN calling from the Team web client

Parameters.Name: PreventTollBypass
Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter to True will send calls through PSTN and incur charges rather than going through the network and bypassing the tolls.

Parameters.Name: BusyOnBusyEnabledType
Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter lets you configure how incoming calls are handled when a user is already in a call or conference or has a call placed on hold. New or incoming calls will be rejected with a busy signal. Valid options are: Enabled, Disabled and Unanswered.

Parameters.Name: CallRecordingExpirationDays
Parameters.Description: Sets the expiration of the recorded 1:1 calls.

Parameters.Name: MusicOnHoldEnabledType
Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter allows you to turn on or turn off music on hold when a PSTN caller is placed on hold. It is turned on by default. Valid options are: Enabled, Disabled, UserOverride. For now setting the value to UserOverride is the same as Enabled. This setting does not apply to call park and SLA boss delegate features. Valid options are: Enabled, Disabled, UserOverride.

Parameters.Name: SafeTransferEnabled
Parameters.Description: This parameter is not available for use. Valid options are: Enabled, Disabled, UserOverride.

Parameters.Name: AllowCloudRecordingForCalls
Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter to True will allows 1:1 Calls to be recorded.

Parameters.Name: AllowTranscriptionforCalling
Parameters.Description: Determines whether post-meeting captions and transcriptions are allowed in a user's meetings. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.

Parameters.Name: LiveCaptionsEnabledTypeForCalling
Parameters.Description: Determines whether real-time captions are available for the user in Teams meetings. Set this to DisabledUserOverride to allow user to turn on live captions. Set this to Disabled to prohibit.

Parameters.Name: AutoAnswerEnabledType
Parameters.Description: This setting allows the tenant admin to enable or disable the Auto-Answer setting. Valid options are: Enabled, Disabled.

Parameters.Name: SpamFilteringEnabledType
Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter determines whether calls identified as Spam will be rejected or not (probably). Valid options are: Enabled, Disabled.

Description:
This resource configures a Teams Calling Policy.

More information: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/teams-calling-policy

Parameters.Name: Identity
Parameters.Description: A unique identifier for the policy - this will be used to retrieve the policy later on to assign it to specific users.

Parameters.Name: AllowCallPark
Parameters.Description: If set to true, customers will be able to leverage the call park feature to place calls on hold and then decide how the call should be handled - transferred to another department, retrieved using the same phone, or retrieved using a different phone.

Parameters.Name: Description
Parameters.Description: Description of the Teams Call Park Policy.

Parameters.Name: ParkTimeoutSeconds
Parameters.Description: Specify the number of seconds to wait before ringing the parker when the parked call hasn't been picked up. Value can be from 120 to 1800 (seconds).

Parameters.Name: PickupRangeEnd
Parameters.Description: Specify the maximum value that a rendered pickup code can take. Value can be from 10 to 9999. Note: PickupRangeStart must be smaller than PickupRangeEnd.

Parameters.Name: PickupRangeStart
Parameters.Description: Specify the minimum value that a rendered pickup code can take. Value can be from 10 to 9999.

Parameters.Description: Present ensures the instance exists, absent ensures it is removed.
